*(Decree text, left column:)*

**О Господину Министру Финансовъ.**

Признавъ нужнымъ для образования особаго денежнаго фонда, предназначаемаго исключительно на довѣрие нашихъ желѣзныхъ дорогъ, произвести продажу въ срокъ, примѣрно до 85 лѣтъ, Николаевской желѣзной дороги, МЫ повелѣли вамъ, Указомъ НАШИМЪ 18 Іюля 1867 года, въ видахъ обезпечения таковой продажи, выпустить 600,000 облигацій Николаевской желѣзной дороги, всего на 75.000,000 руб., или 12.000,000 ф. стрг., 300.000,000 франк., 141.600,000 голл. гульд., съ тѣмъ, что если, въ случаѣ продажи Николаевской желѣзной дороги, уплата интересовъ и погашение означенныхъ облигацій были возложены на общество, которое пріобрѣтетъ эту линию, то это ни въ чемъ не должно ослаблять обязанности Государственнаго Казначейства относительно уплаты интересовъ и погашения сихъ облигацій.

Въ 1868 году Николаевская желѣзная дорога, съ принадлежащими къ ней вѣтвями, уступлена Главному Обществу Россійскихъ желѣзныхъ дорогъ на 84 года, съ тѣмъ, между прочимъ, условіемъ, чтобы изъ чистаго дохода сей дороги прежде всего отчислялось 7.200,000 р. въ годъ на уплату интересовъ и погашения какъ по вышеупомянутымъ, по силѣ вышеупомянутаго Указа НАШЕГО въ 1867 г., облигаціямъ Николаевской желѣзной дороги, такъ и по тѣмъ облигаціямъ сей дороги, которыя новое общество, при уступкѣ оному Николаевской дороги, передало въ распоряжение Правительства.

Находя полезнымъ, согласно представлению вашему, разсмотрѣнному и назначенному НАМИ Комитетѣ, усилить для послѣдовательнаго безостановочнаго продолжения постройки въ Россіи желѣзныхъ дорогъ, предназначенный для сей цѣли особый денежный фондъ, посредствомъ новаго и послѣдняго выпуска облигацій Николаевской желѣзной дороги, повелѣваемъ вамъ выпускъ этотъ произвести на одинаковыхъ съ первымъ выпускомъ условіяхъ, а именно:

*(Decree text, right column:)*

1) Нарицательную цѣну каждой облигаціи назначить въ 20 ф. ст., 500 франк., 236 голл. гульд. или 125 руб., а число всѣхъ облигацій сего окончательнаго выпуска опредѣлить въ 555,500, на нарицательный капиталъ 11.110,000 ф. ст., 277.750,000 франк., 131.098,000 голл. гульд. или 69.437,500 руб., обративъ вышеозначенные 7.200,000 руб. на уплату интересовъ и погашения какъ сихъ облигацій, такъ и облигацій перваго выпуска.

2) Облигаціямъ симъ присвоить съ 19 Апрѣля/1 Мая 1869 г. 4% ежегоднаго дохода, уплачиваемаго по полугодно 20 Октября/1 Ноября и 19 Апрѣля/1 Мая.

3) Погашение сихъ облигацій по нарицательной ихъ цѣнѣ производить, начиная съ 20 Октября/1 Ноября 1870 года, въ 82 года, посредствомъ ежегоднаго въ С.-Петербургѣ тиража, для каковой цѣли образовать особый погасительный фондъ.

4) Подписку на сіи облигаціи открыть чрезъ посредство банкерскихъ домовъ: Братьевъ Берингъ и К° въ Лондонѣ, Гопе и К° въ Амстердамѣ, Готтингера и К° и Учетной Конторы въ Парижѣ.

5) Интересы и погашение означенныхъ облигацій уплачивать по постоянному курсу, считая 20 ф. ст., 500 фр., 236 голл. гульд. за 125 руб., въ Лондонѣ, Амстердамѣ и Парижѣ, по желанию владѣльцевъ облигацій, въ вышеозначенныхъ домахъ и конторѣ. Необходимыя для уплаты интересовъ и погашения по симъ облигаціямъ суммы своевременно доставлять въ Лондонъ банкирамъ Братьямъ Берингъ и К°, въ Амстердамъ Гопе и К° и въ Парижъ Готтингеру и К° и Учетной Конторѣ.

6) Облигаціи сіи освободить навсегда отъ уплаты какой либо пошлины.

7) Если бы, согласно п. 7 Указа 18-го Іюля 1867 года, уплата интересовъ и погашение по облигаціямъ сего выпуска были возложены на пріобрѣвшее Николаевскую желѣзную дорогу общество, то это ни въ чемъ не должно ослаблять обязанности Государственнаго Казначейства относительно уплаты интересовъ и погашения сихъ облигацій.

На подлинномъ Собственною ЕГО ИМПЕРАТОРСКАГО ВЕЛИЧЕСТВА рукою написано: «АЛЕКСАНДРЪ.»

---

## English Translation:

*(Crowned imperial double-headed eagle at top centre; the date 1869 below it. Engraved guilloche border and rosettes. A red circular French tax stamp reads "50% COMPLEMENT DE DROIT / TIMBRE"; other stamps are illegible.)*

**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN GOVERNMENT,**

**NIKOLAEVSKY (NICHOLAS) RAILWAY.**

**ISSUE OF**

**555,500 BONDS (OBLIGATIONS),**

each

**OF ONE HUNDRED AND TWENTY-FIVE RUBLES,**

**or FIVE HUNDRED FRANCS, or TWENTY POUNDS STERLING,**

**or TWO HUNDRED AND THIRTY-SIX DUTCH GUILDERS,**

of which 400,000 bonds are one to a sheet, under Nos. 600,001–1,000,000, and 155,500 are five to a single sheet, under Nos. 1,000,001–1,155,500.

for a nominal capital of

**69,437,500 RUBLES, or 277,750,000 FRANCS, or 11,110,000 POUNDS STERLING, or 131,098,000 DUTCH GUILDERS.**

This issue is authorized by the Most High Imperial Decree of 25 March 1869.

**B O N D (Obligation)**

**No. 946607**

**of 125 rubles = 500 francs = 20 pounds sterling = 236 Dutch [guilders]**

This bond entitles the bearer, until such time as it becomes subject to redemption by drawing, to an income of 4%, that is, twenty francs, or sixteen shillings, or nine Dutch guilders forty-four pence, without any deduction or duty. This income is paid at ten francs, or eight shillings, or four guilders seventy-two pence, half-yearly each year.

The bonds are redeemed at their nominal value over the course of 82 years, by a drawing conducted annually in St. Petersburg, beginning 20 October / 1 November 1870, in accordance with the redemption table printed on the reverse.

Payment of bonds drawn for redemption, as well as of coupons falling due, will be made:

In Paris: at the Comptoir d'Escompte (Discount Office). — In London: at Messrs. Baring Brothers & Co.
In Paris: at Messrs. Hottinguer & Co. — In Amsterdam: at Messrs. Hope & Co.

*(fine print:)* This bond is furnished with thirty-five coupons for the collection of the interest specified for the first eighteen years, together with a talon; thereafter, if the bond has not been drawn for redemption, a new coupon sheet will be issued, upon presentation of the talon, in good time and free of all charge, in Paris, London, or Amsterdam, by the above-named banking houses or office.

Accountant [signature — illegible]     Government Authorized Agent [signature — illegible]

---

*(Decree, left column:)*

**To the Minister of Finance.**

Having deemed it necessary to establish a special monetary fund, intended exclusively for [the trust of] our railways, and to carry out within the term — approximately up to 85 years — [the disposition] of the Nikolaevsky Railway, WE ordered you, by OUR Decree of 18 July 1867, with a view to securing such sale, to issue 600,000 bonds of the Nikolaevsky Railway, amounting in all to 75,000,000 rubles, or 12,000,000 pounds sterling, 300,000,000 francs, 141,600,000 Dutch guilders, with the provision that, should the payment of interest and the redemption of the said bonds be laid, in the event of the sale of the Nikolaevsky Railway, upon the company that acquires this line, this shall in no way weaken the obligation of the State Treasury regarding the payment of interest and the redemption of these bonds.

In 1868 the Nikolaevsky Railway, with the branches belonging to it, was ceded to the Grand Company of Russian Railways for 84 years, on the condition, among others, that from the net income of this line there be set aside, first of all, 7,200,000 rubles per year for the payment of interest and redemption both of the aforesaid bonds of the Nikolaevsky Railway (by virtue of OUR aforementioned Decree of 1867) and of those bonds of this line which the new company, upon the cession of the Nikolaevsky Railway to it, transferred to the disposal of the Government.

Finding it useful, in accordance with your proposal — examined and approved by the Committee appointed by US — to reinforce, for the continuous and uninterrupted continuation of railway construction in Russia, the special monetary fund designated for this purpose, by means of a new and final issue of bonds of the Nikolaevsky Railway, WE command you to carry out this issue on the same terms as the first issue, namely:

*(Decree, right column:)*

1) To fix the nominal value of each bond at 20 pounds sterling, 500 francs, 236 Dutch guilders, or 125 rubles, and to set the number of all bonds of this final issue at 555,500, for a nominal capital of 11,110,000 pounds sterling, 277,750,000 francs, 131,098,000 Dutch guilders, or 69,437,500 rubles, applying the aforementioned 7,200,000 rubles to the payment of interest and redemption of both these bonds and the bonds of the first issue.

2) To assign to these bonds, as from 19 April / 1 May 1869, an annual income of 4%, payable half-yearly on 20 October / 1 November and 19 April / 1 May.

3) To carry out the redemption of these bonds at their nominal value, beginning 20 October / 1 November 1870, over 82 years, by means of an annual drawing in St. Petersburg, for which purpose a special sinking fund is to be formed.

4) To open the subscription for these bonds through the banking houses: Baring Brothers & Co. in London, Hope & Co. in Amsterdam, Hottinguer & Co. and the Comptoir d'Escompte in Paris.

5) To pay the interest and redemption of the said bonds at a fixed rate of exchange, reckoning 20 pounds sterling, 500 francs, 236 Dutch guilders per 125 rubles, in London, Amsterdam, and Paris, at the bondholders' choice, at the above-named houses and office. The sums necessary for the payment of interest and redemption of these bonds are to be remitted in good time to London to the bankers Baring Brothers & Co., to Amsterdam to Hope & Co., and to Paris to Hottinguer & Co. and the Comptoir d'Escompte.

6) These bonds are to be exempted forever from the payment of any duty whatsoever.

7) Should, in accordance with point 7 of the Decree of 18 July 1867, the payment of interest and redemption of the bonds of this issue be laid upon the company acquiring the Nikolaevsky Railway, this shall in no way weaken the obligation of the State Treasury regarding the payment of interest and the redemption of these bonds.

Upon the original, in HIS IMPERIAL MAJESTY'S own hand, is written: "ALEXANDER."

title: Student Loan Marketing Association (Sallie Mae) Yield Curve Note, United States, 1986
description: A registered yield curve note for $100,000 (One Hundred Thousand Dollars) issued by the Student Loan Marketing Association ("Sallie Mae"), described on its face as a stockholder-owned corporation organized under Part B of Title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965, as amended (20 U.S.C. Section 1071 et seq.). Dated March 6, 1986 and due in 1991, it names the nominee Hare & Co. as registered holder and Bankers Trust Company as fiscal agent. The instrument embodies the structured-finance engineering of a government-sponsored enterprise that funded the secondary market in student loans by tailoring exotic coupon formulas—here keyed to the shape of the yield curve—to institutional investors. Engraved by the American Bank Note Company, its vignette of a robed allegorical figure of Learning instructing a youth over an open book and globe lends classical dignity to an obligation underwriting higher education.

title: Republic of Bolivia Government Loan Bond (Empréstito de 1870)
description: This is the verso of a bearer bond of the Republic of Bolivia, issued under the Empréstito de 1870 (the loan of 1870). The left half carries a printed-and-handwritten panel of conditions, headed in cursive Spanish (Condiciones del Empréstito), within an engraved guilloche border in teal ink. The right half preserves an intact sheet of forty detachable interest coupons, arranged four across and ten down, each bearing the legend REPUBLICA DE BOLIVIA, EMPRESTITO DE 1870 and a central value of 40. Bolivia, dated to the 1870 loan. A young Andean republic chronically short of specie, Bolivia turned to the European and New York capital markets to fund its obligations, and the engraving was executed by the Compañía Nacional de Billetes de Banco of New York (the National Bank Note Company), whose dense lathe-work and intricate counters served less as decoration than as a guarantee against forgery for a security meant to circulate abroad among bondholders who would never see the issuer.
notes: Verso of a Latin American government bond, ca. 19th century. Left side: conditions of issue in cursive script. Right side: sheet of 40 coupons of 10 units each. 2-page document; see preceding item for recto.

title: National Pisco to Yca Railway Company Guaranteed Loan Bond, Republic of Peru, London, 1869
description: This is a £100 sterling (Soles 500 Peruvian currency) bearer bond of The National Pisco to Yca Railway Company, issued in London on the first day of May 1869 under "The Guaranteed Loan" and carrying interest of 5 per cent per annum payable half-yearly on 1 January and 1 July. It forms part of a total issue of £290,000 / Soles 1,450,000, split between Series A bonds of £20 (S.100) each, numbered 1 to 3,000, and Series B bonds of £100 (S.500) each, numbered 3,001 to 5,300, with capital redeemable in cash on six months' notice or by semi-annual drawings at par. Crucially, the loan is guaranteed unconditionally by the Supreme Government of the Republic of Peru, charged on the surplus from the sales of Peruvian guano. The bond thus belongs to the guano-financed railway boom of the 1860s, when Peru pledged its fertiliser revenues to underwrite British-built lines from the coast inland. Engraved by Nissen, Parker & Arnold of London, the vignette sets the national arms—the vicuña, the cinchona tree, and a cornucopia spilling coins—upon a locomotive flanked by flags, fusing republican emblem with the promise of steam transport.

title: National Pisco to Yca Railway Company Guaranteed Loan Bond, Peru, 1869 (Reverse)
description: The verso of a £100 bearer bond of the Guaranteed Loan of the National Pisco to Yca Railway Company, Republic of Peru (recto at goetzmann0689). Two engraved undertakings, each dated London, the first day of May, 1869, occupy the right: the Company, signed J. A. de Lavalle as Director, charges and hypothecates its properties and assets to fulfil the loan's conditions, while the Supreme Government of Peru, through its special agent Manuel Fco. Perola, guarantees the loan unconditionally, binding the Republic to an annuity of £29,300 sterling — covering the 5 per cent interest and a sinking fund to redeem the whole within twenty-five years. At lower right the back cover repeats the issue, denomination (£100), and number 3395. The attached coupon sheet, printed in Spanish, fixes each half-yearly cédula at £2:10s (or S/12½ in Lima, payable at José Canevaro é hijos), the dated coupons running to the forty-ninth semester of 1 July 1893. The sheet documents how a young Andean republic borrowed in London to lay rails from Pisco to Ica, pledging the sovereign's guarantee where the enterprise's own credit could not yet stand alone.

title: Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company First Mortgage Gold Bond
description: This is a bearer first mortgage gold bond (obligation de première hypothèque au porteur) of The Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited (Compagnie du Chemin de Fer de l'Est Central Chilien), one of an issue of 4,625 five per cent bonds numbered 1 to 4,625, each of $20 or Frs. 503, carrying interest at five per cent and free of British income tax. Created by resolution of the directors dated 21 March 1910 and executed under the company's common seal on the 2nd day of August 1910, the bond was issued by a London-registered company (share capital £240,000 in 240,000 shares of £1) to finance railway construction in Chile. The bilingual French and English text, the gold clause, and the franc-and-dollar denomination reveal a British promoter raising Continental capital for a South American line. The decorative steel-engraved border, with a small vignette of port works, signals security and engineering ambition to prospective bearers.

title: Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company £20 First Mortgage Gold Bond, London, 1910 (reverse)
description: The reverse of a £20 (twenty pound) bearer bond of The Chilian Eastern Central Railway Company, Limited, a £555,000 issue of Five per Cent. First Mortgage Gold Bonds raised in London in 1910 to build a Chilean line running from Lebu to Los Sauces. The left field prints "The Conditions of Issue," opening with the recital that the bond is one of a series "created by a resolution of the Board of Directors of the Company on the 21st day of March, 1910," secured by first mortgage and resting on a Government guarantee conferred by the railway concession. A redemption schedule tabulates, year by year from 1916 onward, the number of bonds to be drawn, their sterling value, and the balance left outstanding, the mechanism of a sinking fund retiring the debt to maturity. At right, an engraved label in carmine repeats the issuer, "First Mortgage Bond for £20," and "5%," the whole cancelled by hole-punch. The document records how a small Andean railway reached London's capital market under sovereign guarantee, the security on which a remote concession was financed.

title: Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes 4% Agrarian Liquidation Bond for Bosnia and Herzegovina, 500 dinars
description: A 4% bearer bond of 500 dinars issued by the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes "za finansijsko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini" — for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Signed for the Minister of Finance and the Director of State Debts, it bears the sheet of fifty semi-annual coupons (the talon promising a fresh sheet once the fiftieth is drawn), the last falling due 1 May 1947, which places issue around 1921–1922. The instrument is fiscal machinery for one of the new kingdom's most charged tasks: dismantling the late Ottoman kmet tenancy and indemnifying former landholders, the state borrowing against itself to settle the land. The allegory states this plainly. A diademed sovereign-figure presides over a frieze of double-headed eagles; below sit two peasants — a fez-wearing Muslim before a minaret, an Orthodox Serb before a domed monastery — the confessional halves of Bosnia reconciled in agriculture. The drawing is assured fin-de-siècle academic work; the framing draws on Byzantine-Serbian revival motifs rather than strict Art Nouveau.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% State Bond, 500 Dinars / 500 Francs, bearer. Ornate color illustration with allegorical figures. Talon (coupon renewal receipt) attached at left. Bilingual Serbian/French.

# Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes — 4% Bond for the Financial Liquidation of Agrarian Relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina, 500 Dinars (1921) — Transcription

## Original:

*(The sheet holds two elements: at the upper left, a renewal slip [TALON] with an attached strip of coupons; at the right, the engraved bond certificate itself, printed on its side [rotated 90°]. All text is Serbo-Croatian, printed in parallel Cyrillic [left] and Latin [right] columns. The only French elements are the abbreviations "S-ie" [Série] and "N°".)*

### TALON (renewal slip, upper left)

**Cyrillic (left column):**

**ТАЛОН**    S-ie   ✱008546

**КРАЉЕВИНА СРБА, ХРВАТА И СЛОВЕНАЦА**

4% ОБВЕЗНИЦА за Финансијско Ликвидирање Аграрних Односа у Босни и Херцеговини

По исплати последњег 50-ог купона издаје се доносиоцу овог ТАЛОНА нов купонски табак.

**Latin (right column):**

N° 26–30    **TALON**

**KRALJEVINA SRBA, HRV[ATA I SLOVENACA]**

4% OBVEZNICA za Finansijsko Likvid[iranje] Agrarnih Odnosa u Bosni i Herceg[ovini]

Po isplati poslednjeg 50-og kupona [izdaje se donosiocu ovog TALONA nov] kuponski tabak.

### Coupons (strip below the talon)

*(A row of individual semi-annual interest coupons, each printed on its side and alternating Cyrillic / Latin. Each carries the same text with a changing due date; all bear the bond's serial ✱008546.)*

Representative coupon —

**Cyrillic:** СЕРИЈА ✱008546 · Краљевина Срба, Хрв[ата и Словенаца] · **КУПОН 4%** · за Фин. Ликв. Агр. Односа у Босни [и Херцеговини] · [illegible amount] ДИНАРА · Плаћа се 1. новембра 194[.]

**Latin:** SERIJA ✱008546 · Kraljevina Srba, Hrv[ata i Slovenaca] · **KUPON 4%** · za Fin. Likv. Agr. Odnosa u Bosni [i Hercegovini] · [illegible amount] DINARA · Plaća se 1. maja 1943.

*(Due dates visible across the visible coupons include: 1. maja 1943; 1. novembra 194[.]; 1. maja 1944; 1. maja 1945 — semi-annual, falling due 1 May and 1 November. Individual coupon ordinal numbers not separately legible.)*

### BOND CERTIFICATE (right panel)

*(Chromolithographed allegory: a diademed enthroned female figure above a frieze of double-headed eagles; a seated peasant at each lower corner flanking a pastoral landscape. Denomination roundel **500** appears in each lower corner. A round state coat-of-arms seal [double-headed eagle] is centred in the base. Title text runs bilingually across the foot in ornate script.)*

**Left cartouche (Cyrillic):**

**Обвезница за финансијско ликвидирање аграрних односа у Босни и Херцегови[ни]**

Генерални Директор Државних Дугова — Generalni Direktor Državnih dugova

*[signature]* Др Радоје М. Јовановић

**Right cartouche (Latin):**

**Obveznica za finansijsko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini**

Министар Финансија — Ministar Finansija:

*[illegible signature]*

**Lower right margin (artist's imprint, script):** А. Вукичевић изради[о] *(partly legible)*

---

## English Translation:

### TALON (renewal slip)

**TALON**   Series ✱008546   /   N° 26–30

**KINGDOM OF SERBS, CROATS AND SLOVENES**

4% BOND for the Financial Liquidation of Agrarian Relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina

Upon payment of the last, 50th, coupon, a new coupon sheet will be issued to the bearer of this TALON.

### Coupons

Representative coupon — SERIES ✱008546 · Kingdom of Serbs, Croats [and Slovenes] · **COUPON 4%** · for the Financial Liquidation of Agrarian Relations in Bosnia [and Herzegovina] · [illegible amount] DINARS · Payable 1 May 1943 *(dates alternate 1 May / 1 November across the coupons: 1943, 1944, 1945 …)*.

### BOND CERTIFICATE

title: Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes 4% Agrarian Liquidation Bond, Bosnia and Herzegovina (Belgrade, 1921), verso
description: The verso of a 4% bearer obligation issued by the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes "za finansisko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini" — for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina (recto at goetzmann0693). Printed in Serbian Cyrillic, Croatian, Slovene and French, it sets out the conditions of issue: authorized under Article 21 of the Decree on the agrarian liquidation, the loan comprised 1,300,000 obligations in 26,000 series of fifty, for a nominal 130,000,000 dinars, repayable at par over fifty years. The sheet is a block of five bearer bonds of 100 dinars (500 dinars), its TALON exchangeable for a new coupon sheet after the fiftieth half-yearly coupon. Below, a quadrilingual amortization table ("Tableau d'amortissement") schedules the annual sums drawn by lot. The document records the new South-Slav state assuming the fiscal burden of dismantling Ottoman-era feudal land tenure in its newly incorporated provinces, converting expropriated landlords' claims into long-dated public debt. Belgrade, 18 June 1921.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% State Bond, verso. Lottery/amortization draw tables and multilingual conditions of issue. 2-page document; see goetzmann0693 for recto.

# Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes — 4% Agrarian Liquidation Loan Bond (Bosnia and Herzegovina), 1921 — Verso — Transcription

## Original:

*(Layout note: the image is scanned upside-down and has been read rotated 180°. The verso is quadrilingual — Serbian Cyrillic, Croatian (Latin), Slovene, and French — set in four parallel columns of identical legal text, above a quadrilingual amortization table. A TALON block and a strip of coupon stubs occupy the upper right. A large black area (an attached coupon sheet) obscures the lower-left and the right-hand columns of the table's second half. All four language versions are transcribed below.)*

---

**COLUMN 1 — Serbian (Cyrillic)**

**КРАЉЕВИНА СРБА, ХРВАТА И СЛОВЕНАЦА**

**4°/₀ ОБВЕЗНИЦЕ**
за финансиско ликвидирање аграрних односа у Босни и Херцеговини.

Издате на основу овлашћења чл. 21. Уредбе о финансиском ликвидирању аграрних односа у Босни и Херцеговини.

Подељене у 26000 серија по 50 бројева, свега 1,300.000 комада обвезница у номиналном износу од 130,000.000.— динара.

**ИСПЛАТА ЗА 50 ГОДИНА ПО НОМИНАЛИ**

**ПЕТ ОБВЕЗНИЦА**
на доносиоца свака од 100 динара
**500  ПЕТ СТОТИНА ДИНАРА  500**

Овај Зајам издат је у 286000 комада обвезница и то:
130000 комада јединица од Серије    1—2600
78000    „    петица    „    „    2601—10400
78000    „    десетица    „    „    10401—26000
Обвезнице ове носе 4% годишњег интереса од номиналног износа, а интерес се плаћа полугодишње уназад уз предају дотичног купона 1. маја и 1. новембра сваке године.

Исплата овог зајма вршиће се у року од 50 година према приложеном амортизационом плану, путем извлачења, које ће се вршити 2. јануара сваке године, у Генералној Дирекцији Државних Дугова.

Извучене обвезнице исплаћиване се у номиналном износу четири месеца доцније. На извучене обвезнице престаје тећи камата од 1. маја дотичне године.

Потребне суме за исплату купона и извучених обвезница уносиће се сваке године у државни буџет у округлој суми.

Купони којима је рок приспео као и извучене обвезнице, могу се наплаћивати код свију Финансиских Делегација, Обласних Финансиских Дирекција, Окружних и Среских Финансиских Управа, Порезних Уреда, као и код Новчаних Завода у земљи који и ту буду нарочито овлашћени.

У Београду вршиће исплату Генерална Дирекција Државних Дугова, која обавља и сву службу по овом зајму.

Купони застаревају за 5 година од рока, а амортоване обвезнице за 30 година од дана када су извучене.

Обвезнице ове уживају сва права, која имају и остале државне обвезнице.

У Београду, дне 18. јуна 1921. године.

**ГЕНЕРАЛНИ ДИРЕКТОР**
Државних Дугова
*(handwritten signature:)* Д-р Радоје М. Јовановић

---

**COLUMN 2 — Croatian (Latin)**

**KRALJEVINA SRBA, HRVATA I SLOVENACA**

**4°/₀ obveznice**
za financijsko likvidiranje agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini.

Izdane na osnovu ovlašćenja čl. 21. Uredbe o financijskom likvidiranju agrarnih odnosa u Bosni i Hercegovini.

Razdijeljene u 26000 serija po 50 brojeva, svega 1,300.000 komada obveznica u nominalnom iznosu od 130,000.000.— dinara.

**ISPLATA ZA 50 GODINA PO NOMINALI**

**PET OBVEZNICA**
na donosioca svaka od 100 dinara
**500  PET STOTINA DINARA  500**

Ovaj Zajam izdat je u 286000 komada obveznica i to:
130000 komada jedinica od Serije    1—2600
78000    „    petica    „    „    2601—10400
78000    „    desetica    „    „    10401—26000
Obveznice ove nose 4% godišnjeg interesa od nominalnog iznosa, a interes se plaća polugodišnje unazad uz predaju dotičnog kupona 1. maja i 1. novembra svake godine.

Isplata ovog zajma vršiće se u roku od 50 godina prema priloženom amortizacionom planu, [putem] izvlačenja, koje će se vršiti 2. januara svake godine, u Generalnoj Direkciji Državnih [Dugova].

Izvučene obveznice isplaćivaće se u nominalnom iznosu četiri mjeseca docnije. Na izvučene [obveznice pre]staje teći kamata od 1. maja do[tične godine].

Potrebne sume za isplatu kupona i izvučenih [obveznica] unosiće se svake godine u državni [proračun] u okrugloj sumi.

Kuponi kojima je rok prispio kao i izvučene [obveznice], mogu se naplaćivati kod sviju [Finansijskih] Delegacija, Oblasnih Finansijskih [Direkcija], Okružnih i Sreskih Finansijskih Uprava, [Poreznih] Ureda, kao i kod Novčanih Zavoda [u zemlji koji i] tu budu naročito ovlašćeni.

[U Beogra]du vršiće isplatu Generalna Direk[cija Državnih Du]gova, koja obavlja i svu službu [po ovom zajmu].

[Kuponi zastare]vaju za 5 godina od roka, a [amortizovane obvez]nice za 30 godina od dana [kada su izvučene].

[Obveznice ove uživaju] sva prava, koja imaju [i ostale državne o]bveznice.

[U Beogra]du, dne 18. juna 1921. godine.

**GENERALNI DIREKTOR**
Državnih Dugova
*(shared handwritten signature — Dr Radoje M. Jovanović)*

---

**COLUMN 3 — Slovene**

**KRALJEVINA SRBOV, HRVATOV IN SLOVENCEV**

**4°/₀ obveznice**
za finančno likvidiranje agrarnih odnošajev v Bosni in Hercegovini.

Izdane na podlagi pooblastive člena 21. uredbe o finančnem likvidiranju agrarnih odnošajev v Bosni in Hercegovini.

Razdeljene na 26000 serij po 50 števil, skupaj 1,300.000 obveznic v nominalnem znesku 130,000.000.— dinarjev.

**IZPLAČILO V 50 LETIH PO NOMINALI**

**PET OBVEZNIC**
na prinosnika vsaka po 100 dinarjev
**500  PETSTO DINARJEV  500**

To posojilo je izdano v 286.000 obveznicah, in sicer:
130000 edinic    od serije    1—2600
78000 petic    „    „    2601—10400
78000 desetic    „    „    10401—26000
Te obveznice dajo 4% ne[?] [no]minalnega zneska, obresti pa se plačuje[jo] polletno za nazaj proti izročitvi dotičnega kupona dne 1. maja in dne 1. novembra vsakega leta.

Izplačevanje tega posojila se bo vršilo v roku 50 let, po priloženem amortizacijskem načrtu z žrebanjem, ki bo vsako leto dne 2. januarja, v generalni direkciji državnih dolgov.

Izžrebane obveznice se bodo izplačevale v nominalnem znesku štiri mesece pozneje. Na izžrebane obveznice prestanejo teči obresti od dne 1. maja dotičnega leta.

Potrebne vsote za izplačevanje kuponov in izžrebanih obveznic se bodo vsako leto postavljale v državni proračun v okrogli vsoti.

Kuponi, katerim je rok dospel, kako tudi izžrebane obveznice se lahko izplačujejo pri vseh finančnih delegacijah, oblastnih finančnih direkcijah, okrožnih in sreskih upravah, davčnih uradih, nadalje pri denarnih zavodih v državi, ki bodo za to posebe pooblaščeni.

V Beogradu vrši izplačevanje generalna direkcija državnih dolgov, ki opravlja tudi vso službo glede tega posojila.

Kuponi zastarevajo v 5. letih od roka, amortizirane obveznice pa v 30. letih od dne, ko so izžrebane.

Te obveznice uživajo vse pravice, ki jih ima[jo i] ostale državne obveznice.

V Beogradu, dne 18. junija 1921.

**MINISTER ZA FINANCE**
*(handwritten signature:)* Д-р Коста Кумануди [D-r Kosta Kumanudi]

---

**COLUMN 4 — French**

**ROYAUME DES SERBES, CROATES ET SLOVENES**

**Emprunt 4°/₀**
pour la Liquidation Financière de la Situation Agraire en Bosnie et Herzégovine.

Émis en vertu de l'article 21. du Règlement relatif à la liquidation financière de la situation agraire en Bosnie et Herzégovine.

Réparti en 26.000 séries ayant chacune 50 numéros, en tout 1,300.000 obligations dont le capital nominal s'élève à 130,000.000 de dinars.

**Remboursement du montant nominal de l'Emprunt [en] 50 [ans]**

**CINQ OBLIGATIONS**
au porteur de 100 dinars l'une
**500  CINQ CENT DINARS  500**

On a émis pour cet Emprunt 286.000 obligations comme suit:
130.000 titres    unitaires depuis la série    1 à 2.600
78.000 coupures de cinq titres    „    „    2.601 [à] 10.400
78.000    „    dix    „    „    10.401 [à] 26.000
Ces obligations donnent sur le montant [no]minal un intérêt annuel de 4% et l'intérêt [est] payable chaque semestre échu et la remise [du] coupon respectif le 1er Mai et le 1er Nov[em]bre de chaque année.

Le remboursement de cet Emprunt sera effectué dans le délai de 50 ans d'après le tableau d'amortissement y annexé au moyen des tirages officiels, qui aura lieu le 2 Janvier de chaque année à la Direction Générale de la Dette [Pu]blique.

Le payement du montant nominal des titres sortis aux tirages officiels s'effectuera quatre mois après ces tirages. Les titres sortis aux tirages officiels cessent de porter intérêt à partir du 1er Mai de l'année respective.

Les sommes nécessaires à l'annuité pour le payement des coupons et des titres sortis aux tirages officiels seront portées chaque année au Budget de l'Etat en chiffres ronds.

Les coupons échus ainsi que les titres encaissés aux tirages officiels pourront être encaissés à toutes les Délégations financières, aux Directions régionales, aux Directions financières départementales et d'arrondissement, aux Directions des contributions, ainsi qu'aux établissements de crédit dans le pays, qui seront autorisés à cet effet.

Le payement des coupons échus et des titres sortis aux tirages officiels sera fait à Belgrade par la Direction Générale de la Dette Publique qui est chargée de tout le service de cet emprunt.

Les coupons échus sont prescrits après 5 ans à compter de l'échéance et les obligations amorties après 30 ans à partir de la date de leur sortie aux tirages officiels.

Les obligations de cet Emprunt jouissent [de] tous les privilèges accordés aux autres titres [de] l'Emprunt.

Fait à Belgrade, le 18. Juin 1921.

**LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL** de la Dette Publique
**— LE MINISTRE DES FINANCES**
*(handwritten signatures as above)*

*(The two printed signature-line headings appear across the foot of all four columns: left pair — ГЕНЕРАЛНИ ДИРЕКТОР Државних Дугова / GENERALNI DIREKTOR Državnih Dugova / GENERALNI DIREKTOR Državnih dolgov / LE DIRECTEUR GÉNÉRAL de la Dette Publique, signed **Д-р Радоје М. ## English Translation:

**KINGDOM OF SERBS, CROATS AND SLOVENES**

**4% Bonds** for the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Issued under the authority of Article 21 of the Decree on the financial liquidation of agrarian relations in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Divided into 26,000 series of 50 numbers, in all 1,300,000 bonds of a nominal value of 130,000,000 dinars.

**REPAYMENT OVER 50 YEARS AT PAR**

**FIVE BONDS** to bearer, each of 100 dinars — **500 FIVE HUNDRED DINARS 500**

This Loan was issued in 286,000 bond certificates, namely:
130,000 single-unit certificates, of Series 1–2,600;
78,000 five-bond certificates ("petice"), Series 2,601–10,400;
78,000 ten-bond certificates ("desetice"), Series 10,401–26,000.
These bonds carry 4% annual interest on the nominal value; the interest is paid half-yearly in arrears against surrender of the relevant coupon on 1 May and 1 November of each year.

Repayment of this loan will be effected over a period of 50 years according to the attached amortization plan, by drawing lots, which will take place on 2 January of each year at the General Directorate of State Debts.

Drawn bonds will be paid at their nominal value four months later. Drawn bonds cease to bear interest from 1 May of the relevant year.

The sums required to pay coupons and drawn bonds will be entered each year in the state budget in round figures.

Matured coupons and drawn bonds may be cashed at all Financial Delegations, Regional Financial Directorates, District and County (srez) Financial Administrations, Tax Offices, as well as at credit institutions in the country specially authorized for that purpose.

In Belgrade the payment is made by the General Directorate of State Debts, which also performs all service relating to this loan.

Coupons lapse (are time-barred) 5 years after maturity, and amortized bonds 30 years after the date on which they were drawn.

These bonds enjoy all the rights enjoyed by the other state bonds.

Belgrade, 18 June 1921.

**GENERAL DIRECTOR of State Debts** — signed Dr Radoje M. Jovanović
**MINISTER OF FINANCE** — signed Dr Kosta Kumanudi

**AMORTIZATION PLAN / TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT** — a 50-year schedule giving, for each year (Echéance): the annual Interest, the Amortization (principal repaid), the Number of bonds and Series drawn/redeemed, and the remaining Balance of the debt (Solde de la dette). The debt runs from 130,000,000 dinars down to zero over 50 years; totals: interest 172,584,600 dinars, amortization 130,000,000 dinars, 1,300,000 bonds redeemed.

**TALON:** After payment of the last coupon (No. 50), the bearer of this talon will be issued a new coupon sheet.

**COUPONS:** interest coupons of 10 dinars each, payable 1 May / 1 November of successive years.

---

*Notes / conflicts vs. verified facts:*
- *Currency: the document (including the French column) states all amounts in **dinars** ("100 dinars l'une", "CINQ CENT DINARS", "130,000.000 de dinars"). The verified-facts list "Serbian dinar; French franc" — no "franc" figure appears on this verso; the franc may pertain to the recto (goetzmann0693).*
- *Named individuals: the facts list none, but two officials sign the verso — **Dr Radoje M. Jovanović** (General Director of State Debts) and **Dr Kosta Kumanudi** (Minister of Finance). Kosta Kumanudi was indeed Yugoslav Finance Minister in 1921, consistent with the date.*
- *Table, year 25 balance: printed "96,540.000" but the amortization sequence (year 26 interest 3,781,600 = 4% × 94,540,000; year 26 solde 92,270,000 + amort 2,270,000 = 94,540,000) requires **94,540,000**. Transcribed as printed with this flag; likely an original typographic error (or a smudged "4" read as "6").*
- *"286000 komada obveznica" (physical certificates) is internally consistent: 130,000 singles + 78,000 fives (=390,000 bonds) + 78,000 tens (=780,000 bonds) = 1,300,000 bonds total, matching the facts.*

title: Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortizable Loan of 1895 — Amortization Plan (Belgrade)
description: This is the official amortization plan for the Kingdom of Serbia's 4% amortizable loan of 1895, a state bond with a nominal capital of 355,292,000 francs. Printed in three parallel languages — French (Tableau d'Amortissement), Serbian Cyrillic (Амортизациони план), and German (Tilgungs-Plan) — it sets out the schedule of half-yearly redemption payments, falling each 13 January and 13 July (new style), beginning in 1896. The conditions panels explain that service of the loan is secured on the newly reorganized Administration des Monopoles, which independently manages the state's tobacco, salt, and petroleum monopolies together with stamp, spirits, customs, and railway receipts, and which is bound to forward these revenues to designated banking houses. The instrument records Serbia's large 1895 conversion operation, by which scattered earlier obligations — including the lottery loans of 1881 and 1888 — were consolidated and refinanced under foreign-supervised monopoly security. Dated Belgrade, 1/13 August 1895, and signed by government delegates and bank representatives (among them Schröder). The austere ruled tables and unadorned typography present the bond as an exercise in fiscal discipline rather than display.
currency: French franc; Serbian dinar; German mark
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Kingdom of Serbia, 4% Bond, Amortization/Redemption Lottery Plan. Trilingual: Tableau d'Amortissement (French) / Amortizacioni Plan (Serbian) / Tilgungs-Plan (German). Separate schedule sheet accompanying the bond.

title: Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortisable Gold Bond
description: A bearer amortisable bond (Obligation / Обавезница) of the Kingdom of Serbia, denominated 500 Francs = 500 Dinars in gold, equivalent to R.M. 405 and 200 Austrian gold florins, bearing 4 per cent interest payable semi-annually and redeemable at par in gold over seventy-two years. It is one of 710,584 obligations forming a nominal capital of 355,292,000 Francs, created under the law of 8/20 July 1895. The trilingual text in French, Serbian (Cyrillic) and German, with payment places at Belgrade, Paris, Berlin and Vienna, marks this as Serbia's great unification loan, consolidating earlier debts and pledging the net revenues of the state railways together with the tobacco, salt and petroleum monopolies and the customs, all administered by the Autonomous Monopolies Administration. The instrument's appeal to foreign capital rested less on its restrained engraved guilloche border and small royal crown than on this elaborate pledge of revenue, a sovereign borrower binding its fiscal apparatus to reassure the Berlin and Paris markets. Printed in Vienna.
currency: French franc; Serbian dinar; German mark
creator: Kingdom of Serbia

title: Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan Bond, Berlin, 1898
description: A £100 sterling bearer bond of the Chinese Imperial Government's 4½ per cent Gold Loan of 1898, printed bilingually in English and German and styled as both a "Bond for £100 Sterling" and an "Obligation über £100 Sterling." Interest runs from 1 March 1898 at four and a half per cent, payable each 1 March and 1 September; the principal is repayable by annual drawings beginning January 1899, redeemable in sterling at the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation in London and at the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank in Berlin, which acted as agent for the emission. The bond forms part of a total nominal loan of £16,000,000 sterling, divided among denominations of £25, £50, £100, and £500. Authorised by Imperial Edict of 2 March 1896 and secured upon the revenues of the Imperial Maritime Customs of the Treaty Ports together with a first charge on the likin of certain ports and districts, the issue records how the late Qing state, pressed by indemnity and reform after the war with Japan, raised capital through Anglo-German banking houses and pledged its customs receipts to foreign creditors. The ornate engraved frame, executed at the Reichsdruckerei in Berlin, joins European scrollwork with Chinese motifs and a red seal in seal-script characters, the visual idiom of a loan negotiated between empires.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government / Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
notes: Chinese Imperial Government, 4½% Gold Loan of 1898, £100 Sterling bearer bond. Deutsch-Asiatische Bank. Total issue: 1,600,000 Sterling. Cancelled (holes punched). 2-page document; see goetzmann0698 for verso and conditions.

title: Chinese Imperial Government Gold Loan Bond (Reverse)
description: The reverse of a £100 sterling bond of the Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan of 1898 (Littera C), a bilingual sheet whose English and German columns reproduce extracts from the loan agreement ("Extracts" / "Auszug ... aus der Obligation erwähnten Vertrage") and, at right, the Table of Drawings or Amortisations-Plan. Interest fell due half-yearly on 1 March and 1 September. The amortisation schedule begins with the drawing of 1 March 1899, when £115,225 became repayable against an outstanding balance of £15,884,775, figures that fix the total issue at £16,000,000. Floated in 1898 through the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank, the loan belongs to the wave of European-secured Chinese borrowing that followed the indemnity owed to Japan after the war of 1894–95; the gold-denominated, sterling-fixed terms and the prominent redemption table reveal creditors' demand for security and predictable amortisation in lending to a hard-pressed empire. The engraved frame is restrained, its corner ornament conventional rather than emblematic.

title: Chinese Imperial Government Coupon Sheet (Upper Row)
description: This is a horizontal strip of interest coupons detached from a £100 bearer bond of the Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan of 1898. Fifteen coupons, numbered 76 through 90 and all keyed to Bond C. No. 076230, each promise £2 5s.—a half-year's interest at 4½ per cent—falling due at successive March and September dates, the printed examples running from 1 March 1936 to 1 March 1943. Each is payable in London at the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation or in Berlin at the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank. The loan, raised to fund the indemnity China owed Japan after the war of 1894–95, bound the empire's revenues to European creditors; the twin London-Berlin paying agents register the Anglo-German banking rivalry over China's finances. Printed in orange on engraved guilloche borders, the coupons are cancelled by hole-punch, marking interest already drawn.

title: Government of Honduras State Railway Loan Bearer Obligation, Paris, 1869
description: This is a bearer obligation of the Government of Honduras for its 1869 State Railway loan (Emprunt du Gouvernement de Honduras 1869 / Obligation des Chemins de Fer de l'État), a 300-franc bond carrying coupons and bearing interest from 1 March 1869. The text appears twice on the sheet, in French above and Spanish below, with the great "300 Francs au Porteur" / "300 Francos al Portador" numerals dominating each half and a block of coupons attached at the right. The bond was issued at Paris and dated 1 February 1869, payable in gold francs free of tax, secured by a mortgage on the State railway, with the loan partly guaranteed by France and England. The obligation belongs to the wave of European-placed railway loans by which a small Central American state sought to finance an interoceanic line, drawing on Paris and London capital it could not raise at home. The restrained engraved frame and the national arms favour the sober probity expected of a sovereign credit instrument rather than ornament for its own sake.
creator: Republic of Honduras

title: Honduras Government Loan Bond, Paris, 1869 (verso)
description: The verso of a bearer bond for the Honduras Government Loan of 1869 (Emprunt du Gouvernement de Honduras / Staats-Anleihe der Regierung von Honduras), a sovereign loan floated at Paris by Victor Herran, the Republic's minister there. The upper portion sets out the loan's conditions in parallel English, French, and German, declaring an emission of 207,509 obligations of 300 francs (£12) on the bearer, dated Paris, 28 February 1869, the proceeds pledged to complete the State Railways; interest is payable 1 March and 1 September, with triennial drawings on 1 February and 1 August. A printed amortization table (Tableau d'Amortissement) schedules redemptions every March and September from 1869 to 1886, summing to the full 207,509 bonds. Backed by railway revenues and citing the neutrality of England and the United States, the issue exemplifies the speculative European lending to small Latin American states that culminated in Honduras's notorious default. Ornament is confined to engraved blue lettering and patterned borders; the cover panels reprise the denomination and terms.

title: Republic of Bolivia Bearer Bond with Coupon Sheet
description: A bearer bond (bono al portador) of the Republic of Bolivia for one thousand pesos fuertes (Mil Pesos Fuertes), issued under the Empréstito de 1870 and dated at La Paz de Ayacucho on 1 January 1870. The attached coupon sheet pays interest semi-annually, each coupon promising forty pesos fuertes — eighty per year, an effective rate of eight per cent — with maturities running to 1889 and 1890, so a term of roughly twenty years; principal and interest were payable in Santiago de Chile. The engrossed text reproduces the enabling decree of the Asamblea Nacional Constituyente: the loan was authorised expressly to fund the conversion of the debased "feble" silver coinage into moneda de buena ley, the State bearing the conversion loss and the holder no charge beyond five per cent toward minting costs. The bond thus speaks to Bolivia's mid-century monetary disorder and its recourse to foreign credit to restore a sound silver standard. The central vignette presents an allegory of abundance — a seated figure amid sheaves of wheat, fruit, and grapes — engraved in the neoclassical manner; the imprint identifies the printer as the National Bank Note Company of New York (Compañía Nacional de Billetes de Banco, Nueva York).

title: City of Naples Unified Debt Bond
description: A bearer bond (Cartella al Portatore / Titre au Porteur) of the Unified 5% Debt of the City of Naples (Debito Unificato 5% della Città di Napoli), of 1,000 lire nominal capital bearing a 5% annual rente payable half-yearly from 1 January 1881. It forms part of a consolidated municipal loan of 86,953,310 lire, guaranteed by the Royal Government under the Law of 14 May 1881 and authorised by council and provincial resolutions of late 1880. It was issued at Naples on 30 November 1881 over the signatures of the Sindaco and Delegato Governativo. The instrument folds a southern municipality's debt into the fiscal architecture of the young Italian kingdom, the sovereign guarantee lending a provincial loan the credit of the state. The engraved frame, a winged putto cradling the crowned Savoy arms amid classicising scrollwork, binds civic borrowing to dynastic legitimacy. An attached sheet of 98 coupons, dated 1931 to 1979, attests the loan's long life.

Stamped "Debito Nuovo Foglio" 1931–1975. New coupon sheet of 98 coupons valid July 1, 1931 to December 31, 1979 attached.

title: Liberian Government 7% Loan Arrear Interest Certificate, London, 1899
description: A certificate of arrear interest issued by the Council of Foreign Bondholders in London, certifying £176 3s 4d of unpaid interest accrued on a £100 bond of the Liberian Government 7% External Loan of 1871. The arrears span 1 February 1874 to 31 March 1899, and the certificate is made redeemable under the Arrangement approved by the law of the Liberian Congress of 23 January 1899, an extract of which is printed on the reverse. Dated at Council House, 17 Moorgate Street, on 1 October 1899, and signed by the Council's Secretary. The document records the settlement of a sovereign default: Liberia's first foreign loan, raised in the London market, fell into arrears within three years, and a quarter-century of unpaid coupons was here converted into a fresh redeemable claim brokered by the bondholders' own protective committee. Printed by Bradbury, Wilkinson & Co. within a finely engraved green border, the certificate's restrained classical lettering conveys the institutional authority of late-Victorian London finance.
creator: Council of Foreign Bondholders, London

title: República Mexicana 5% Consolidated External Debt Gold Bond, 1899
description: This is a bearer bond (bono al portador) of the República Mexicana for the five-per-cent Consolidated External Debt loan of 1899 (Bono de la Deuda Consolidada Exterior Mexicana del 5% del año de 1899). Its face value is stated in parallel across the markets that took the loan: £200 sterling, $970 United States gold dollars, and 4,080 German Reichsmark, with capital and interest expressly payable in gold ("Capital é Intereses pagaderos en oro"). The bond forms one of 198,000 pieces, in series of £20, £100 and £200, of a total issue of about $22,700,000, raised under contracts of 2 June and 3 July 1899 and serviced in Berlin, Paris, London and Mexico. Issued under President Díaz and finance minister Limantour, it consolidated and refunded Mexico's older external obligations on the strength of the regime's restored credit, the gold clause reassuring European and American holders. The engraved Mexican eagle on cactus, grasping a serpent, asserts sovereign authority over the instrument.

title: Mexico Gold Treasury Bond
description: A bearer treasury bond (Bono del Tesoro al Portador) of the Federal Government of the United Mexican States, styled "6% Oro, 10 Años, de 1913." This certificate represents the smallest tranche of the Primera Serie "A": a face value of $Mex.195, rendered in gold and quoted simultaneously in francs (Fr.505), pounds (£20), marks (RMK.409), United States dollars ($EU.97), and Dutch guilders (Fl.P-B.242), and paying six per cent annually. The whole series comprised 222,358 bonds across three denominations—4,050 of $Mex.1,950, 10,298 of $Mex.975, and 208,010 of $Mex.195—for a total of $Mex.58,500,000. Dated México, 8 June 1913, the loan was floated by the Huerta government to fund a treasury strained by revolution, courting European and American capital through its multi-currency promise. The engraved national arms—the golden eagle upon a nopal, devouring a serpent—asserts sovereign credit at a moment when that sovereignty was bitterly contested.

title: Provisional Bearer Receipt for the 4% Ottoman Unified Converted Debt, Paris, 1933
description: A provisional bearer receipt (récépissé provisoire au porteur) issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, delivered against the surrender of one unit obligation of the 4% Emprunt Ottoman Dette Convertie Unifiée, nominal Frs. 500 (or £20, or Ltqs 22). It was issued through the Hamburg branch of the Deutsche Bank und Disconto-Gesellschaft and dated Paris, 1 October 1933. The receipt rests on the Commission decision of 9 June 1933, taken under Article 49 of the Treaty of Lausanne, which apportioned the nominal capital of the old Ottoman public debt among the empire's successor states. A central table sets out those États Débiteurs — Greece, Bulgaria, Yugoslavia, Albania, Transjordan, the Hedjaz, Asir, Yemen and others — with each one's proportion and share of capital. The document thus records the financial liquidation of a vanished empire: a debt once owed by Constantinople, redistributed by treaty across the territories carved from it, its engraved frame and guilloche borders lending the settlement the gravity of a sovereign bond.
creator: Conseil de la Dette Publique Repartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman
notes: Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, Reçu Provisoire au Porteur No. 0,951,240. Emprunt Ottoman Dette Convertie Unifiée 4%. German paying agent: Deutsche Bank und Disconto-Gesellschaft Filiale Hamburg. Coupon sheet attached below with orange cancellation stamps.

# Transcription

## Original (French):

**CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN**

**RÉCÉPISSÉ PROVISOIRE AU PORTEUR N° 0,951,240**

Délivré contre remise d'une obligation unitaire d'un capital nominal de Frs. 500 ou £ 20 ou Ltqs. 22

**DE L'EMPRUNT OTTOMAN DETTE CONVERTIE UNIFIÉE 4°/o.**

Conformément à la décision en date du 9 Juin 1933 de la Commission pour la répartition du capital nominal de la Dette Publique de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, créée en vertu de l'article 49 du Traité de Lausanne, le présent récépissé a été délivré, contre retrait de l'obligation suivante, par l'entreprise

**DEUTSCHE BANK UND DISCONTO-GESELLSCHAFT FILIALE HAMBURG**

Vu que la Turquie a fait l'objet d'une émission spéciale; que les quotas-parts de l'Italie (Dodécanèse), de la Palestine, de l'Irak et des États voisins, par suite de ces événements, à la date d'émission du présent récépissé, le capital nominal non amorti de l'emprunt suscité se trouve réduit aux montants spécifiés dans le tableau ci-dessous, lesquels correspondent aux États qui en sont les débiteurs à raison du change d'or.

**ÉTATS DÉBITEURS**

| État | Pourcentage % | Capital non amorti sur les 1,371,051 titres unitaires de l'émission primitive, à la date d'émission du présent récépissé | Capital non amorti de change d'or |
|------|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| | | on £ | on Ltqs. | Frs. | on £ | on Ltqs. |
|GRÈCE|48.46|81,518.843|3,380.754|3,719.820|61.65|2.47|5.71|
|BULGARIE|13.39|18,340.996|549.768|804.221|9.96|0.39|0.94|
|ROUMANIE|18.03|24,719.814|1,130.034|1,301.639|13.50|0.54|1.24|
|ALBANIE|7.27|12,677.818|567.113|557.856|9.25|0.37|0.81|
|YOUGOSLAVIE (y compris la Caza de Maaan)|7.27|9,928.298|300.970|221.662|4.75|0.19|0.46|
|HERJAVON|3.18|5,468.183|273.409|196.828|4.04|0.16|0.39|
|ASSYRIE|0.98|192.285|6.092|5.701|0.11|0.01|0.01|
|CILICIE|0.09|6,867.498|78.089|395.622|0.32|0.01|0.02|
|NEDJI|0.43|[unclear]|7.759|53.100|0.34|0.01|0.02|
|TOTAL|100.—|174,422.227|6,976.388|7,674.578|127.92|5.00|5.60|

Ce récépissé provisoire est exclusivement émis pour constater les droits des porteurs des titres retirés de la circulation sur les États débiteurs de l'Emprunt Ottoman. Le Conseil de la Dette ne pourra procéder, par le moyen de ce récépissé, à l'amortissement du capital nominal à leur charge. Les opérations futures qui pourraient être effectuées au moyen du présent récépissé provisoire, en vue de l'application des Accords conclus avec les États qui le remplacent, seront portées à la connaissance du public par avis publiés par le Conseil de la Dette.

**POUR CONTRÔLE,**

Par Délégation du Conseil:

**POUR LE CONSEIL DE LA DETTE PUBLIQUE RÉPARTIE DE L'ANCIEN EMPIRE OTTOMAN,**

Le Président:

Paris, le 1er Octobre 1933.

Delivered in exchange for a unit bond of a nominal capital of Frs. 500 or £ 20 or Ltqs. 22

**OF THE OTTOMAN LOAN UNIFIED CONVERTED DEBT 4°/o.**

In accordance with the decision dated June 9, 1933 of the Commission for the distribution of the nominal capital of the Public Debt of the Former Ottoman Empire, established by virtue of Article 49 of the Treaty of Lausanne, the present receipt has been delivered, in exchange for withdrawal of the following bond, by the enterprise

**DEUTSCHE BANK UND DISCONTO-GESELLSCHAFT HAMBURG BRANCH**

Given that Turkey has been subject to a special issue; that the share-holdings of Italy (Dodecanese), Palestine, Iraq and neighboring States, as a result of these events, at the date of issuance of the present receipt, the unamortized nominal capital of the aforementioned loan is found to be reduced to the amounts specified in the table below, which correspond to the States which are the debtors thereof at the ratio of the gold exchange.

**DEBTOR STATES**

[Table with same structure and data as above]

This provisional receipt is issued exclusively to establish the rights of the holders of bonds withdrawn from circulation against the debtor States of the Ottoman Loan. The Debt Council will not be able to proceed, by means of this receipt, to amortize the nominal capital at their charge. Future operations that could be carried out by means of this provisional receipt, with a view to the application of the Agreements concluded with the States that it replaces, will be brought to the attention of the public through notices published by the Debt Council.

**FOR CONTROL,**

By Delegation of the Council:

**FOR THE COUNCIL OF THE PUBLIC DEBT DISTRIBUTED FROM THE FORMER OTTOMAN EMPIRE,**

The President:

Paris, October 1st, 1933.

title: Société Ottomane des Chemins de Fer de Damas-Hamah Bond, Constantinople, 1910
description: This is a bearer bond (obligation au porteur) of the Société Ottomane des Chemins de Fer de Damas-Hamah et Prolongements, the Ottoman company chartered to build and operate railways radiating from Damascus and Hamah. It carries a face value of 500 francs, or 22 livres turques, and pays 4 percent annual interest — 20 francs, or 88 piastres — by half-yearly coupons of 10 francs falling due on 1 March and 1 September, with redemption at par by lot beginning 1 September 1910. Issued at Constantinople and dated 1 February 1910, it belongs to the "Emprunt 1909 — Homs-Tripoli" loan, a series of 43,747 obligations sanctioned by the shareholders' assembly of 16 December 1909 to finance the line's extension toward Tripoli on the Syrian coast. The bilingual French and Ottoman Turkish text, the recital of imperial firmans granting the concession, and the dense violet engraving register the close entanglement of European finance and Ottoman state infrastructure on the eve of the empire's final decade.
creator: Societe Ottomane des Chemins de Fer de Damas-Hamah et Prolongements

title: Ottoman Public Debt Bearer Receipt, Amsterdam, 1930
description: A bearer receipt (Reçu au Porteur) issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman, recording a nominal claim of Ltqs 2.20, equivalent to £2.0.0 (deux livres sterling) or Frs fr. 50. The document does not bear interest; it is exchangeable (Échangeable) for a bearer title of the Bons Représentatifs des Annuités Arriérées de la Dette Publique Ottomane, "Série A" 1928, with holders invited to combine receipts up to Ltqs 22 / £20 / Frs fr. 500 to claim unitary bonds. Issued at Amsterdam on 22 April 1930 by the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, par procuration, and bearing a Dutch 20-centime revenue stamp. It was created under Article IV of the contract of 13 June 1928 between the Turkish Republic and the bondholders, ratified by Turkish law of 1 December 1928, which apportioned the late Ottoman Empire's debt among its successor states. The trilingual face and Parisian printing (Imprimerie Chaix) attest to the international administration of a defunct empire's obligations; unredeemed receipts were to lapse to the benefit of the debtor states within one year.

title: Ottoman Public Debt Bearer Receipt, Amsterdam, 1930
description: This bearer receipt (Reçu au Porteur) was issued by the Conseil de la Dette Publique Répartie de l'Ancien Empire Ottoman for a nominal amount of Ltqs 4.40 / £4 / Frs fr. 100, the figures stated in Turkish, sterling and French currencies side by side. It records a claim under article IV, paragraph 9 of the contract of 13 June 1928 between the Turkish Republic and the bondholders' representatives, ratified by the Grande Assemblée Nationale de Turquie by the law of 1 December 1928, and is exchangeable for a bearer title of the "Bons Représentatifs des Annuités Arriérées de la Dette Publique Ottomane 'Série A' 1928" once holders assemble receipts totalling Ltqs 22, £20 or Frs fr. 500. Issued at Amsterdam on 22 April 1930 by the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, par procuration, and bearing a Dutch 40-centime revenue stamp, the document is a fragment of the protracted apportionment of the Ottoman public debt among the empire's successor states. Created solely for the mechanics of conversion, it expressly bore no interest and lapsed within a year, a plain administrative instrument marking the orderly liquidation of a vast inherited liability.

title: Republic of Peru 100-soles bond for the Arica customs house, Lima, 1871
description: A bearer bond (bono al portador) of the República del Perú with a face value of one hundred soles (cien soles), bearing six per cent annual interest and five per cent amortization, payable to the holder under the terms of the law of 4 February 1869. Issued at Lima on 17 July 1871, the bond was floated to fund construction of the customs house at Arica (la Aduana de Arica), authorized by the law of 28 January 1869, its proceeds earmarked for that single public work. The instrument illustrates how the guano-era Peruvian treasury raised dedicated capital for port and fiscal infrastructure on the southern coast, pledging customs revenue to service the loan. The engraved head bears the national arms flanked by flags, cannon, and a cornucopia; at right a seated female figure in classical drapery raises a staff topped by a Phrygian cap and holds a laurel wreath and sun-emblazoned shield, an allegory of liberty and the republic in the neoclassical idiom favored by nineteenth-century state securities.
creator: Republica del Peru / Direccion del Credito
notes: República del Perú, 100 Soles bearer bond, No. 165. Construcción de la Aduana de Arica, per law of January 28, 1869. 6% interest, 5% amortization. Lima, July 17, 1871. Signed by Felipe Medina and José Félix García, El Director del Crédito.

title: City of Warsaw Bearer Bond
description: A bearer bond (Obligation / Obligacja) issued by the Municipality of the City Capital of Warsaw for its 4½% loan of 1931. The face value is payable, at the holder's choice, in any of three currencies: 284 French francs, 99.188 Polish złoty, or 57.666 Swiss francs, with interest of 4½% per annum paid free of Polish income tax on securities. Drawn at Warsaw on 1 June 1931, the instrument forms part of a total emission of 55,912,500 French francs (equivalently 19,527,243.75 złoty or 11,352,993.75 Swiss francs), authorized by the Municipal Council on 10 June 1930 and sanctioned by the Government Commissioner overseeing the conversion of the city's earlier bonds. The triple-currency clause and the tax exemption reveal a municipality courting foreign capital while shielding it from currency risk in the unsettled financial climate following the 1929 crash. Crowning the green frame is Warsaw's heraldic Syrenka, the sword-bearing mermaid, set above the city's mural crown; engraved vignettes of the capital's monuments fill the lower corners.

title: Republic of Poland Dollar Premium Loan Bond, Warsaw, 1931
description: A bearer bond (obligacja) of the Republic of Poland (Rzeczpospolita Polska), issued as Series III of the Dollar Premium Loan (Premjowa Pożyczka Dolarowa). Its face value is five United States dollars — five dollars "STANÓW ZJEDNOCZONYCH AMERYKI" — fixed at the equivalent of 44.57 złotych, and it carries interest at 4% per annum. Drawn up at Warsaw and dated 1 February 1931 under the law of 1 February 1930, the instrument belongs to the inter-war Polish treasury's effort to fund itself by dollar-denominated borrowing from a domestic public, hedging against the złoty's frailty by pegging the obligation to a foreign currency and sweetening it with a lottery premium. The engraving is austere and architectural: a large numeral 5, restrained classical borders and a guilloche field, the typographic discipline of state credit rather than ornamental flourish.

title: Republic of Poland Dollar Premium Loan Bond, Warsaw, 1931
description: This is a bearer premium-loan bond (obligacja) of Series III of the Dollar Premium Loan (Premjowa Pożyczka Dolarowa) issued by the Republic of Poland (Rzeczpospolita Polska) through its Ministry of the Treasury. The face value is five United States dollars, rendered also as 44.57 złotych, with attached coupons each worth 0.10 dollar (0.89 złoty). Issued at Warsaw on 1 February 1931, under the authorising statute of 1 February 1930, it carried prize draws alongside scheduled redemption. Dollar denomination here speaks to a young republic seeking hard-currency credit and the confidence of holders amid the deflationary strains then settling over Europe; pairing fixed coupons with lottery premia was a familiar device for coaxing small savers into public debt. The restrained, symmetrical engraving and plain lettering favour sober official assurance over ornament.

title: Portugal External Debt Provisional Certificate
description: This is a provisional certificate of Three per Cent. Deferred Stock issued in the conversion of the external funded debt of Portugal, denominated in pounds sterling and printed in English for circulation in the London market. It certifies that the holder is entitled to a residual sum of deferred stock — here a mere "Three pence" entered by hand — bearing interest from 1 January 1863, with the promise that such fractional balances may be exchanged, once accumulated to a sufficient amount, for permanent bonds of £50, £100, £200, or £500. Drawn up by the Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government in London under decrees of 18 December 1852 and 23 March 1853, and printed by Whiting, the instrument records the unglamorous machinery of sovereign debt restructuring: the tidy ledger detail of a few pence held over speaks to a conversion that left small odd balances which only a paper acknowledgement could carry until they grew tradable. Its restrained engraved border and copperplate lettering project the sober creditworthiness London investors expected.
creator: Financial Agency of the Portuguese Government in London
notes: Conversion of the External Debt of Portugal, Decree of December 18, 1852. Provisional Certificate of Deferred Stock. No. 1463. Three Per Cent. Deferred Stock, interest from January 1, 1863. Amount: Three Pence. Signed by Financial Agent of the Portuguese Government in London. Printed by Whiting, London.
transcription:
# Transcription

**CONVERSION OF THE EXTERNAL DEBT OF PORTUGAL,**
**UNDER THE DECREE OF 18th DECEMBER, 1852.**

---

**PROVISIONAL CERTIFICATE OF DEFERRED STOCK.**

---

No. [1463]                                                                                          [Date]

In conformity with Article 25th of the Regulations approved of by a Decree of Her Most Faithful Majesty the Queen of Portugal, under date of 23d March, 1853, by which the Financial Agency in London is authorised to carry out the Conversion of the External Funded Debt of Portugal, in pursuance of the previous Decree of 18th December, 1852:

This is to Certify THAT the HOLDER hereof is entitled to the Sum of

**Three Pounds** [amount in handwriting - illegible]

**THREE PER CENT. DEFERRED STOCK,** bearing Interest from the 1st of January, 1863, which Sum proceeds from the above Conversion.

---

This Certificate will be exchanged on presentation with others, making up a sufficient amount, for Bonds of any of the following Series, viz. £50, £100, £200, or £500, into which the said Stock is divided; and for any remaining Balance a fresh Certificate will be given.

---

FINANCIAL AGENT OF THE PORTUGUESE GOVERNMENT IN LONDON.

WHITING, LONDON.

---

title: Portuguese External Fund Zero-Coupon Bond, Lisbon, 1902
description: This is a single zero-coupon bond (Título Especial Sem Juro) of the Fundo Externo Portuguez, third series, issued by the Junta do Crédito Público at Lisbon and dated 31 December 1902. It carries a face value of 308$000 réis, set out in parallel as £6 12s 8d, 166.67 francs, 135.34 marks, and 79.34 florins — the multi-currency tariff of a debt placed across the European exchanges. Bearing no interest, the title promised only its eventual reimbursement, drawn by public lottery from a total emission of 477,517 obligations; the holder, as the text concedes, gained "nenhuma vantagem além do seu opportuno reembolso." Such interest-free, sinking-fund paper let a hard-pressed treasury spread redemption over time under the public-credit reforms decreed in June 1862. A French one-per-cent fiscal stamp and the inked note "rembᵗ juillet 1903" record its circulation and retirement in Paris. The engraved Portuguese arms within laurel and acanthus lend sober official dignity.
creator: Junta de Crédito Público
notes: Fundo Externo Portuguez, Título Especial sem Juro, 3.ª Série (Portuguese External Fund, Special Zero-Coupon Bond). No. 303486. title: Hope & Co. Certificate of Russian 6% Bank Assignations, Amsterdam, 1825
description: This bilingual Dutch-and-French certificate entitles its bearer to an inscription of 1,000 roubles, bearing interest at 6 per cent, in Russian Bank Assignations (Bank-Assignatiën / Assignations de Banque) — the assignat paper currency of the Imperial Russian debt — registered in the Groot-Boek (Grand-Livre, or Great Book) of the public debt at St. Petersburg in the name of the administrative office at Amsterdam. It was issued at Amsterdam on 11 May 1825 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and the widow (Wed. / Veuve) W. Borski, the Dutch bankers who managed Russia's foreign borrowing. Ten semi-annual coupons accompanied it, running to 1 July 1829. The document attests to Amsterdam's role as the financing hub for Russian sovereign credit, repackaging the Tsar's rouble debt into denominated certificates that Dutch investors could hold and trade. The engraved frame and restrained classical lettering convey the sober respectability the issuing houses sought to project.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski
notes: Certificaat van 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien / Certificat d'Inscription Russe à 6 pour Cent en Assignations de Banque. No. 1326, R° 1000 rubles. Inscribed in the Grand Livre at St. Petersburg. Managed by Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Wed. W. Borski. Amsterdam, May 11, 1825. Bilingual Dutch/French. Ten half-yearly coupons to July 4, 1829.

title: USSR State Loan Bond
description: A bearer bond (облигация) of the State Loan for the Restoration and Development of the National Economy of the USSR, with a face value of twenty-five rubles, issued in 1946. The first of Stalin's postwar mass subscription loans, it was floated to fund reconstruction after the devastation of the Great Patriotic War, channelling the savings of citizens—often through compulsory payroll subscription—into the state treasury rather than the consumer market, thereby financing recovery while damping inflationary pressure on a goods-starved economy. The engraved design is austere and statist: the Soviet emblem, with hammer, sickle, globe and encircling wheat, crowns a frame of fine guilloche lining, while heavy slab lettering announces the denomination in figures and words. The visual language is one of bureaucratic solemnity rather than ornament, presenting public debt as an act of patriotic participation in rebuilding the war-ravaged Soviet state.
creator: Government of the USSR
notes: USSR, State Loan for Restoration and Development of the National Economy (Государственный Заем Восстановления и Развития Народного Хозяйства СССР), 1946. No. 012594, Series 33, Category 98. 25 rubles. Soviet state emblem design.

title: Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan Bond, Provisional Government, 1917
description: This is a bearer lottery bond (билетъ) of the Государственный внутренній 4½% выигрышный заемъ 1917 года — the Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan of 1917 — with a face value of two hundred rubles (въ двѣсти рублей), bearing 4½ per cent interest and entitling the holder to participate in the loan's prize drawings. The heading states that it was issued on the authority of the resolution of the Provisional Government of 11 August 1917, with the final coupon dated 16 January 1928. Floated in the desperate fiscal summer that followed the February Revolution, the loan sought to draw private savings toward a treasury drained by war and political collapse, its lottery feature an inducement to a public unwilling to lend on patriotism alone; within months the Bolshevik seizure of power would repudiate such obligations. An engraved vignette of a draped allegorical woman attended by putti crowns the certificate, lending an air of established credit to a state already near dissolution.
creator: Imperial Russian Government (Provisional Government)
notes: Russian Imperial State Internal 4½% Prize Loan of 1917 (Государственный Внутренний 4½% Выигрышный Заем 1917 года). Series No. 15800, Bond No. 32, Category 2. 200 rubles nominal. Bearer. Issued per decree of August 11–19, 1917 (Provisional Government). Last coupon January 16, 1928.
transcription:
# Russian State Internal 4½% Lottery Loan Bond of 1917, 200 Rubles (Second Series) — Transcription

## Original:

**Разрядъ второй.**

(upper centre: engraved allegorical vignette — a draped female figure attended by putti; engraver's mark **ABNCo** at lower right of the vignette)

*(left, in red)* Серія номеръ
**15800**

*(right, in red)* Билетъ номеръ
**32**

**ГОСУДАРСТВЕННЫЙ ВНУТРЕННІЙ 4½% ВЫИГРЫШНЫЙ ЗАЕМЪ 1917 ГОДА,**
выпущенный на основаніи постановленія Временнаго Правительства отъ 11-го Августа 1917 года.

**БИЛЕТЪ**
**ВЪ ДВѢСТИ РУБЛЕЙ НАРИЦАТЕЛЬНЫХЪ**
**НА ПРЕДЪЯВИТЕЛЯ.**

Заемъ сей внесенъ въ Государственную Долговую Книгу подъ названіемъ "Государственный внутренній четырехъ съ половиной процентный выигрышный заемъ 1917 года".

Билеты сего займа выпускаются именные и на предъявителя, достоинствомъ въ 200 рублей нарицательныхъ.

Владѣлецъ настоящаго билета участвуетъ въ займѣ суммою въ 200 рублей, приносящей четыре съ половиной процента въ годъ до полнаго погашенія билета вышедшаго въ тиражъ выигрышемъ.

Доходъ по билетамъ второго разряда уплачивается два раза въ годъ: 16 Іюля и 16 Января по предъявленіи купоновъ въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка, Казначействахъ, а равно и въ Государственныхъ Сберегательныхъ Кассахъ.

Теченіе процентовъ по билетамъ настоящаго разряда начинается 16 Января 1918 года.

Тиражи выигрышей, происходящіе публично въ мѣстахъ, назначаемыхъ Министромъ Финансовъ, производятся для настоящаго разряда 16 Іюля и 16 Января каждаго года.

Первый тиражъ выигрышей для второго разряда займа назначается на 16 Іюля 1918 года.

Выплата выигрышей производится въ Конторахъ и Отдѣленіяхъ Государственнаго Банка спустя три мѣсяца послѣ тиража выигрышей, по предъявленіи билетовъ со всѣми купонами, срокъ оплаты коихъ наступаетъ послѣ срока тиража, на которые палъ выигрышъ на предъявляемый билетъ; стоимость недостающихъ купоновъ будетъ удержана изъ суммы выигрыша.

Приносящій означенный заемъ доходъ въ размѣрѣ 4½% въ годъ, а равно уплачиваемые по займу выигрыши, освобождаются навсегда отъ сбора съ доходовъ отъ денежныхъ капиталовъ.

Оплата выигрышей производится въ теченіе 30 лѣтъ со дня, назначеннаго для начала таковой уплаты, а купоновъ — въ теченіе 10 лѣтъ по наступленіи по нимъ сроковъ.

Билеты сего займа принимаются по цѣнамъ, устанавливаемымъ на каждое полугодіе Министромъ Финансовъ и публикуемымъ черезъ Правительствующій Сенатъ, въ залоги: а) по казеннымъ подрядамъ и поставкамъ, б) по обезпеченію таможенныхъ пошлинъ.

*(signature block, right)* Управляющій Государственною Коммиссіею Погашенія Долговъ [signature — illegible]
*(left)* Бухгалтеръ [signature — illegible] (red control stamp overprinted)

**Срокъ послѣдняго купона 16 Января 1928 года.**

## English Translation:

**Second Series.**

*(left)* Series number **15800**
*(right)* Ticket number **32**

**RUSSIAN STATE INTERNAL 4½% LOTTERY LOAN OF THE YEAR 1917,** issued on the authority of the resolution of the Provisional Government of 11 August 1917.

**BOND**
**FOR TWO HUNDRED RUBLES NOMINAL**
**TO BEARER.**

This loan has been entered in the State Debt Register under the name "State Internal four-and-one-half per cent Lottery Loan of the year 1917."

The bonds of this loan are issued both registered and to bearer, in the denomination of 200 nominal rubles.

The holder of the present bond participates in the loan in the sum of 200 rubles, yielding four and one-half per cent per year until the full redemption of the bond drawn for prize.

Income on second-series bonds is paid twice a year: on 16 July and 16 January, upon presentation of the coupons at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank, at the Treasuries, and likewise at the State Savings Banks.

The running of interest on the bonds of the present series begins on 16 January 1918.

The prize drawings, held publicly in places designated by the Minister of Finance, are conducted for the present series on 16 July and 16 January of each year.

The first prize drawing for the second series of the loan is set for 16 July 1918.

Payment of prizes is made at the Offices and Branches of the State Bank three months after the prize drawing, upon presentation of the bonds together with all coupons whose payment date falls after the date of the drawing, on which bond the prize was drawn; the value of any missing coupons will be deducted from the prize sum.

The said loan income at the rate of 4½% per year, as well as the prizes paid on the loan, are forever exempt from the tax on income from monetary capital.

Payment of prizes is made within 30 years from the day appointed for the commencement of such payment, and of coupons — within 10 years from the falling due of their terms.

The bonds of this loan are accepted, at prices established for each half-year by the Minister of Finance and published through the Governing Senate, as security: (a) for state contracts and supplies, and (b) for the guaranteeing of customs duties.
