title: City of Baku 5% Municipal Loan Bond, 1910
description: A bearer bond (Облигація) of the 5 per cent Municipal Loan of the City of Baku, dated 1910 and printed trilingually in Russian, French, and English so as to court foreign capital. The certificate carries a face value of 189 rubles, fixed for international subscribers at 504 francs or £20 sterling, against a total issue of nearly 26,000,000 rubles, and promises a five per cent annual return. Issued by the municipality of Baku—then the booming oil port of the Russian Empire on the Caspian, in present-day Azerbaijan—the loan funnels European savings into the city's rapid expansion. The restrained engraved border and the civic coat of arms framing the central denomination present the obligation in a sober, mercantile guise, the visual rhetoric of creditworthiness rather than ornament: a provincial city addressing the Paris and London markets in their own terms.
creator: City of Baku
notes: 5% Loan of the City of Baku, 1910 (5% Заем Города Баку 1910 года). No. 20783. 189 rubles / 504 francs / 20 shillings sterling. Total issue: 25,999,973 rubles = £2,857,140 = Fr. 71,999,928. Trilingual Russian/French/German. Bearer. Red official stamp.

title: Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan Bond
description: This is a bearer bond of the Imperial Russian Three per Cent Loan of 1859, an inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia representing a capital of One Hundred Pounds Sterling (L.St. 100). The holder draws three per cent annually, paid half-yearly in London by Messrs. J. Thomson, T. Bonar & Co. on 16 October and 16 April as two warrants of one pound ten shillings each, or at the holder's option in Berlin by F. Mart. Magnus at six and three-quarter Thalers per pound; coupons run to 16 April 1869, after which a talon yields a fresh sheet. Floated abroad in sterling and serviced through London and Berlin houses, the loan reveals an autocracy reaching into Western capital markets to fund obligations its own savings could not bear. The bond cites the Imperial Ukase of 20 March 1859 and a sinking fund amortising at one and a quarter per cent yearly. The engraved frame, with its restrained scrollwork and copperplate script, projects the probity such cross-border borrowing demanded; affixed Danish revenue stamps mark a later transfer.
creator: Imperial Russian Government
notes: Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan 1859. No. 41150. £100 Sterling. Inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia. Interest payable halfyearly in London via Thomson, Bonar & Co. and in Berlin via F. Mart. Magnus at 6¾ thalers/£. Talon with dividend warrants. Two Danish revenue stamps at top left.
transcription:
# Transcription of Imperial Russian Three Per Cent Loan 1859 Bond

**IMPERIAL RUSSIAN THREE PER CENT LOAN 1859.**
**ONE HUNDRED POUNDS STERLING**

Inscription in the Great Book of the Public Debt of Russia representing a Capital of **ONE HUNDRED Pounds Sterling**.

Entered the _______ Book _______ folio 1546

The bearer of this Inscription is entitled to an annual Interest of **THREE per CENT**, to be paid half-yearly in London, One Pound ten shillings on the **1st OCTOBER** and **1st NOVEMBER** and One Pound ten shillings on the **1st MAY** and **1st JUNE** by Messrs **J. Thomson, T. Bonar & Co.**, or at the option of the holder in Berlin by **Mr. F. Mart, Magnus in Thalers** at the rate of six and three quarter Thalers per Pound Sterling on the said dates.

Attached to this Inscription are half-yearly dividend warrants up to **18 APRIL 1869**, with a talon. After the expiration of that date new dividend warrants will be delivered in exchange for the talon.

Extract from the Regulations of the **IMPERIAL COMMISSION OF THE SINKING FUND** (Code of Russian laws Tom. XI edit. 1857.)

§. 89. The payment of interminable annuities by the State, as well as in times of war as of peace, without any distinction whatsoever, whether the creditor be of a friendly or of a hostile nation.

§. 90. If a foreigner, proprietor of an Inscription, shall die, the Inscriptions shall pass to his heirs, in the order of succession established by the laws of the country, of which he was a subject.

§. 91. Investments in the irredeemable funded debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor are exempt from all imposts whatsoever.

Extract of the **IMPERIAL UKASE** of the **20 March 1859**, addressed to the Minister of Finance.

For the amortization of this Loan a special sinking fund, to be kept separate from the other Loans, will be assigned to begin from the year 1859, which fund will form annually 1½ % of the capital of the Loan. On the closure of this fund, for the purpose of buying in the inscriptions at the exchange of the day. If the holder of an inscription of this Loan should wish to have the same repurchased at any time at such price as he may require, the inscription to the bearer will be then changed into an inscription in the name of the holder, without dividend warrants, and the interest due on such inscriptions will be paid at the **IMPERIAL COMMISSION OF THE SINKING FUND** at **St. Petersburg** every half year, at the period fixed here above.

Countersigned: **THOMSON, BONAR & CO.**
per pro. **F. MART MAGNUS,**
Contractors.

Director of the Commission [**signature - illegible**]

---

## Notable Markings:

- Serial number: **No. 41150** (appears three times on document)
- Denomination: **U.S. $100** (shown in decorative oval)
- Two Danish revenue stamps affixed to upper left (pink/red colored, denominations visible: "12 KRONER" and "30 ØRE")
- Ornamental borders throughout in classical engraved style
- Decorative corner elements and scroll work
- Handwritten folio reference: "1546"

title: City of Bari Prize Loan Bond
description: This is a bearer prize-loan bond (Obbligazione al Portatore) of 100 lire, redeemable at 150 lire, issued by the Consiglio Comunale of the Città di Bari delle Puglie under its Prestito a Premi. The loan was resolved by municipal ordinance of 31 December 1867 and approved by Royal Decree of 11 June 1868; the printed margin records an Italian emission of 9,000,000 lire in 90,000 bonds (900 series of 100 numbers each), with amortisation set at 27,350,000 lire, divided between 13,500,000 for redemption and 13,850,000 for the payment of premi. Issued at Bari on 1 May 1900 over the signature of the Sindaco, with Francesco Compagnoni of Milan as assuntore. A printed plan of drawings and an attached Annotazione dei Premi sheet govern the periodic lottery extractions through which the obligation is amortised. Such municipal prize loans let a southern comune raise public-works capital cheaply by selling the chance of a prize in place of ordinary interest, blending civic borrowing with the appeal of the lottery. The restrained engraved frame and classicising allegorical vignette announce municipal authority rather than commercial enterprise.
creator: Consiglio Comunale di Bari delle Puglie
notes: Città di Bari delle Puglie, Prestito a Premi (Prize Loan). Deliberated December 31, 1867; approved by Royal Decree June 11, 1868. Emission of L. 3,500,000. Serie 317, No. 049. Obbligazione di Lire 100 al portatore, rimborsabile con Lire 150. Lottery/amortization schedule (Piano delle Estrazioni) and prize payment sheet (Annotazione dei Premi) at right. Bari, May 11, 1869. Signed by Il Sindaco.

title: Principality of Bulgaria Gold Loan Bond
description: A bearer bond (Облигация / Obligation) of the Principality of Bulgaria, one of 212,000 issued under the Bulgarian Government 5% Gold Loan of 1902. Its face value is 500 francs, equal to 500 leva, with equivalences printed for sterling (£18 16s 0d), Austrian crowns (476) and Dutch guilders (240); it bears five per cent in gold and is redeemable at par over fifty years. Issued at Sofia and dated 1/14 January 1903 under the Old- and New-Style calendars, it is signed for the Minister of Finance, M. K. Sarafov, in four languages — Bulgarian, French, German and English — and is expressly secured on the state's tobacco duties ("Gagé sur les droits des tabacs"). For a principality only recently autonomous from the Ottoman Porte and still nominally tributary, the bond shows a young treasury reaching into Western capital markets, pledging a dependable excise to foreign holders to fund its gold obligations. The engraved frame answers that ambition with dense guilloche and arabesque borders in the security-printing manner of the period, centred on a medallion bearing the crowned shield and rampant lion of the Bulgarian arms — an assertion of sovereign credit as much as of statehood.
creator: Principality of Bulgaria

title: Principality of Bulgaria 5% Gold Loan (conditions)
description: This sheet sets out the conditions governing a 5% gold loan of the Principality of Bulgaria, headed by a Bulgarian banner (УСЛОВИЯ НА ЗАЕМА) and laid out in four parallel columns — French, Russian, German and English — with a half-yearly amortisation table. The bonds bear five per centum yearly interest on their nominal value, paid against two equal coupons due on 1/14 March and 1/14 September, each worth 12.50 gold leva or its equivalent in francs, roubles, reichsmark, sterling, Austrian crowns or Dutch florins, payable across a dozen capitals from Sofia and Paris to St. Petersburg, Berlin, London and Amsterdam. The whole issue of 212,000 bonds is redeemable at par by drawings over fifty years, the first set for 1/14 August 1903 and the last in 1953, with early redemption reserved from 1/14 September 1913; the loan is served through the Russian State Bank and the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas, and the French text governs. The multilingual frame and Franco-Russian backing reveal a young Balkan principality binding its credit to several hard currencies at once to borrow on foreign markets.
notes: Reverse/back page of goetzmann0734; loan conditions in four languages (Bulgarian, French, German, Russian) and amortization table (Tilgungsplan / Tableau d'Amortissement)

title: Province of Buenos Aires Consolidated Internal Debt Bond, La Plata, 1935
description: This is a bearer bond of one hundred pesos moneda nacional, part of the consolidated internal debt (Deuda Interna Consolidada) of the Province of Buenos Aires, Argentina, issued under provincial Law Nº 4293 of 27 June 1935 and dated at La Plata, 1 July 1935. The title earns 5% annual interest with an additional 1% of cumulative annual amortisation, interest payable quarterly in February, May, August and November against the attached coupons, a near-complete sheet of which survives. Issued amid the fiscal strains of the early 1930s, the bond reflects a province consolidating earlier obligations into a single internal loan placed with domestic holders in national currency rather than borrowing abroad. The engraved frame is conventionally academic: classical allegorical figures flank the provincial arms above the denomination, the restrained ornament projecting the sober creditworthiness such an instrument was meant to convey.
creator: Provincia de Buenos Aires
notes: Provincia de Buenos Aires, Deuda Interna Consolidada, Serie A, Ley N° 4293, 100 Pesos (Cien Pesos), 5% annual interest; large attached coupon sheet; No. 133,050

title: Province of Buenos Aires Consolidated Internal Debt Coupon Sheet, Law No. 4393
description: This is a sheet of detachable interest coupons belonging to a registered bond of the Consolidated Internal Debt (Deuda Interna Consolidada, Serie A) of the Province of Buenos Aires, issued in pesos moneda nacional under Article 9 of provincial Law No. 4393, with the public-debt provisions of Articles 50, 59 and 80 of Law No. 4225, and put into circulation by executive emission decrees dated 11 September 1925, 20 November 1926 and 16 December 1926. The reproduced decree texts authorise consolidation issues reaching into the hundreds of millions of pesos. More than a hundred small pink coupons, each bearing an engraved number from 19 onward, fill the sheet; the opening run of lower numbers has already been clipped away, marking interest periods drawn down against the principal. The dense guilloché lathe-work and medallioned frames are the standard anti-forgery engraving of the era's securities, asserting the document's negotiability and the province's pledge of payment. The sheet documents a Buenos Aires province leaning on long-term funded debt to consolidate and refinance its obligations through the central provincial loan office.

title: Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortizable Loan of 1895 (reverse: amortization plan and coupon talons)
description: The reverse of a bond of the Kingdom of Serbia's 4% Amortizable Loan of 1895, a unification loan with a nominal capital of 355,292,000 francs secured on the revenues of the state monopolies. The sheet is dominated by a trilingual redemption schedule — TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT, AMORTIZACIONI PLAN and TILGUNGS-PLAN — setting out, year by year, the sums drawn and the bonds redeemed until extinction; a note affirms that the French text of the obligations governs. Below run the detachable coupon talons (TALON), each headed Kraljevina Srbija. Dated at Belgrade 1/13 August 1895, the instrument records a small Balkan kingdom consolidating its external debts under foreign-bondholder oversight, the monopoly pledge and the careful sinking-fund arithmetic standing in for the fiscal discipline its creditors demanded. The plain three-column typography is utilitarian rather than ornamental, foregrounding the numbers themselves.
creator: Kingdom of Serbia
notes: Reverse/back page of a Kingdom of Serbia (Kraljevina Srbija) 4.5% loan bond; trilingual amortization table (Tableau d'Amortissement / Amortizacioni Plan / Tilgungs-Plan) in French, Serbian, and German; Talon section with 4.5% coupon stubs
transcription:
# Kingdom of Serbia 4% Amortizable Loan of 1895 — Reverse (Amortization Plan, Security Clause and Coupon Talons) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Layout note: The reverse carries three zones. TOP — a large trilingual redemption/amortization table, printed **inverted (rotated 180°)** relative to the coupons, so that it reads right-way-up when the sheet is turned. CENTRE — a trilingual explanatory/security note (German | Serbian Cyrillic | French, in three parallel columns) with signatures, also printed inverted relative to the coupons. BOTTOM — a grid of detachable coupon "talons," printed right-way-up. Languages present: **German, Serbian (Cyrillic) and French**, matching the trilingual layout throughout. The dual dates such as "1/13" and "8/20" give the Julian [Old Style, "a. St." / "ст. кал."] and Gregorian dates.)*

---

### 1. Amortization table (top of sheet)

Three parallel headings, one per language:

**TILGUNGS-PLAN**
für das Königlich Serbische 4 %ige Anlehen vom Jahre 1895 im Nominalbetrage von 355,292,000 Francs.

**АМОРТИЗАЦИОНИ ПЛАН**
за краљ. Српски 4 %, Зајам од 1895. године у номиналној вредности од 355.292.000 динара.

**TABLEAU D'AMORTISSEMENT**
du Royaume de Serbie de l'emprunt amortissable 4 % de l'année 1895, de Francs 355,292,000 capital nominal.

**Column headers** (repeated across each of the six side-by-side panels, in all three languages):
- German: *Datum (a. St.) der Zahlungen* | *Tilgung*
- Serbian: *Рок наплата* | *Отплата*
- French: *Dates (a. St.) des Payements* | *AMORTISSEMENT*

**Table structure and content:** Each row gives a year, the semi-annual payment dates (trilingual: e.g. *13 Janvier · 1. Јануар · 13. Jänner* and *13 Juillet · 1. Јули · 13. Juli*), and the amount amortized ("Tilgung / Отплата / Amortissement") in francs. The schedule runs year by year from **1896 to 1967**, with the sums drawn rising steadily to extinction. This is a very dense numeric grid; individual cell values are printed small and are **not exhaustively transcribed here** — representative, clearly legible figures follow (numbers not confidently read are marked):

- 1896: 432,000 · 440,000
- 1897: 460,000 · 460,000 [approx.]
- 1898: 480,000 · 480,000 [approx.]
- … *(intervening years — dense numeric grid, [not individually transcribed])* …
- 1958: 5,080,000 · 5,170,000
- 1959: 5,280,000 · [illegible]
- 1963–1965: 6,310,000 · 6,440,000 · 6,560,000 · 6,700,000 · 6,830,000 [approx.]
- 1966: 6,970,000 · 7,100,000
- 1967: 7,250,000 · 7,400,000

Carry-forward subtotal line (each panel foot): **À reporter — За пренос — Fürtrag** Frcs. 13,242,000 [example value; other panels differ, values [not all legible]].

Final totals row: (left column subtotal) 212,652,000 · **Total — Укупно — Summe** Frcs. **355,292,000**.

---

### 2. Trilingual security & redemption note (centre)

**German column:**

Mit der Verwaltung der Sicherheiten, welche für dieses Anlehen bestellt sind, ist eine autonome Monopolverwaltung betraut, welche auf Grund des Gesetzes vom 8./20. Juli 1895 eingesetzt worden ist. Dieselbe verwaltet selbstständig die Monopole und die Einnahmen, welche für dieses Anlehen als Pfand gegeben sind, nämlich: das Tabakmonopol, das Salzmonopol, das Petroleummonopol, sowie auch die übrigen bestehenden Staatsmonopole und die Einnahmen aus dem Stempel- und Getränkegebühren. Ferner sind die Reineinnahmen der Eisenbahnen, die Zolleinnahmen und die Erträgnisse der Obrtsteuer ebenfalls an die Casse der Monopolverwaltung abzuliefern. Zu dem Behufe sind die Direction der Serbischen Staatseisenbahnen, sowie sämmtliche Zollämter Serbiens unwiderruflich angewiesen, ihre ganzen Reineinnahmen jeden Monat, respective jede zehn Tage, unmittelbar an die Monopolverwaltung abzuliefern.

Der Verwaltungsrath der Monopolverwaltung ist gesetzlich verpflichtet, die Reineinnahmen aus dem Tabak- und Petroleummonopolen, eventuell nach Einlösung der Salzbons aus dem Salzmonopol, dann aus dem Stempel- und Getränkegebühren, endlich alle Eingänge aus den Eisenbahnen, dann aus den Zoll- und Obrtgebühren, in erster Reihe und ausschliesslich zur Bezahlung der fälligen Coupons und der verlosten Obligationen dieses Anlehens, wie auch der Coupons der Losenlehen v. J. 1881 und der Treffer dieser Losenlehen v. J. 1881 [und 1888] zu verwenden und an die für den Dienst dieser Anlehen bestimmten Zahlstellen in der Weise abzuliefern, dass letztere spätestens 14 Tage vor jedem Termine in den Besitz des vollen, jeweilig zur Auszahlung der fälligen Coupons und der verlosten Obligationen erforderlichen Betrages gesetzt sein sollen. Demnach dürfen diese Einnahmen und Erträgnisse zu gar keinem anderen Zwecke als für den vorstehenden verwendet werden.

Falls die Einnahmen der Monopolverwaltung für den jährlichen Dienst dieses Anlehens nicht ausreichen sollten, ist die Königlich Serbische Regierung verpflichtet, den eventuellen halbjährigen Fehlbetrag aus anderen Einkünften zu ergänzen.

Der französische Text der Obligation ist massgebend.

Die nicht zur Einlösung gelangten Coupons verjähren 5 Jahre, und die verlosten Obligationen 30 Jahre nach ihrer Fälligkeit.

Belgrad, am 1./13. August 1895.

**Serbian column (Cyrillic):**

Гарантијама датим за овај зајам управља Самостална Монополска Управа, установљена на основу закона од 8/20. Јула 1895. Она управља самостално Монополима и приходима заложеним за овај зајам, т. ј. Монополом Дувана, Соли, Петролеума, као и осталим државним Монополима који постоје и приходима од таксених и крчмарских марака. Даље се имају предавати благајници Монополске управе чист Железнички и Царински приход, као и приход од Обртне [пореза]. Тога ради упућене су неопозиво Дирекције српских државних Железница, као и све српске царинарнице, да све своје чисте приходе сваког месеца, односно сваки десети дан, предају непосредно Монополској Управи.

Управни одбор Монополске управе законом је обавезан, да све чисте приходе Дуванског и Петролеумског Монопола, и по исплати Соних Обвезница, [приходе] Соленог Монопола, даље од таксених и крчмарских Марака и напослетку све Железничке и Царинске приходе, као и приход од Обртне [пореза], употребљава [првенствено] и искључиво на исплату купона и извучених Обвезница од овог зајма, као и на исплату купона лутријског зајма од 1881 године и згодитака из истог зајма и Дуванских лозова од 1888 године, и да их за службу овог зајма одређеним исплатним местима предаје тако, да ови буду 14 дана пре сваког одређеног рока потпуно поседовали целокупну потребну своту за исплату купона и извучених Обвезница потребиту.

Ако приходи Монополске Управе не би били довољни за годишњу службу овога зајма, обавезна је краљевска српска влада, да [могући] полугодишњи недостатак другим приходима попуни.

Француски текст на Обвезницама меродаван је.

На исплату неподнесени купони застаревају после пет, а извучене Обвезнице после тридесет година, од дана њихова рока плаћања.

Београд, 1/13. Августа 1895.

**French column:**

*(The visible French text begins mid-sentence at the words below; the opening clause — supplied in the German and Serbian columns, "an autonomous Monopoly Administration set up under the law of…" — is not legibly printed above this first line.)*

… [du] 8/20 Juillet 1895. Elle administre en toute indépendance les monopoles et les recettes qui ont été données en gage à cet emprunt, savoir: le monopole des tabacs, le monopole du sel, le monopole du pétrole, de même que les autres monopoles d'État existants et les recettes du timbre et des taxes sur les boissons. De plus, les recettes nettes des chemins de fer, les recettes des douanes et le produit de l'Octroi devront être également versés à la caisse de l'Administration des monopoles. A cet effet, la Direction de chemin de fer de l'État et celles des douanes ont reçu l'ordre irrévocable de verser, la première chaque mois, les autres tous les dix jours leurs recettes nettes directement à l'Administration des Monopoles.

Le Conseil de l'Administration des monopoles est obligé par la loi à employer les recettes nettes des monopoles du tabac et du pétrole, du monopole du sel après le remboursement des bons de sel, le produit du timbre et des taxes sur les boissons, enfin les recettes nettes des chemins de fer, des douanes et de l'Octroi, en premier lieu et exclusivement au paiement des coupons échus et des obligations sorties de cet emprunt, ainsi qu'au paiement des coupons de l'emprunt à lots de 1881 et des titres sortis au tirage des emprunts à lots de 1881 et de 1888. Il est également obligé par la loi à faire parvenir les recettes précitées aux établissements ou maisons de banque désignés pour le service de ces emprunts de telle façon que les dits établissements ou maisons de banque soient en possession 15 jours au plus tard avant chaque échéance de la somme entière nécessaire au paiement des coupons et des obligations sorties. En conséquence, les recettes et revenus précités ne peuvent recevoir aucune affectation autre que celles ci-dessus mentionnées.

Dans le cas où les recettes de l'Administration des monopoles ne seraient pas suffisantes pour assurer le service annuel de cet emprunt, le Gouvernement Royal Serbe est tenu de parfaire, au moyen d'autres ressources, la somme qui pourrait éventuellement manquer pour un semestre.

Le texte français des obligations fait foi.

Les coupons non présentés à l'encaissement sont prescrits après cinq ans et les obligations sorties après trente ans de leur échéance.

Belgrade, le 1/13 Août 1895.

---

### 3. Signatures (centre, beneath the note)

**Le ministre des finances: — Министар финансија: — Der Finanzminister:**
[handwritten signature, illegible — reads approximately "А. Ђ. …овић" / "A. Đ. …ović"]

**Pour contrôle: — За контролу — Controllirt:** [handwritten signature/flourish]

**Le Délégué du Gouvernement Royal Serbe: — Der Delegirte der Königlich [Serbischen Regierung]:** [handwritten signature]

Left-margin caption (rotated): **Пуномоћник краљевске Српске владе: — [Bevollmächtigter der] Serbischen Regierung:** [handwritten signature]

---

### 4. Coupon talons (bottom of sheet)

A grid of small detachable coupons, each identical in wording except for the maturity date, plus one master **TALON** block at the right.

**Master talon block:**

**ТАЛОН.**
**Краљевина Србија.**
4 %/₀ зајам с отплатом.
По исплати последњег овде наштампаног купона, издаје се доносиоцу овог талона нов купонски табак.

**Individual coupon (template, repeated):**

**Краљевина Србија.**
4 %/₀ зајам с отплатом.
Купон исплатив у злату 1/13. [Јануара/Јула] 19[..]
у Београду и Паризу: са 10 { Динара / Франака }
„ Берлину: са 8.10 Марака
„ Бечу: са 4 аустријске форинте у злату.

Coupons run semi-annually (1/13 January and 1/13 July). Clearly legible dates on the visible coupons include **1/13 Јануара 1943, 1/13 Јула 1943, 1/13 Јануара 1944, 1/13 Јула 1944** (top row), with the remaining boxes continuing the series (further dates ≈ 1942–1947, several printed too small to read each digit confidently — [individual coupon dates not all legible]).

---

## English Translation:

### 1. Amortization table

**AMORTIZATION PLAN** — for the Royal Serbian 4% Loan of the year 1895, of a nominal amount of 355,292,000 Francs (given identically in German, Serbian and French headings).

Column headers: *Dates (Old Style) of Payments* | *Amortization (Redemption)*.

The plan lists, year by year from **1896 to 1967**, the two semi-annual payment dates and the sum amortized (redeemed) in that year, the sums rising to full extinction. Individual figures form a dense numeric grid and are not transcribed in full; the grand total of the amounts redeemed is **355,292,000 Francs** (with a running column subtotal of 212,652,000 and per-panel "carried forward" subtotals such as 13,242,000 Frcs).

### 2. Trilingual security & redemption note (identical in the three columns; the French text is declared to govern)

The securities pledged for this loan are administered by an autonomous Monopoly Administration, established under the law of 8/20 July 1895. It independently manages the monopolies and revenues given as security for this loan, namely: the tobacco monopoly, the salt monopoly, the petroleum monopoly, and the other existing State monopolies, together with the revenues from stamp duties and beverage (excise) duties. Furthermore, the net receipts of the railways, the customs receipts and the yield of the trades (business) tax [Obrt/Octroi] are likewise to be delivered to the treasury of the Monopoly Administration. To that end the Directorate of the Serbian State Railways and all Serbian customs offices are irrevocably instructed to deliver their entire net receipts — the railways each month, the others every ten days — directly to the Monopoly Administration.

The Administrative Council of the Monopoly Administration is legally bound to apply the net receipts of the tobacco and petroleum monopolies, then (after redemption of the salt bonds) those of the salt monopoly, then the stamp and beverage duties, and finally all receipts of the railways, customs and trades tax, first and exclusively to the payment of the matured coupons and drawn bonds of this loan, as well as to the payment of the coupons of the 1881 lottery loan and the prizes drawn on the lottery loans of 1881 and 1888. It must forward those receipts to the paying agents (banks) appointed for the service of these loans in such a way that the agents hold, at the latest 15 days before each due date, the full sum required to pay the coupons and drawn bonds. Consequently these receipts and revenues may not be applied to any purpose other than those stated above.

Should the receipts of the Monopoly Administration not suffice for the annual service of this loan, the Royal Serbian Government is bound to make up any half-yearly shortfall from other resources.

**The French text of the bonds is authoritative (governs).**

Coupons not presented for payment are barred after five years, and drawn bonds after thirty years, from their due date.

Belgrade, 1/13 August 1895.

### 3. Signatures

The Minister of Finance: [signature]. — For control / Pour contrôle: [signature]. — The Delegate of the Royal Serbian Government / Plenipotentiary of the Royal Serbian Government: [signature].

### 4. Coupon talons

**TALON. Kingdom of Serbia. 4% amortizable loan.** Upon payment of the last coupon printed here, a new coupon sheet is issued to the bearer of this talon.

Each coupon: **Kingdom of Serbia. 4% amortizable loan. Coupon payable in gold on 1/13 [January/July] 19—**, in Belgrade and Paris with 10 Dinars/Francs, in Berlin with 8.10 Marks, in Vienna with 4 Austrian florins in gold. (Semi-annual series; clearly legible dates include January and July 1943 and 1944.)

title: Bulgarian Government Gold Loan Bond
description: This is a 500-franc bearer obligation of the Bulgarian 5% State Gold Loan of 1902 (Български 5% държавенъ заемъ въ злато отъ 1902 година / Emprunt de l'État Bulgare 5% Or 1902), one of 212,000 bonds in a loan of 106,000,000 gold levs—stated at par against francs, roubles, German marks, sterling, Austrian crowns and Dutch florins, the principality's metallic lev being pegged to the franc. The bond declares itself a direct obligation of the State, secured on the tobacco duty. It rests on a law sanctioned by decree of 3/16 June 1902 and a contract of 7/20 June 1902 with the Banque d'État de Russie in Paris and the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas. Issued at Sofia and printed in French, Bulgarian, German and English, the bond shows young Bulgaria, barely a generation from Ottoman suzerainty, borrowing on the Paris market through Franco-Russian houses, its credit anchored to the predictable revenue of a state tobacco monopoly. The crowned lion arms and disciplined neoclassical guilloche frame project the sober solvency such a placement required.
creator: Principauté de Bulgarie (Bulgarian Government)
notes: Bulgarian Government 5% Gold Loan of 1902 (Emprunt de l'État Bulgare 5% Or 1902), secured on tobacco duties; 500 Francs denomination; quadrilingual bond (Bulgarian, French, German, English); Principauté de Bulgarie; No. 008,761

title: Bulgarian Government Gold Loan Bond (Conditions)
description: This interior leaf sets out the conditions and amortization schedule of the Principality of Bulgaria's five-per-cent loan, printed across parallel columns in Bulgarian (Условия на Заема), French, Russian, German and English. Each obligation bears interest at 5% on its nominal value, the half-yearly coupon payable as 12.50 gold leva, the equivalent of 12.50 gold francs, and quoted also in roubles, reichsmark, sterling, Austrian crowns and florins—a gold-standard instrument floated simultaneously on the Paris, St Petersburg, Berlin, London, Vienna, Amsterdam and Brussels markets through the Banque de Paris et des Pays-Bas. Redemption proceeds at par over fifty years by semi-annual drawings held at Sofia, the printed table opening in 1903. Issued as Bulgaria, newly autonomous, borrowed abroad to fund its young state, the document's sober, rule-bound typography—dense conditions framed by a restrained engraved border—mirrors the contractual sobriety expected by Western European creditors.

title: Bulgarian 5% State Gold Loan of 1902 — Bond Talon
description: This is the talon — the coupon-renewal stub — of a bond of the Bulgarian 5% State Gold Loan of 1902, obligation No. 008,761. Printed in Bulgarian and French within a chain-link border of pale red guilloche, the cartouche names the issuer, "ЦАРСТВО БЪЛГАРИЯ / ROYAUME DE BULGARIE," and states that the loan bears five per cent in gold and is secured by a pledge on the tobacco duties ("gagé sur les droits des tabacs"). Surrendering the talon to the Ministry of Finance entitled the bearer to a fresh sheet of coupons — the device by which a long-dated bond kept paying interest once its original coupons were spent. The text sets redemption over fifty years by semi-annual drawings, the first on 1/14 August 1903. Earmarking a particular consumption tax to service a foreign-placed gold loan reflects how a small Balkan state borrowed on Western European markets, where pledged revenues reassured creditors. The sober frame, free of allegory, presents the bond as a plain undertaking.
creator: Principauté de Bulgarie (Bulgarian Government)

title: Bulgarian 5% Gold Loan Talon, 1902
description: This is the talon, or coupon-renewal slip, belonging to a bond of the Bulgarian Kingdom's 5% Gold Loan of 1902, secured on the revenues of the tobacco duties. Printed in parallel columns of Russian, German, and English (Болгарское Царство / Königreich Bulgarien / Bulgarian Kingdom), the talon promises that upon its surrender the Ministry of Finance shall in due time deliver to the bondholder a fresh sheet of interest coupons. The device speaks to the long maturity of such gold loans: once the original coupon sheet was exhausted, the talon entitled the holder to its successor. The trilingual setting marks the issue as one floated abroad for international subscription, the young principality pledging a state monopoly's income to reassure foreign creditors. The frame is a restrained typographic border, free of allegory, the sober dress of public credit rather than ornament.

title: Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan Bond, Berlin, 1898
description: This is a £25 Sterling bond from the Chinese Imperial Government's 4½% Gold Loan of 1898 (Kaiserlich Chinesische 4½% Staatsanleihe in Gold), part of a total issue of £16,000,000 nominal and printed in parallel English and German. It was placed in Berlin through the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank, acting as agent for the emission, and is dated Berlin, 1 March 1898, authorised by Imperial Edict communicated through the Tsungli Yamên to the British and German Legations at Peking. The loan was raised to settle the indemnity owed to Japan after the Sino-Japanese War, and its security tells the story: the bond is charged upon the revenues of the Imperial Maritime Customs and the likin (lekin) of certain provinces, foreign-administered receipts that reassured European creditors while ceding fiscal control. The red ornamental frame, ruyi-scroll borders and porcelain-vase medallions invoke a Chinese visual idiom for instruments underwritten and governed from Berlin and London.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government; Deutsch-Asiatische Bank

title: Chinese Imperial Government Gold Loan Bond (Conditions)
description: The interior leaf of a £25 sterling bond from the Chinese Imperial Government 4½% Gold Loan of 1898 ("Kaiserlich Chinesische 4½% Staatsanleihe in Gold von 1898"), Littera A, printed in parallel English and German. A central panel restates the instrument; flanking columns reproduce extracts of the loan agreement, and a full "Table of Drawings" sets out the amortization plan across the loan's long life. Along the foot runs the surviving coupon sheet, each half-yearly coupon promising Eleven Shillings and Threepence — the exact semi-annual yield on £25 at 4½% — with maturities reaching into the 1940s. The extracts pledge the Maritime Customs and Likin revenues and name the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation and the Deutsch-Asiatische Bank as the loan's joint security and paying agents. Issued in the aftermath of the Sino-Japanese War indemnity, the bond shows a hard-pressed empire mortgaging its customs receipts to foreign banking houses, and the bilingual text marks the rising Anglo-German contest for Chinese finance.
creator: Chinese Imperial Government; Deutsch-Asiatische Bank
notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0970 (Chinese Imperial Government 4.5% Gold Loan £25 bond); bond extracts and conditions in English and German; Table of Drawings / Amortizations-Plan on right; remaining coupons 82-90 attached at bottom; No. 024,487

title: Exposition Coloniale Internationale Paris Lottery Bond
description: A bearer lottery bond (bon à lot au porteur) of sixty francs, issued for the Exposition Coloniale Internationale held at Paris in 1931 and placed through the Crédit Foncier de France under authority of the law of 22 July 1927. The emission ran to 2,300,000 bonds, divided into 100 series of 23,000 numbers each. Tax-free to its holder, the bond entitled the bearer to twenty entry tickets to the Exposition worth three francs apiece, to assorted travel privileges, and to participation in twelve drawings of 2,136 prizes totalling 24,264,000 francs. The bond reveals the French state's habit of financing prestige ventures by harnessing the public appetite for a flutter rather than by direct taxation: the colonial fair is sold to small savers as both spectacle and speculation. The design speaks to this purpose, framing the figures in palms, a distant minaret, and railway, maritime, and aviation coupons that promise passage to the empire on display.
creator: Exposition Coloniale Internationale Paris 1931; Crédit Foncier de France
notes: Exposition Coloniale Internationale de Paris 1931, Bon à Lot de Soixante Francs au porteur (60 Francs lottery bond); 2,300,000 bonds authorized by law of July 22, 1931; travel coupon stub attached (Chemins de Fer, Transports Maritimes, Avions); Série 018, No. 08682

title: Exposition Coloniale Internationale Paris Lottery Bond (Reverse)
description: The reverse of a French lottery bond (bon à lots) issued to finance the Exposition Coloniale Internationale held in Paris, authorised under the law of 22 July 1927 and managed by the Crédit Foncier de France, denominated in francs. A ruled Tableau des Tirages et des Lots sets out the redemption lottery: twelve drawings spread across 1929, 1930 and 1931, each distributing 178 prizes worth 2,022,000 francs, for an aggregate of 2,136 lots totalling 24,264,000 francs, with a single top prize of 1,000,000 francs per draw descending to lots of 250 francs. A subjoined extract of the convention enumerates the bond's non-monetary privileges: twenty admission tickets to the exposition and graduated reductions on railway, maritime and aviation fares, followed by a list of the shipping companies honouring them. The structure shows how a state-sponsored colonial showcase was capitalised through the public's appetite for chance, the prospect of a fortune underwriting an imperial spectacle. The plain ruled tabular layout is utilitarian rather than ornamental, prizing legibility of terms over decoration.
creator: Exposition Coloniale Internationale Paris 1931; Crédit Foncier de France

title: Grand Russian Railway Company 3% Bond, St. Petersburg, 1881
description: This is a 3% bearer obligation of 125 silver-metallic rubles issued by the Grand Russian Railway Company (Главное Общество Россійскихъ Желѣзныхъ Дорогъ), the joint-stock concern chartered under an imperially confirmed statute of 3 November 1861 to build the empire's first trunk lines. The bond belongs to the Third Emission (Третій выпускъ облигацій) of 105,176 obligations, together amounting to 13,147,000 silver rubles, and offers convertible equivalents of 500 francs, 20 pounds sterling, 402 German marks, or 236 Dutch guilders — a denominational fan that announces the issue's purpose: to draw western European capital into Russian railway finance. Signed at St. Petersburg and dated 4 January 1881, it reflects the still-unfinished work of integrating the empire by rail two decades after emancipation. The teal frame crowds an imperial double-headed eagle among allegorical figures and a viaduct, binding sovereign authority to the promise of industry and transit.
creator: Grand Russian Railway Company (Главное Общество Российских Железных Дорог)

# Grand Russian Railway Company — 3% Bond of 125 Silver Rubles, Third Emission, St. Petersburg 1881 — Transcription

## Original:

(Curved banner across the top border, repeated:)
**ГЛАВНОЕ ОБЩЕСТВО РОССІЙСКИХЪ ЖЕЛѢЗНЫХЪ ДОРОГЪ**

(Left denomination cartouche:) **125 р.**  (Right corner:) **3%**

**ГЛАВНОЕ ОБЩЕСТВО РОССІЙСКИХЪ ЖЕЛѢЗНЫХЪ ДОРОГЪ,**
утвержденное ВЫСОЧАЙШИМЪ Указомъ 26-го Января 1857 года.

МѢСТОПРЕБЫВАНІЕ ОБЩЕСТВА ВЪ С.-ПЕТЕРБУРГѢ.

Основной капиталъ Общества по Уставу ВЫСОЧАЙШЕ утвержденному 3-го Ноября 1861 г.:
въ акціяхъ : 600,000 акцій по 125 рублей сер. на 75,000,000 руб. сер. (металлич.).
въ облигаціяхъ: 1-го вып. 70,000 облиг. 4 % по 500 р. на 35,000,000 руб. с. (метал.).
2-го вып. 18,377 облиг. 4 % по 125 руб. на 2,359,625 руб. с. (мет.).

**ТРЕТІЙ ВЫПУСКЪ ОБЛИГАЦІЙ:**
105,176 облигацій по 125 руб. сер. на 13.147,000 руб. сер. (металлическихъ).

Выкупъ, по нарицательной цѣнѣ, производится посредствомъ ежегоднаго тиража по жеребью, въ теченіе 70 лѣтъ, ¹/₁₃ Іюня каждаго года, начиная съ ¹/₁₃ Іюня 1881 года. Выдача суммъ, слѣдующихъ къ погашенію по облигаціямъ, вышедшимъ въ тиражъ, производится съ ¹/₁₃ Декабря каждаго года, начиная съ ¹/₁₃ Декабря 1881 года.

Годовой доходъ составляетъ: 3 р. 75 к. = 15 фр. = 12 шилл. = 12 мар. 6 пф. = 7 гульд. 6 центс. и уплачивается по полугодно, ¹/₁₃ Іюня и ¹/₁₃ Декабря.

**ОБЛИГАЦІЯ ВЪ СТО ДВАДЦАТЬ ПЯТЬ РУБЛЕЙ СЕР. МЕТАЛЛИЧЕСКИХЪ,**
или 500 франк. = 20 фунт. стерл. = 402 герм. мар. = 236 голл. гульд.

**№ 210979**

(Ministry of Finance control stamp, circular:) ГЕРБОВЫЙ СБОРЪ УПЛАЧЕНЪ / М.Ф. / I. / С.П.

(Fine-print body paragraph:)
Капиталъ Главнаго Общества, состоящій, по § 6 Высочайше утвержденнаго 3 Ноября 1861 года Устава Общества, въ акціяхъ и облигаціяхъ, увеличивается, на основаніи послѣдняго пункта этого параграфа и согласно Высочайше утвержденному 19 Декабря 1880 года Положенію Комитета Гг. Министровъ, третьимъ выпускомъ облигацій. Правительство даруетъ этимъ облигаціямъ, выпускаемымъ на покрытіе расходовъ по постройкѣ вторыхъ путей и увеличенію подвижнаго состава и матеріальнаго оборотнаго капитала Общества, абсолютную гарантію трехъ процентовъ чистаго въ годъ дохода и погашенія al pari въ теченіи семидесяти лѣтъ. На уплату процентовъ и выкупъ облигацій Общества обращаются, преимущественно передъ акціями, всѣ чистые доходы С.-Петербурго-Варшавской и Московско-Нижегородской желѣзныхъ дорогъ, уступленныхъ Обществу, равно какъ поручительство, данное Государствомъ. Капиталы и доходы желѣзныхъ дорогъ, Обществу уступленныхъ, освобождены отъ всякаго налога (§ 22 Устава). Доходъ и погашеніе уплачиваются: въ С.-Петербургѣ въ мѣстѣ пребыванія Общества, въ Парижѣ въ Русско-Французскомъ Банкѣ, въ Берлинѣ у Гг. Мендельсонъ и К°, въ Лондонѣ въ Отдѣленіи Русскаго для внѣшней торговли Банка, въ Амстердамѣ у Гг. Липпманъ, Розенталь и К° и въ Брюсселѣ у Г. Брюгманнъ сына. Проценты, оставшіеся не взятыми въ теченіи пяти лѣтъ отъ назначеннаго для выдачи ихъ срока, обращаются въ пользу Общества. Всѣ облигаціи сего выпуска на предъявителя.

(Signature block, left:)
С.-Петербургъ. 23 Декабря 1880 г. / 4 Января 1881 г.
Уполномоченный отъ Правительства
[handwritten signature — illegible]

(Signature block, right:)
За Главное Общество Россійскихъ Желѣзныхъ Дорогъ:
Предсѣдатель Совѣта [handwritten signature — illegible]
По порученію Совѣта [handwritten signature — illegible]

(Line beneath left signature:)
При сей облигаціи выданъ первый талонъ съ сорока купонами на первые двадцать лѣтъ.

## English Translation:

(Top banner:) **GRAND RUSSIAN RAILWAY COMPANY**

(Left cartouche:) **125 r.**  (Right corner:) **3%**

**GRAND RUSSIAN RAILWAY COMPANY,**
established by IMPERIAL Decree of 26 January 1857.

SEAT OF THE COMPANY IN ST. PETERSBURG.

Founding capital of the Company under the Statute IMPERIALLY confirmed on 3 November 1861:
in shares: 600,000 shares at 125 silver rubles, totaling 75,000,000 silver rubles (metallic).
in bonds: 1st emission, 70,000 bonds at 4%, at 500 r., totaling 35,000,000 silver rubles (metallic).
2nd emission, 18,377 bonds at 4%, at 125 r., totaling 2,359,625 silver rubles (metallic).

**THIRD EMISSION OF BONDS:**
105,176 bonds at 125 silver rubles, totaling 13,147,000 silver rubles (metallic).

Redemption, at par value, is carried out by means of an annual drawing by lot, over the course of 70 years, on the 1/13 June of each year, beginning 1/13 June 1881. Payment of sums due upon redemption of bonds that have been drawn is made from 1/13 December of each year, beginning 1/13 December 1881.

The annual income amounts to: 3 r. 75 k. = 15 francs = 12 shillings = 12 marks 6 pfennigs = 7 guilders 6 cents, and is paid semi-annually, on 1/13 June and 1/13 December.

**BOND OF ONE HUNDRED TWENTY-FIVE SILVER METALLIC RUBLES,**
or 500 francs = 20 pounds sterling = 402 German marks = 236 Dutch guilders.

**No. 210979**

(Ministry of Finance control stamp, circular:) STAMP DUTY PAID / M.F. / I. / St. P[etersburg].

(Fine-print body:)
The capital of the Grand Company, consisting — under § 6 of the Company's Statute imperially confirmed on 3 November 1861 — of shares and bonds, is increased, on the basis of the final clause of that paragraph and in accordance with the imperially confirmed Resolution of the Committee of Ministers of 19 December 1880, by a third emission of bonds. The Government grants to these bonds, issued to cover the expenses of building second tracks and of increasing the rolling stock and material working capital of the Company, an absolute guarantee of three percent net annual income and of redemption at par over the course of seventy years. Toward the payment of interest and the redemption of the Company's bonds are applied, in preference to the shares, all the net revenues of the St. Petersburg–Warsaw and Moscow–Nizhny Novgorod railways ceded to the Company, as well as the guarantee given by the State. The capitals and revenues of the railways ceded to the Company are exempt from every tax (§ 22 of the Statute). Income and redemption are paid: in St. Petersburg at the seat of the Company; in Paris at the Russo-French Bank; in Berlin at Messrs. Mendelssohn & Co.; in London at the Branch of the Russian Bank for Foreign Trade; in Amsterdam at Messrs. Lippmann, Rosenthal & Co.; and in Brussels at Mr. Brugmann fils. Interest not claimed within five years from the date appointed for its payment reverts to the benefit of the Company. All bonds of this emission are to bearer.

(Signature block, left:)
St. Petersburg. 23 December 1880 / 4 January 1881.
Authorized Representative of the Government
[handwritten signature — illegible]

(Signature block, right:)
For the Grand Russian Railway Company:
Chairman of the Council [handwritten signature — illegible]
By order of the Council [handwritten signature — illegible]

(Line beneath left signature:)
With this bond a first talon is issued, bearing forty coupons for the first twenty years.

title: Grand Russian Railway Company Bond, St. Petersburg, 1880
description: The conditions side of a bond of the Grand Russian Railway Company (Grande Société des Chemins de fer Russes / Grosse Russische Eisenbahn-Gesellschaft), one obligation of its Third Emission with a face value of 125 silver rubles in metal, rendered equivalent to 500 francs, 20 pounds sterling, 402 Reichsmark and 236 Dutch guilders. It carries annual interest of 3 rubles 75 kopecks, a 3 percent coupon payable half-yearly, and is to be amortised at par over seventy years by annual drawings from 1881. Dated St. Petersburg, 23 December 1880 / 4 January 1881, the issue rests on statutes confirmed in 1861 and a sovereign decree of 19 December 1880 authorising 13,147,000 rubles in 105,176 obligations. Printed in French, German and English with a paired conversion table, the document shows how the imperial railway financed track-doubling and rolling stock by courting bourses in Paris, Berlin, London, Amsterdam and Brussels at once.

title: Grand Russian Railway Company 3% Bond Coupon Sheet, St. Petersburg, 1893
description: This is the coupon sheet attached to a 3% bond of the Grand Russian Railway Company (Главное Общество Российскихъ Желѣзныхъ Дорогъ), third emission of 1893. The heading and the terminal talon fix the bond's face value at 187 roubles 50 kopecks, expressed in parallel as 500 francs, £20 sterling, 236 Dutch guilders and 402 German marks (1 rouble = ¼ imperial). The page is a printed grid of numbered semi-annual interest coupons, one of which announces the "100th coupon," indicating a long redemption horizon. The multilingual valuation is the telling detail: a Russian railway financed through the Paris, London, Amsterdam and Berlin markets, its debt deliberately denominated so foreign rentiers could clip coupons in their own money. The restrained green lithography, ruled into a ledger-like matrix, subordinates ornament to fiduciary clarity, the document presenting itself as an instrument of account rather than display.
creator: Grand Russian Railway Company (Главное Общество Российских Железных Дорог)

# Grand Russian Railway Company 3% Bond — Coupon Sheet (Third Issue, 1881) — Transcription

## Original:

*(Green lithographed coupon sheet. Vertical year marks in the upper corners: left "1915", right "1930". Bond serial number printed throughout: №210979.)*

**TALON (heading strip, top of sheet):**

**3% облигація ГЛАВНАГО ОБЩЕСТВА РОССІЙСКИХЪ ЖЕЛѢЗНЫХЪ ДОРОГЪ**
**третьяго выпуска 1881 года.**

**ТАЛОНЪ** къ облигаціи въ 187 руб. 50 коп. (1 руб.=¹⁄₁₆ импер.)=500 франк.=20 фунт. стерл.=236 голл. гульд.=402 имп. герм. марк.

№210979

*(Fine-print paragraph below the heading:)*

По предъявленіи сего талона, отъ 1/14 Декабря 1930 г. по 1/14 Іюня 1932 г., будетъ выданъ въ Россіи—въ Государственной Коммиссіи Погашенія Долговъ, а за границей—въ мѣстахъ, назначенныхъ для оплаты купоновъ, новый купонный листъ, если, однако, до дня выдачи его не будетъ предъявлена другимъ лицомъ для той же цѣли самая облигація. Въ этомъ случаѣ новый купонный листъ выдается предъявителю облигаціи. Послѣ 1/14 Іюня 1932 г. талонъ сей не дѣйствителенъ, и новый купонный листъ будетъ выданъ лишь по предъявленіи самой облигаціи. Къ облигаціямъ, вышедшимъ въ тиражъ 1/14 Іюня 1930 г. и предыдущіе тиражи, новые купонные листы не будутъ выдаваемы.

**TALON** to the bond of 187 roubles 50 kopecks (1 rouble = ¹⁄₁₆ imperial) = 500 francs = 20 pounds sterling = 236 Dutch guilders = 402 imperial German marks.

№210979

Upon presentation of this talon, between 1/14 December 1930 and 1/14 June 1932, a new coupon sheet will be issued — in Russia at the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts, and abroad at the places appointed for the payment of coupons — provided, however, that before the day of its issue the bond itself has not been presented by another person for the same purpose. In that case the new coupon sheet is issued to the holder of the bond. After 1/14 June 1932 this talon is void, and a new coupon sheet will be issued only upon presentation of the bond itself. For bonds drawn for redemption on 1/14 June 1930 and in previous drawings, no new coupon sheets will be issued.

title: Grand Russian Railway Company 3% Bond Coupon Sheet, Third Issue of 1881 (French/English)
description: This is the coupon sheet, with attached renewal talon, for a 3% bond of the Grande Société des Chemins de Fer Russes (Grand Russian Railway Company), Third Issue of 1881. Printed in green in parallel French and English columns, it carries the bond's value across the era's principal financial centres: 187 roubles 50 copecks (one rouble reckoned at one-fifteenth of an imperial), equal to 500 francs, 20 pounds sterling, 236 Dutch florins, and 402 reichsmark. The grid holds semi-annual interest coupons, here running into the late 1920s, while the talon entitles the holder to a fresh sheet from the Imperial Commission of the Sinking-Fund. The multi-currency face reflects how Russia financed its railways through the bourses of Paris, London, Amsterdam, and Berlin, the long-dated coupons binding foreign capital to the empire's rail expansion. The sheet is utilitarian, its only ornament the ruled coupon table.

title: Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock, London, 1904
description: A government redeemable stock certificate for £1,000 sterling bearing interest at 3½ per cent per annum, issued by the Province of Nova Scotia within the Dominion of Canada and dated 19 October 1904. The stock is transferable and payable through the National Provincial Bank of England, Limited, at 112 Bishopsgate Street in the City of London, and is redeemable at par there on 1 July 1954, the government reserving the option to redeem at par at any time on twelve months' notice advertised in the London Gazette and The Times. A clause invoking 40 and 41 Vict., Ch. 59, Sec. 19 confines liability to the province and its consolidated fund, expressly excluding His Majesty's Treasury. Raised in London rather than at home, the issue shows a colonial government tapping the deep imperial capital market on sterling terms. The engraved frame, provincial arms, and emphatic blackletter heading project metropolitan creditworthiness for a distant borrower.

notes: Dominion of Canada, Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock; £1,000 denomination; 3.5% per annum; redeemable at par in London; National Provincial Bank of England Ltd., 112 Bishopsgate Street; dated October 17, 1930; with attached coupon sheet; No. C 1515

title: Province of Nova Scotia 3½% Redeemable Stock, Coupon Sheet, payable in London
description: A sheet of detachable interest coupons for the Province of Nova Scotia Government 3½% Redeemable Stock, part of the Dominion of Canada's sterling-denominated borrowing on the London market. Each coupon represents one half-year's dividend of seventeen pounds ten shillings (£17.10.0), less income tax, on a £1,000 certificate — the semi-annual half of the 3½% rate — payable at the National Provincial Bank of England, 112 Bishopsgate Street, London. The coupons fall due successively on 1 January and 1 July; those surviving on this sheet (the second of four) run through the early-to-mid 1920s. Signed by the Provincial Secretary, G. H. Murray, the coupons bear a small heraldic device and a statutory caveat that the revenues of the Province alone are liable for the stock (40 & 41 Vict., ch. 59, sec. 19). The restrained engraved borders and sober typography befit a colonial government raising long-term capital in the imperial money market.

title: Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock (Conditions)
description: This sheet forms the conditions-and-coupon portion of a Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock certificate, a sterling loan of one thousand pounds bearing interest at three and one-half per cent per annum. The detachable coupons each promise a half-yearly dividend of seventeen pounds ten shillings, less income tax, due 1 January and 1 July, payable in London at the Bank of England, Limited, and countersigned for the National Provincial Bank as registrar. The reverse conditions state pointedly that the revenues of the Province of Nova Scotia alone answer for the stock and its dividends, the Consolidated Fund of the United Kingdom and His Majesty's Treasury being expressly not liable, citing the statute 40 & 41 Vict. Floated on the London market, it shows a colonial government borrowing in imperial sterling on its own credit. The dense engine-turned border in red ochre, framing a thistle vignette, signals security against forgery rather than any artistic programme.

title: Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock Stubs
description: These are the binding-spine stubs (counterfoils) of registered certificates for the Province of Nova Scotia Government Redeemable Stock, an inscribed colonial loan bearing interest at 3½ per cent per annum on a principal of £1,000 sterling. The two engraved stub labels are headed "Dominion of Canada — Province of Nova Scotia — Government Redeemable Stock," with principal repayable and coupons payable half-yearly, the first instalment falling due 1st January. Issued in London around 1910 under Imperial colonial-stock arrangements, the loan let a Canadian province borrow on the sterling market at rates far below colonial terms. The recurring legend on the lower coupon panels disclaims any Imperial guarantee: the revenues of Nova Scotia alone answer for the stock, while the Consolidated Fund of the United Kingdom and the Lords Commissioners of His Majesty's Treasury are "not directly or indirectly liable." The restrained foliate engraving in terracotta ink serves trust rather than display.

title: Banque Industrielle de Chine Share
description: An ordinary bearer share (action ordinaire au porteur), fully paid, for a face value of 500 French francs, issued by the Banque Industrielle de Chine (中法實業銀行, the Sino-French Industrial Bank), a société anonyme with a capital of 150,000,000 francs and its registered office in Paris. The text records statutes deposited before notaries Lavoignat and Moyne on 15 March 1913 and amended by shareholders' general assemblies of 19 May 1919, 28 November 1919, and 7 May 1920; the share therefore dates from about 1920, in the bank's last and most strained phase before its suspension of payments in 1921. The certificate speaks to the ambitions of Franco-Chinese finance on the eve of collapse: founded to channel European capital into Chinese railways, ports, and industry, the bank had over-extended, and shares such as this funded a venture soon ruined. The engraved frame turns that ambition into ornament — a Chinese gateway, paired guardian lions, and a pagoda-studded skyline in ochre and grey present China as a picturesque field for investment, a decorative orientalism characteristic of European concession finance.

title: Banque Industrielle de Chine Share (Reverse)
description: The reverse of an ordinary share in the Banque Industrielle de Chine (中法實業銀行), a Paris-domiciled joint-stock bank denominated in francs. The upper panel reproduces the "Extraits des Statuts": Article 3 defines the object as all banking and finance operations "en tous pays," and especially the carrying-out of agreements concluded with the Government of the Chinese Republic; Article 4 fixes the head office at Paris; Article 5 sets a duration of ninety-nine years; Article 6 places the share capital at 150 million francs. Below, a sheet of detachable dividend-coupon stubs is printed only with the bank's name and the words 息票 ("dividend coupon"). Established in 1913, the bank was a vehicle of Franco-Chinese financial diplomacy, channelling French capital toward concessions and public works in republican China; the bilingual statutes and Chinese coupons register that hybrid ambition before the bank's collapse in 1921.

notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0988 (Banque Industrielle de Chine share); Extraits des Statuts (corporate statutes) in French; Chinese dividend coupon sheet at bottom (Zhongfa Shiye Yinhang interest coupons)

# EXTRAITS DES STATUTS

## Original French Text:

**Art. 3.** — La Société a pour objet :
L'étude et l'exécution de travaux de Travaux généralement quelconques, en tous pays, spécialement dans l'Empire Chinois. Elle pourra notamment entreprendre l'établissement et l'exploitation de chemins de fer, de routes, de canaux, de ports, d'établissements agricoles, industriels ou commerciaux, l'extraction et le traitement des minerais, la création de scieries, de filatures, de tanneries, d'usines, l'établissement de banques, de maisons de commerce ou de change, l'exploitation de mines, de forêts ou de concessions quelconques en tous pays; en général, tous les travaux et opérations commerciales, industrielles ou financières que pourrait la compter la mise en valeur et l'exploitation des richesses naturelles de ces pays.

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond
description: This is a 3½ % bearer bond (obligation au porteur) for £20 sterling, equal to 504 French francs, issued by the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, Société Anonyme, with its registered office in Cairo. The bond belongs to an emission of 12,500 obligations of £20 each, representing a total of £250,000 or 6,300,000 francs, raised against the company's authorised capital of £200,000 and sanctioned by Khedival decree of 8 June 1897. Interest of £0.14.0 (17.64 francs) is payable yearly in gold, half on 1 January and half on 1 July, redeemable by annual drawings within a maximum of seventy years, with coupons honoured in gold at Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels and Geneva. Dated Cairo, 1 July 1897, and signed by two directors. The bilingual French and English text, gold-clause interest and seven European and Egyptian paying centres locate the issue within the foreign-financed railway building that knit Khedival Egypt into the capital markets of London and Paris. The dense blue guilloche border and ornate engraved lettering follow the protective convention of the period's securities, intended as much to deter forgery as to dignify the obligation.

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond (Conditions)
description: The reverse of a 3½% bond issued in pounds sterling by the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, presenting under twin headings "Garantie" and "Guarantees," in French and English, the security underpinning the loan. The conditions describe the concession: the construction and working of narrow-gauge agricultural railways, electric or steam, in the Egyptian provinces of Charkieh, Dakahlieh, and Kalioubieh, some 350 kilometres in extent. Under article 5 of the concession act of 21 May 1896, the Egyptian Government guarantees a net annual receipt of £36 sterling per kilometre, making good any shortfall. Below, a sinking-fund table ("Tableau d'Amortissement") schedules redemption from 1 January 1900 through 1960. The text speaks to the financing of light railways serving the Delta's cotton lands, where a sovereign guarantee was the instrument that drew European capital into Egyptian infrastructure.

## GARANTIE / GUARANTEES

**Original (French):**

La concession a pour objet la construction et l'exploitation des chemins de fer agricoles, a voie étroite a traction électrique ou a vapeur, dans les provinces de Charkich, Dakahlieh et Kaloubieh.

Les lignes concédées représentent actuellement 350 kilomètres environ.

Aux termes de l'article 5 de l'acte de concession du 31 mai 1896, le Gouvernement Égyptien garantit pour l'ensemble de toutes les lignes du Réseau concédé, une recette annuelle calculée a raison de livres sterling/tranches (£ 36) par kilomètre, et s'il s'engage a parfaire, jusqu'a concurrence de cette somme, toute insuffisance de ladite recette réelle réalisée dans une année pour atteindre le chiffre ci-dessus de £ 36 par kilomètre.

Le montant des recettes brutes, comprenant les recettes quelconques en bonne matière dues chemins de fer agricoles et leurs dépendances, après avoir été diminué du 90 /1, pour frais et dépenses d'entretien, dépublication et d'administration, a compris l'amortissement annuel en 90 ans du capital leur et miroisons paragraphe de l'article 2, constitue la recette nette des chemins de fer agricoles et de leurs dépendances pour l'application de la garantie mentionnée ci-dessus.

Le dit paragraphe prévoit en outre, pour chaque année de l'exercice de la concession, la mise en exploitation générale des lignes concédées et spécifiées a l'acte de concession (350 kilomètres), et a partir de la 1re mise en exploitation générale pour les lignes qui auraient été concédées ultérieurement au termes du même acte.

Audit paragraphe il est reçu en générale des lignes pour leur mise en exploitation générale sera par section de 25 kilomètres et la garantie commencera a s'en acquitter a partir pour chaque des sections au fur et a mesure de la mise en exploitation générale.

L'audit garantie sera payable dans le mois qui suivra clôt de la clôture de l'exercice financier de l'exploitation.

---

**English translation:**

The object of the concession is to build and work agricultural narrow gauge railways with either electric or steam traction, in the provinces of Charkich, Dakahlieh and Kaloubieh.

The conceded lines have at present a length of about 350 kilometres.

In accordance with article 5 of the Act of concession dated May 31, 1896, the Egyptian Government guarantees for the whole system of conceded lines, annual net receipts at the rate of thirty-six pounds sterling (£ 36) per kilometre and undertakes to supply, up to that amount, any deficiency in the said annual net receipts effected in the course of 13 months.

The amount of gross receipts, including various receipts of all kinds from the agricultural railways and their dependencies, after deducting 60/, for repairs etc., working and management expenses, including the annual sum to provide for the redemption in 90 years of the capital as per § 3 of article 2, constitutes the net receipts of the agricultural railways and their dependencies for the application of the above mentioned guarantees.

The said guarantee shall only from the commencement of the general working of the conceded lines mentioned in article 1 of the Act of concession (350 kilometres) and from the date of the general working of the lines which may hereafter be conceded in conformity with this said article.

The examination of the lines previous to their general working shall be proceeded with, by sections of 25 kilometres, and the guarantee shall be available from the date of such examination for each of the sections respectively at their general working commences.

The said guarantee shall be payable in the course of the month following that closing the financial year of the working.

---

title: East Egyptian Railways Bond Coupon Sheet
description: This is a sheet of attached interest coupons from a bond (obligation no. 10592) of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, the company chartered to build and work light railway lines in the eastern Egyptian delta. The eleven coupons here, numbered 110 to 120, each entitle the bearer to eight francs and eighty-two centimes (8.82 fr.), payable on the first of January and the first of July in successive half-years; the dates run from 1 January 1953 (no. 111) through 1 July 1957 (no. 120). Each coupon may be presented at Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels, or Geneva, the breadth of paying agents marking the international syndicate that financed Egyptian railway construction in francs. The printed terms beside the coupons set out the concession's working by sections of twenty-five kilometres. The plain guilloche frames and engraved lettering announce a fiduciary instrument whose value lay in its promise, not its ornament.
creator: Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien

title: Coupon Sheet of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien, Egypt
description: This is the final sheet of interest coupons detached from a bond of the Compagnie des Chemins de Fer Économiques de l'Est Égyptien (Company of Economic Railways of Eastern Egypt). Printed in French and English, the sheet ranges twelve identical coupons in a grid, each redeemable for £0.7.0 — seven shillings sterling — and each "Payable" on the first of January or the first of July across the years 1952 to 1958, the company's semi-annual dividend cycle. Payment is offered at a cosmopolitan roster of agencies — Cairo, Alexandria, Paris, London, Amsterdam, Brussels, and Geneva — mapping the European capital that financed Egyptian railway building and the international market in which the loan circulated. The remnant of the parent certificate, visible at the right edge with its company seal, anchors these coupons to the underlying obligation. As the last of several sheets, the document records the long tail of a fixed-income security maturing in the mid-twentieth century.

title: Esterhazy Central Casse Partial Bond
description: This is a partial bond (Partial-Schuldverschreibung) for twenty gulden in Conventions-Münze on the 20-fl. standard, issued by the central treasury of Count Casimir Esterházy von Galántha, an imperial chamberlain and proprietor of lordships in Hungary and Carinthia. The certificate represents one of 50,000 equal partial obligations into which a one-million-gulden loan was divided, bearing five percent interest and redeemed by lottery drawings over forty years beginning in 1848, with the underlying principal deed dated Vienna, 6 November 1847; a treasury stamp records 15 December 1847. The loan was placed through the Vienna wholesale house Hammer & Karis and secured on the count's Carinthian estates, among them the lordship of Tarvis with its forest districts. A magnate borrowing against landed property here taps the public credit market through a banking house rather than private lenders, the engraved comital arms with griffin supporters and coronet lending aristocratic assurance to a paper obligation.
creator: Gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'sche Central-Casse
notes: Partial-Schuldverschreibung (partial bond certificate), 20 Gulden Conventions-Münze; loan of 1,000,000 guilden issued against estate of Count Casimir Esterhazy von Galantha; managed by banking house Hammer & Koch; with lottery repayment recapitulation table; No. 14,725

# Transcription

## Original (German):

**[Top of page - Repayment table header]**

Rekapitulation der Rückzahlungen

**[Main document text]**

№14725

**Partial-Schuldverschreibung**

über

*Zwanzig Gulden Conventions-Münze im 20 fl. Fusse,*

welche als Antheil an der von mir **Grafen Casimir Esterhazy von Galantha**, k. k. Kämmerer, Herschafts- und Hauptmann-Inhaber in Frágnó et Semptén, bei dem bleibenden k. k. priv. Grosshandelshause **Hammer & Karoly** contralierten Anleihe von **Einer Million gulden Conv-Münze im 20 fl. Fusse** auf 50,000 gleiche Partial-Schuldverschreibungen ausgegeben hierdurah anerkannt werde, daß ich die hier angegebene Summe nebst dem gesetzlichen Rückzahlungsplane augezebten Zeit, mit fünf [percent] jährlichem Zinseszinse während der ganzen Dauer dieser Partial-Schuldverschreibung verzinset werden soll, und daß diese Partial-Schuldverschreibung nach erfolgter Verlosung derselben auf der auch in dem Prospekte angegebenen Zeit, sammt dem darauf entfallenden Betrag mit dem aufgelaufenen Zinsen, bey dem Grundhandlungshause **Hammer & Karoly** in Wien, unanfechtlich jeden Presentanten bezahlt werden wird. Es steht allgenen diese Partial-Schuldverschreibung weder Pfand noch sonstige Sicherheit auf dem Grundhandelshause **Hammer & Karoly** oder ihren Hechsnachfolgern baar angerecht werden. Es steht allgenen diese Partial-Schuldverschreibung weder Pfand noch sonstige Sicherheit auf dem Grundhandlungshause zu; daher jeder Inhaber dieser Partial-Schuldverschreibung sie zu dem in dieser Verlosung angegebenen Kurse, wann sich dieselbe **Wochsen früherer Verfallstag** nicht minder anlegen kann, jederzeit versetzen darf, wenn sich derselbe für Verfallstag nicht minder anlegt.

Zur Vollzugs Beglaubigung unterzeichne ich die, diesem Anleihen als Basis dienende Hauptschuldkunde d. Wien den 6. November 1847 **Einer Million gulden Conv.-Münz.** [k. k.] Herz. Esterhazy Esterhazy **[unclear]**, mathematische und buchförderung Ausweise als: **[unclear]** in Herzogthume Kärnten, Villacher **Weiß-schrift, Pederschein** und **[unclear]** Tarig all: des Portfetorillsten Goggau, Graffl.

## English Translation:

**[Top of page - Repayment table header]**

Recapitulation of Repayments

**[Main document text]**

№14725

**Partial Bond Certificate**

for

*Twenty Guilders Conventional Coinage at 20 fl. Standard,*

which, as a share of the loan of **One Million Guilders Conventional Coinage at 20 fl. Standard** contracted by me, **Count Casimir Esterházy of Galantha**, Imperial Royal Chamberlain, Lord and Captain-Proprietor of Frágnó and Semptén, with the established Imperial Royal privileged wholesale house **Hammer & Karoly**, is hereby acknowledged to be issued as one of 50,000 equal partial bond certificates. I acknowledge that I shall repay the sum stated herein, in accordance with the statutory repayment plan, within the period specified, with five percent annual compound interest throughout the entire term of this partial bond certificate, and that upon the drawing of lots of this partial bond certificate, at the time also specified in the prospectus, together with the amount of interest accrued, it shall be paid without objection to every presenter by the wholesale banking house **Hammer & Karoly** in Vienna. This partial bond certificate carries neither mortgage nor other security on the wholesale banking house **Hammer & Karoly** or its legal successors, yet it may be pledged by every holder of this partial bond certificate at the rate specified in this drawing of lots, should he wish to do so prior to the maturity date.

In witness whereof I sign this document, which serves as the basis for this loan, in Vienna on November 6, 1847, **One Million Guilders Conv.-Coinage.** [Imperial Royal] Duke Esterházy Esterházy [unclear], mathematical and book-keeping statements including: [unclear] in the Duchy of Carinthia, Villacher documentation, Pedersen, and [unclear] Tarig, all: of the Portfolio holder Goggan, Count.

title: Esterházy Central-Casse 20-Gulden Partial Debenture, Vienna, 1847
description: The reverse of a partial debenture (Partial-Schuldverschreibung) issued by the Gräflich Casimir Esterházy'sche Central-Casse, the central treasury of the comital estate of Count Casimir Esterházy, for a sum of 20 Gulden Conventionsmünze. It belongs to a loan of 1,000,000 florins C.M. raised in 50,000 such 20-florin debentures, secured on the count's domains. The page sets out the Rückzahlungs-Plan, a table of dated drawings by which the bonds were progressively redeemed, followed by the printed conditions, which enumerate the pledged estates and forests and bind the central treasury to repay. Dated Vienna, 15 December 1847, it bears the engraved Esterházy arms, the manuscript signature of Count Casimir Esterházy, and a countersignature. Such estate-backed amortizing loans let a great landed house tap public credit on the security of its lands rather than through a bank, on the eve of the 1848 upheavals.

notes: Reverse (p.2/2) of goetzmann0996 (Count Esterhazy bond); Rückzahlungs-Plan (repayment plan) for the gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'schen Anlehen von fl. 1,000,000 C.M. in 20 fl. Piecen; signed by trustees Werner Morris and Hoffmann Berliner; Die gräflich Casimir Esterhazy'sche Central-Casse; Vienna, 1848

title: USSR Third State Loan for Reconstruction Bond, 1948
description: A state loan bond with a face value of one hundred rubles (Облигация на сумму Сто Рублей), issued under the Third State Loan for the Reconstruction and Development of the National Economy of the USSR (Третий Государственный Заём Восстановления и Развития Народного Хозяйства СССР) and dated 1948. The bond belongs to the cycle of mass subscription loans by which the Soviet state, in the wake of wartime devastation, drew savings from the population to finance the recovery of industry and agriculture under the Fourth Five-Year Plan; such loans were promoted as patriotic duty and largely deducted from wages. The engraved frame is unambiguously didactic: the state emblem with hammer, sickle, and wheat crowns a panorama of a factory, a hydroelectric dam, and combine harvesters working a grain field, fusing heavy industry and mechanized agriculture into a single image of planned reconstruction.
creator: USSR (Soviet Government)
