title: Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid Lottery Loan Share, Rotterdam, 1871
description: A lottery-loan share (Loterij-Geldleening) of f 2.50, one of 400,000 making up a total loan of f 1,000,000 raised by the Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid, the Dutch association for the promotion of national defence readiness. Authorised by Ministerial Resolution of 2 July 1870, the loan was secured by a guarantee fund of roughly f 500,000 effectief and bore interest reckoned at 5 percent. Issued at Rotterdam in May 1871, it sets out a drawing plan: twice yearly, on 31 July and 15 January, 12,000 shares are redeemed by lot with premiums headed by f 75,000 and descending through f 30,000, f 25,000, f 20,000 and many smaller prizes down to f 10. The instrument fuses charity, patriotism and speculation, a private patriotic body funding post-1870 military preparedness by selling the public tickets dressed as redeemable bonds. The engraved trophy of arms, flanked by provincial shields and crowned with the royal motto JE MAINTIENDRAI and the banner ALLES VOOR HET VADERLAND, binds the subscriber's hope of a prize to the defence of the fatherland.
creator: Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid
notes: Loterij-Geldleening, f. 1,000,000 / 400,000 shares at f. 2.50. Series 7734, No. 38. Rotterdam, May 1877. Draws: July 31 and January 15 annually; 12,000 shares. Top prize f. 10,000. Vereeniging tot Bevordering van 's Lands Weerbaarheid (national defense association).

title: Imperial Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate, St Petersburg, 1822
description: This printed certificate is a registered claim to perpetual income (Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ, "Nepreryvny Dokhod") yielding five per cent, issued through the Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts (Коммиссія погашенія долговъ) and entered in the state debt book on 1 March 1822 at Pavlovsk. Its capital is stated twice over — 960 silver roubles equal to 148 pounds sterling — the dual face, together with the London paying-agent stamp at upper right, marking it as part of Russia's sterling-denominated foreign loan placed on the Western market. As a non-redeemable instrument paying interest in perpetuity, it functioned as the Russian counterpart of the British consol, converting the empire's borrowing into a permanent charge on treasury revenue. The crowned double-headed eagle clasping orb and sceptre asserts that this perpetual obligation rests on imperial sovereign credit.
creator: Imperial Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate (Непрерывный Доход по 5 на сто), 960 Roubles. Registered State Debt Book, March 1, 1822. Imperial double-headed eagle. Государственная Комиссия погашения долгов. Perpetual 5% income, semi-annual payments. Russian equivalent of consols.
transcription:
# Imperial Russian 5% Perpetual Income Certificate — Transcription

## Original:

(Printed on both left and right vertical side borders, repeated: **Russian 5 PerCent Loan (1822)**)

(Upper left, in a printed box:) **№ 79948**

(Centre top: crowned imperial double-headed eagle with orb and sceptre)

(Upper right, in a printed box:) **№ 15163**   (handwritten to the right: 79)

**ROUBLES 960**   1822 года.   **£148 Sterling**

(handwritten beneath: 528 / 328)

**НЕПРЕРЫВНЫЙ ДОХОДЪ ПО 5 НА СТО.**

Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ на капиталъ, внесенный въ Государственную долговую книгу Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ, состоящій въ девяти стахъ шестидесяти рубляхъ серебромъ, равняющихся 148 фунтамъ стерлинговъ.

Записанный 1го Марта 1822.

(handwritten:) Книга 2. страни. 479. 2 займа Лит. А.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства имѣетъ право на непрерывный доходъ, заключающійся въ сорока [осми] (обозрѣнію мѣшаетъ штемпель) рубляхъ серебромъ, платимый каждые полгода по его произволу въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ, [то есть] двадцать четыре рубля 1го числа Марта и двадцать четыре рубля серебрянными рублями настоящаго вѣса и пробы, естьли въ С. Петербургѣ; естьли же въ Лондонѣ, по [три шиллинга] одинъ пенсъ стерлинговъ за каждый серебряный рубль: въ томъ и другомъ случаѣ по предъявленіи купона на причитающійся къ платежу непрерывный доходъ.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства можетъ, представивъ оное въ Государственную Коммиссію погашенія долговъ, требовать, чтобы вмѣсто онаго выданъ былъ безсрочный билетъ на туже сумму на его имя, или на имя лица, или лицъ, которыя онъ назначитъ: въ такомъ случаѣ непрерывный доходъ имѣетъ [быть] платимъ токмо въ С. Петербургѣ въ вышепостановленные сроки, и трансфертъ или передача таковыхъ билетовъ должна быть производима по существующимъ правиламъ.

Двадцать четыре купона принадлежатъ къ сему Свидѣтельству. Естьли при наступленіи срока послѣдне[го] изъ нихъ, капиталъ не будетъ выкупленъ, или обращенъ въ безсрочный билетъ Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ; то имѣютъ быть выданы вновь двадцать четыре купона, и такъ далѣе, дабы обезпечить [предъ]явителю полученіе непрерывнаго дохода въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ.

Прил. Особенный фондъ, или капиталъ, составляющій (ежегодно) одинъ процентъ со всей суммы сего [зай]ма, назначенъ на выкупъ его.

(В правой боковой рамкѣ, въ рамкѣ:) Капиталъ 960 рублей серебромъ равняющійся 148 фун. стерл.

**Выписка изъ Устава Коммиссіи:**

22. Безсрочный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и платежъ срочныхъ долговъ [...] въ мирное и военное время безъ всякаго различія, къ дружественной ли или не[пріятельской нации] принадлежитъ вкладчикъ.

23. Въ случаѣ смерти иностраннаго вкладчика безъ завѣщанія, безсрочный долгъ имѣетъ принадлежать его наслѣдникамъ по порядку и правамъ наслѣдія той націи, къ которой онъ принадлежалъ.

24. Капиталы, въ безсрочный долгъ обращенные, составляя ненарушимую собственность вкладчика, не подвергаются секвестру ни по казеннымъ, ни по частнымъ искамъ; изключая, естьли весь или часть капитала представлена въ залогъ по подрядамъ, или другимъ сдѣлкамъ съ казною или частными людьми, или въ обезпеченіе иска; въ такомъ случаѣ оный подлежитъ общимъ правиламъ о залогахъ и взысканіяхъ. Равнымъ образомъ таковые капиталы ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ никакимъ налогамъ.

25. Никто не можетъ быть противъ его желанія принужденъ къ обратному полученію всего или части капитала, въ безсрочномъ долгѣ помѣщеннаго. Но дабы съ другой стороны предоставить каждому [возможн]ости обратить оный, когда пожелаетъ, въ наличныя деньги, Коммиссія имѣетъ упо[треблять] на выкупъ оныхъ по курсу особенный капиталъ погашенія, ежегодно сверхъ платежа не[прерывныхъ] доходовъ на предметъ сей назначаемый.

За Управляющаго   (подпись)

Бухгалтеръ   (подпись)

(Наложенные штемпели и печати: два красныхъ штемпеля «TEN SHILLINGS» и «FIVE SHILLINGS»; круглая печать съ цифрами «1900»; французскіе фискальные штемпели «UNIS DES ETATS ETRANGERS — 1% — PLEIN TARIF» и «DROITS DES ETRANGERS EN LONDRES 1834»; круглый штемпель платежнаго агента въ верхнемъ правомъ углу.)

---

## English Translation:

(Printed on both side borders, repeated: **Russian 5 PerCent Loan (1822)**)

(Upper left, boxed:) **No. 79948**

(Centre: crowned imperial double-headed eagle with orb and sceptre)

(Upper right, boxed:) **No. 15163**   (handwritten: 79)

**ROUBLES 960**   in the year 1822.   **£148 Sterling**

(handwritten: 528 / 328)

**PERPETUAL INCOME AT 5 PER HUNDRED.**

Certificate of perpetual income on capital entered in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Repayment of Debts, amounting to nine hundred and sixty silver roubles, equal to 148 pounds sterling.

Registered 1st March 1822.

(handwritten:) Book 2, page 479, 2nd loan, Letter A.

The bearer of this Certificate has the right to a perpetual income consisting of forty-[eight] (partly covered by a stamp) silver roubles, payable every half-year at his choice in St Petersburg or London, [that is] twenty-four roubles on the 1st of March and twenty-four roubles in silver roubles of proper weight and standard, if in St Petersburg; but if in London, at [three shillings] one penny sterling for each silver rouble: in either case upon presentation of the coupon for the perpetual income falling due for payment.

The bearer of this Certificate may, on presenting it to the State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, demand that in its place a perpetual (registered) bill be issued for the same sum, in his name or in the name of the person or persons he shall designate: in that case the perpetual income shall be payable only in St Petersburg on the aforesaid dates, and the transfer or conveyance of such bills must be carried out according to existing rules.

Twenty-four coupons belong to this Certificate. If, when the last of them falls due, the capital has not been redeemed or converted into a perpetual bill of the Commission for the Repayment of Debts, then twenty-four new coupons shall be issued, and so on, in order to secure to the bearer the receipt of the perpetual income in St Petersburg or London.

Note. A special fund, or capital, amounting (annually) to one per cent of the whole sum of this loan, is designated for its redemption.

(In the right-hand border, boxed:) Capital of 960 silver roubles equal to 148 pounds sterling.

**Extract from the Statute of the Commission:**

22. The perpetual income, as well as the payment of term debts [...] in time of peace and of war without any distinction, whether the depositor belongs to a friendly or a [hostile nation].

23. In the event of the death of a foreign depositor without a will, the perpetual debt shall belong to his heirs according to the order and rights of inheritance of the nation to which he belonged.

24. Capitals converted into perpetual debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor, are not subject to sequestration whether for treasury or for private claims; except where the whole or part of the capital has been given as security for contracts, or for other transactions with the treasury or with private persons, or as security for a claim; in that case it is subject to the general rules on pledges and recoveries. Likewise such capitals are in no case liable to any taxes.

25. No one may, against his will, be compelled to take back the whole or part of the capital placed in the perpetual debt. But in order, on the other hand, to allow everyone the possibility of converting it, whenever he wishes, into ready money, the Commission shall employ for their redemption, at the market rate, a special sinking-fund capital, appointed annually for this purpose over and above the payment of the perpetual incomes.

For the Manager   (signature)

Accountant   (signature)

(Overlaid stamps and seals: two red stamps "TEN SHILLINGS" and "FIVE SHILLINGS"; a circular seal bearing the figures "1900"; French fiscal stamps "UNIS DES ETATS ETRANGERS — 1% — PLEIN TARIF" and "DROITS DES ETRANGERS EN LONDRES 1834"; a circular paying-agent stamp in the upper right corner.)

title: Russian Public Debt Inscription, Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission, Saint Petersburg, 1876
description: A French-language inscription in the Grand Livre de la dette publique de Russie, registered through the Commission Impériale d'amortissement (Imperial Sinking-Fund Commission) and dated 1876. It records a capital of 500 roubles in silver, entered in the Grand Livre on 11 October 1876, yielding a perpetual rente of five per cent per year — an annual income of 25 roubles, paid each half-year as twelve roubles fifty copecks between the 1st and 15th of March and of September. The capital stands inscribed to the Amsterdam house under the direction of Hope & Cie, together with Ketwich & Voombergh and the widow W. Borski. Issued from Saint Petersburg for Dutch account, the document belongs to the registered-debt machinery through which the Russian treasury drew foreign capital; the engraved guilloche borders and restrained roundel frame project the fiscal solidity such inscriptions were meant to convey. An Extrait du Règlement, Chap. II, sets out the conditions of inscription and transfer.
creator: Commission Impériale d'amortissement de la dette publique de Russie
notes: Inscription au Grand Livre, dette publique de Russie. Commission Impériale d'amortissement. 1876, Première Série. Capital [?] Roubles silver; annual income 500 Roubles. Amsterdam: Ketwich & Voombergh; Mme. W. Borski. Payable March 1/15 and September 1/15. French-language certificate.

each half-year of [**twelve thousand five hundred roubles**]
payable from 1 to 15 March and from 1 to 15 September.

**EXTRACT FROM THE COMMISSION REGULATIONS CHAP. II.**

[Long regulatory text regarding perpetual income, capital investment conditions, and administrative procedures]

The Director of the Commission
[Signature]

This Inscription is the one to
Enforced as signed                at the ___________

Certify the signature and above by

The General Council of Russia

NOTE. The collector or bearer touched the income that had to justify the inscription in his name in the grand ledger of Russian public debt with the Commission.

---

## Notable Markings:

- **Serial numbers:** No. 117442 (left); No. 67444 (right)
- **Document number:** 1876
- **Annual income (Rente annuelle):** 500 Roubles (left); Capital (Capital): 500 Roubles (right)
- **Decorative border:** Ornamental frame with circular medallions
- **Signature:** Director's signature (partially illegible)
- **Handwritten annotations:** Various manuscript entries including dates and names
- **Ornamental corners:** Spiral rosette designs in all four corners

title: Russian Perpetual Capital Certificate, State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, St. Petersburg, 1839
description: This is a printed certificate (Билетъ) of the Imperial Russian Государственная Коммиссія погашенія долговъ, the State Commission for the Repayment of Debts, registering a Perpetual Capital (Капиталъ Безсрочный) of 5,000 roubles inscribed in the holder's name in the state debt book under letter A, page 589. It yields a continuous income of 300 roubles a year, the rate stated as "по Шести на Сто въ Годъ" — six per hundred annually — disbursed in two coupons of 150 roubles, payable between the 15th and 31st of January and the 15th and 31st of July. Issued at St. Petersburg and dated 16 January 1839, it bears the Imperial double-headed eagle and the manuscript signatures of the Administrator (Управляющій) and Bookkeeper (Бухгалтеръ). A perpetual claim on the Treasury rather than a dated loan, it is Russia's counterpart to the Western consol or rente perpétuelle — a parallel the bilingual document makes explicit in its French inscriptions referring to a "rente perpétuelle" entered in the "dette publique."
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 300 Roubles. 1839. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Signed by Administrator and Bookkeeper.

title: Russian Perpetual Capital Certificate, 500 Rubles Silver
description: This is a perpetual-debt certificate (Билетъ … на Капиталъ Безсрочный) of the Imperial Russian Commission for the Repayment of Debts, recording a principal of 500 rubles "in Russian silver coin" (Россійскою серебряною монетою) yielding a fixed annual income of 30 rubles, that is, six per hundred (по Шести на Сто) per year, paid in two half-yearly installments in January and July. Issued at St. Petersburg and dated February 1854, under Nicholas I, it is subscribed by the Commission's Administrator (Управляющій) and Bookkeeper (Бухгалтеръ). The instrument has no maturity: the state undertakes perpetual interest rather than repayment of capital, a convenient form for funding standing obligations on the eve of the Crimean War. The crowned double-headed eagle dominating the upper border, flanked by guilloche panels and a punched coupon margin, asserts sovereign credit; the silver-denominated face reflects the Kankrin reform that anchored the ruble to a silver standard.
creator: Russian State Commission for the Repayment of Debts
notes: Билет Государственной Комиссии погашения долгов. Perpetual Capital (Капитал Безсрочный), 50 Roubles in Russian silver (серебряным монетою). 1854. Income 6% p.a., paid January 15 and July 15. Imperial double-headed eagle. Post-Kankrin monetary reform denomination.

title: Hope & Co. Russian 6% Silver Certificate, Amsterdam, 1857
description: A bilingual Dutch-French certificate for a 500-ruble inscription in the 6% Russian Funds in Silver (Russische Fondsen in Zilver / Inscription Russe en Argent), issued at Amsterdam on 19 May 1857 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and Weduwe W. Borski. The instrument represents the holder's share of an inscription entered in the Russian State Debt Book at St. Petersburg (Groot Boek / Grand Livre) and administered through a bureau the three firms maintained in Amsterdam; interest of six per cent runs for the holder's account and risk, drawn against half-yearly coupons, and the original inscription may be reclaimed upon surrender of the certificate and its unused tickets. The document exemplifies Amsterdam's enduring role as the conduit through which Dutch savings reached the Russian treasury, the silver denomination assuring investors of a hard-money claim. The engraved frame, with its restrained neoclassical roundels and balanced typographic banners, projects the sober probity of an established merchant-banking partnership rather than ornamental display.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Weduwe W. Borski
notes: 6% Certificaat/Certificat d'Inscription Russe, Russische Fondsen in Zilver/en Argent. No. 670, R° 500 Roubles. Amsterdam, November 19, 1857. Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski. Interest 6% p.a. Ten half-yearly coupon tickets.
transcription:
# Transcription

## Original (Dutch/French):

**LEFT COLUMN (Dutch):**

CERTIFICAAT
Van 6 per Cents
RUSSISCHE FONDSEN
IN
ZILVER.

N°. 670.—

HOUDER dezes is geregtigd tot eene Inscriptie in het GRONDBOEK
nomens ƒ 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen, betaalbaar in ZILVER, ingeschreven in het Groot-Boek te St. Petersburg, ten name van het ADMINISTRATIEBURO, opgericht te Amsterdam, onder directie van

HOPE en COMP.,
KETWICH en VOOMBERGH,
EN
WED. W. BORSKI.

Waarvan de Interessen voor rekening en risico van den HOUDER en ontvangen en uitbetaald zullen worden op Coupons, ingevolge Art. 6 van het uitgegeven Bericht.

De HOUDER van dit Certificaat zal te allen tijde geregtigd zijn de originale INSCRIPTIE terug te vorderen, tegen terugave van dit Certificaat, met de onderwijl afgestelde HALVE JAARLIJKS COUPONS, tegen betaling van de onkosten, in Art. 10 van het Bericht vermeld.

Amsterdam, den 19 der Maand 1857

Gezien bij mij
S. Gimmelman
Notaris

Hierbij afgegeven Halfjarige COUPONS, tot 19 Juli 1859 en Calon.

---

**RIGHT COLUMN (French):**

CERTIFICAT
D'INSCRIPTION RUSSE
à 6 pour Cent
EN
ARGENT.

R° 500:—

LE PORTEUR à droit à une Inscription de Rente CENTRALE nominale capital à 6 pour Cent, de la Dette russe de Suisse, payable en ARGENT, inscrit sur le Grand-Livre à St. Pétersbourg, au nom du BUREAU d'ADMINISTRATION, établi à Amsterdam, sous la direction de

HOPE et COMP.,
KETWICH et VOOMBERGH,
ET
Veuve W. BORSKI.

Dont les Intérêts seront reçus pour compte et risque du PORTEUR et payés contre la remise des Coupons, suivant Art. 6 du Prospectus.

Le PORTEUR de ce Certificat pourra en tout temps réclamer l'INSCRIPTION originale, contre restitution du Certificat, avec les Coupons non échus y appartenant, et la RECONNAISSANCE y annexée pour obtenir le paiement des FRAIS, moyennant le paiement des frais, mentionnées dans l'Art. 10 du Prospectus.

Amsterdam, den 19' der Mei 1857

[Signature]
Ferdinand Mombault
Patr. Wooels.

Délivré COUPONS de Rentes de Six Mois, jusqu'au 19 Juillet 1859 avec Calon.

---

## English translation:

**LEFT COLUMN:**

CERTIFICATE
Of 6 per Cent
RUSSIAN FUNDS
IN
SILVER.

No. 670.—

THE HOLDER of this is entitled to an Inscription in the REGISTER
in the name of ƒ 6 per Cent Russian Funds, payable in SILVER, registered in the Grand Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATIVE BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
WED. W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest for the account and risk of the HOLDER shall be received and paid on Coupons, according to Art. 6 of the published Notice.

The HOLDER of this Certificate shall at all times be entitled to reclaim the original INSCRIPTION in return, against return of this Certificate, with the half-yearly COUPONS detached in the meantime, against payment of the costs mentioned in Art. 10 of the Notice.

Amsterdam, the 19th of the Month 1857

Seen by me
S. Gimmelman
Notary

Half-yearly COUPONS hereby issued, up to 19 July 1859 and Calon.

---

**RIGHT COLUMN:**

CERTIFICATE
OF RUSSIAN INSCRIPTION
at 6 per Cent
IN
SILVER.

R° 500:—

THE BEARER is entitled to an Inscription of CENTRAL Rent with nominal capital at 6 per Cent, of the Russian Debt of Switzerland, payable in SILVER, inscribed on the Grand-Book at St. Petersburg, in the name of the ADMINISTRATIVE BUREAU, established in Amsterdam, under the direction of

HOPE and COMP.,
KETWICH and VOOMBERGH,
AND
Widow W. BORSKI.

Of which the Interest shall be received for account and risk of the BEARER and paid against delivery of the Coupons, according to Art. 6 of the Prospectus.

The BEARER of this Certificate may at any time reclaim the original INSCRIPTION, against return of the Certificate, with the Coupons not yet due belonging thereto, and the ACKNOWLEDGMENT annexed thereto to obtain payment of the FEES, upon payment of the costs mentioned in Art. 10 of the Prospectus.

Amsterdam, the 19th of May 1857

[Signature]
Ferdinand Mombault
Patr. Wooels.

Delivered Six-Month Income COUPONS, up to 19 July 1859 with Calon.

- **Serial Number:** No. 670 (left side), R° 500 (right side)
- **Date:** 19 November 1857 (Amsterdam)
- **Signatures:** S. Gimmelman (Notary - left), Ferdinand Mombault and Patr. Wooels (right)
- **Coupon validity:** Half-yearly coupons issued until 19 July 1859
- **Decorative border:** Ornamental frame in classical style surrounding entire document
- **Language:** Bilingual Dutch and French text in parallel columns
- **Denomination:** 500 Roubles in Silver (Zilver/Argent)
- **Interest rate:** 6% per annum

title: Hope & Co. Russian 6% Inscription Certificate in Bank Assignations, Amsterdam, 1825
description: A bilingual Dutch and French certificate of Russian inscription ("Certificaat van 6 per Cents Russische Fondsen in Bank-Assignatien" / "Certificat d'Inscription Russe à 6 pour Cent en Assignations de Banque") for 1,000 roubles, bearing interest at six per cent and reckoned in bank assignations, the paper rouble then in circulation. The certificate was issued at Amsterdam on 16 April 1825 by the houses of Hope en Comp., Ketwich en Voombergh, and the Widow W. Borski, who placed the underlying stock in the holder's name and undertook to procure payment of interest and principal. The document attests to Amsterdam's role as the conduit through which the Russian crown's debt reached the European investing public, the great Dutch merchant banks lending their credit to a sovereign borrower. That the sum is denominated in assignations, rather than silver, fixes the instrument before Kankrin's monetary reform of 1839. The engraving is plain and clerical, its ornament confined to a beaded border, foregrounding contractual exactitude over display.
creator: Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Weduwe W. Borski
notes: 6% Certificaat/Certificat d'Inscription Russe, in Bank-Assignatien/Assignations de Banque. No. 4031, R° 1,000 Roubles. Amsterdam, August 16, 1822. Hope en Comp.; Ketwich en Voombergh; Wed. W. Borski. Ten half-yearly coupons (from July 1, 1829). Denominated in Bank Assignations (pre-Kankrin Reform Russian paper currency).

title: Sillem, Benecke & Co. Russian Funds Certificate, Hamburg, 1821
description: A Dutch-language certificaat issued at Hamburg on 13 March 1821, representing an inscription of five hundred roubles (Vyfhonderd Roebels) of 5 per cent Russian funds, payable in metallic coin (Metalick) and entered in the grootboek der Russische Inschryvingen in the name of an administration established at Hamburg. The bureau was directed by the firm Sillem, Benecke & Comp. together with H.J. Stresow; interest at 5 per cent was collected for the holder's account against coupons under Article 5 of the published notice, and the holder could at any time reclaim the original inscription against return of the certificate and unused coupons. The document records how a Hamburg house repackaged Russian sovereign debt for circulation among northern European savers, the city serving as an entrepôt that channelled Dutch and German capital toward the Tsar's treasury after the Napoleonic wars. The typography is austere and mercantile, the engraved 'CERTIFICAAT' heading and the vertical 'KANTOOR VAN ADMINISTRATIE' panel asserting bureaucratic order over a purely financial promise.
creator: Sillem, Benecke & Comp.; H.J. Stresow
notes: Certificaat No. 13549, 500 Roubles 5% Russian Funds payable in Metalick (specie). Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and H.J. Stresow, Hamburg. Grand Ledger inscription. Hamburg, March 13, 1821. Notary: N. Dongkants. Hamburg-issued Dutch certificate for Russian government debt.

# Transcription

**Original (Dutch):**

CERTIFICAAT.

No. 13549.

Goed voor eene Inscriptie van Vyfhonderd Roebels 5 pct. Russische fondsen, betaalbaar in Metalick, ingeschreven in het grootboek der Russische Inschrijvingen ten Naame van de administratie opgericht te Hamburg onder directie van de Heeren Sillem, Benecke & Comp. en de Heer H.J. Stresow,

waarvan de Intressen voor Rekening van den Houder ontfangen & uitbetaald zullen worden op Coupons, in gevolge Art 5. van het uitgegeven bericht.

De houder van dit Certificaat zal ten allen tyde en op deszelfs eerste aanvrage, gerechtigd zyn, de voorschreven Som van Vyfhonderd Roebels terug te vorderen, in Originneele Inscriptie, tegen terug gave van dit Certificaat, met de onverscheene Coupons, en het daarby afgegeven bewys voor de afgift van volgende Coupons, alles in gevolge Art. 2. van het bericht.

Hamburg 13 Maart 1821.

[Signature] Sillem Benecke & Co
[Signature] H.J. Stren [or Strem - unclear]

Voor depot ingevolge Art. 4. van het bericht

[Signature] M. Donghants
Notaris.

No. 13549.

Good for an Inscription of Five Hundred Roubles 5 pct. Russian Funds, payable in Specie, registered in the Grand Ledger of Russian Inscriptions in the Name of the administration established in Hamburg under the direction of the Gentlemen Sillem, Benecke & Comp. and Mister H.J. Stresow,

of which the Interest for Account of the Holder shall be received & paid out on Coupons, in accordance with Art. 5 of the published notice.

The holder of this Certificate shall at all times and upon his first request be entitled to reclaim the aforesaid Sum of Five Hundred Roubles, in Original Inscription, against return of this Certificate, with the unused Coupons, and the thereby issued proof for the delivery of following Coupons, all in accordance with Art. 2 of the notice.

title: Volksstaat Hessen Brown-Coal-Rye Loan Bond, Darmstadt, 1923
description: This is a Schuldverschreibung (bearer bond) of the Volksstaat Hessen, the People's State of Hesse, issued for the value of one unit (Eine Einheit) of the state's 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe — the Brown-Coal-Rye Loan of 1923. The Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration) executed it at Darmstadt on 5 April 1923, attaching thirty interest coupons (Zinsscheine) and one renewal coupon (Erneuerungsschein). Interest of 6% runs annually from 1 May to 30 April, with the loan term beginning 1 May 1923 and redemption opening from 1 May 1926. Crucially, the bond's worth is fixed not in money but in the market value of brown coal and rye. Issued as the Reichsmark dissolved into hyperinflation, it is a Sachwertanleihe, a real-value loan whose denomination in tangible commodities shielded creditors from a currency losing all meaning. The restrained typographic frame, free of allegory, lets the contractual text and the Hessian arms speak plainly to fiscal emergency.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)
notes: Schuldverschreibung, Volksstaat Hessen. Eine Einheit, 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-Anleihe 1923. Reihe 37, No. 20002. Darmstadt, April 5, 1923. Value indexed to brown coal and rye prices. 30 interest + 1 renewal coupon. Weimar hyperinflation commodity-indexed bond (Sachwertanleihe).
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (German):**

Reihe 37                           Eine                              № 20002
                                   Einheit

Schuldverschreibung

des

Volksstaats Hessen

über den Geldwert von 1 Einheit der 6% Braunkohle-Roggen-
Anleihe von 1923.

Gegenwärtige Schuldverschreibung ist für jeden Inhaber gültig. 30 Zinscheine
und 1 Erneuerungsschein sind beigefügt.

Das Zinsjahr läuft vom 1. Mai bis 30. April. Die Zinsen werden jährlich
gezahlt am 1. Mai und am 1. November fällig, erstmals am 1. Februar 1924 für die Zeit
vom 1. Mai 1923 bis 30. April 1924.

Die Rückzahlung der Anleihe erfolgt durch Auslosung über freihandigen
Rückkauf vom 1. Mai 1926 an mit jährlich 2 2/10 des ursprünglichen Gesamtnennn-
betrages. Vom 1. Mai 1928 an können die noch umlaufenden Schuldbor-
schreibungen mit halbjährlicher Frist ganz oder teilweise gekündigt werden.
Für die Anleihe haftet der hessische Staat mit seiner Steuerkraft und seinem
gesamten Vermögen.

Im übrigen gelten die auf der Rückseite abgedruckten Bedingungen.

Darmstadt, den 5. April 1923.

Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung.

Ausgefertigt: [signature]

---

**English translation:**

Series 37                          One                               No. 20002
                                   Unit

Debt Certificate

of the

People's State of Hesse

for the monetary value of 1 unit of the 6% Brown Coal-Rye
Loan of 1923.

This debt certificate is valid for any bearer. 30 interest coupons
and 1 renewal coupon are attached.

The interest year runs from May 1 to April 30. Interest is paid annually,
due on May 1 and November 1, first due on February 1, 1924 for the period
from May 1, 1923 to April 30, 1924.

Repayment of the loan occurs by drawing lots via free negotiated
repurchase from May 1, 1926 onward at 2 2/10 percent annually of the original total
principal amount. From May 1, 1928 onward, the still-circulating debt
certificates may be terminated in whole or in part with six-month notice.
The Hessian state guarantees the loan with its tax revenue and its
entire assets.

Moreover, the conditions printed on the reverse side apply.

Darmstadt, April 5, 1923.

Hessian State Debt Administration.

title: Spanish Debt Conversion Passive Stock Certificate, London, 1835
description: This bearer certificate, headed "Conversion of Spanish Debt. Passive Stock," entitles its holder to fourteen pounds, three shillings and four pence (£14.3.4) of Spanish Five per Cent Passive Stock, issued at London on 4 May 1835. It is an interim, fractional claim: on presentation of enough such certificates to make up one of the standard amounts printed below — £42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, or £1,020 — the signatory engages to deliver in exchange a bond of the Spanish Government. It bears the signature of the Ricardo house, London agents for the operation. The piece belongs to the restructuring of Spain's defaulted external debt under the Martínez de la Rosa government, in which arrears of interest were capitalised into a deferred "passive" class. Plainly typeset within a guilloche border, it speaks less to ornament than to the workaday machinery of the London market in foreign sovereign paper.
creator: Spanish Government (London agent: Ricardo)
notes: Conversion of Spanish Debt, Passive Stock Certificate No. 457, £14 3s 4d. Spanish 5% Passive Stock. Bearer exchanges certificate at standard amounts: £42.10, £85, £170, £255, £510, £1,020 for Spanish government bond. Signed by [Ricardo]. London, May 4, 1835. 1834–35 Spanish debt restructuring.

title: Stadnitski & van Heukelom Russian Loan Certificate, Amsterdam, 1825
description: A bilingual Dutch and French certificate documenting an inscription in the State Debt Book of the Imperial Russian Amortization Commission at St. Petersburg, entitling the bearer to a capital of Duizend Roebels / Mille Roubles (1,000 roubles) in assignats at 6 percent interest. Issued at Amsterdam, dated 30 June 1825, it was administered by a syndicate of Amsterdam banking houses headed by Stadnitski & van Heukelom and including Jacob van Beeck Vollenhoven, Samuel & David Saportas, Lamaison & Bouwer, Johannes Samuel Wurfbain, Hendrik Oyens, and De Lanoy & Burlage. The certificate is drawn up under the prospectus published 30 June 1824, and the holder may at any time reclaim the underlying inscription by surrendering the certificate with its unused coupons; twelve half-yearly interest coupons were attached. The instrument speaks to the early-nineteenth-century channeling of Dutch capital into Russian sovereign debt, with Amsterdam intermediaries repackaging the tsar's St. Petersburg inscriptions into transferable paper for investors in the Low Countries. The typography is plain and commercial, its restrained roman and italic lettering signalling fiscal probity rather than ornament.
creator: Stadnitski & van Heukelom; Jacob van Beeck Vollenhoen; Samuel & David Saportas; Lamaison & Bouwer; Johannes Samuel Wurfbain; Hendrik Oyens; De Lanoy & Burlage

title: London Stock Exchange WWI Good Delivery Certificate
description: This printed certificate, issued under the London Stock Exchange's Temporary Regulations for the Re-Opening of the Stock Exchange, applies Regulation 10 to a single bearer security: a City of Baku 5% 1910 bond for £20. Completed in manuscript and dated 29 April 1916, it bears the signature and inked stamp of the Secretary of the Share and Loan Department, certifying that the bond "has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown." Regulation 10 barred bearer or blank-endorsed securities from good delivery unless impressed with the government stamp dated before the 1st of October and accompanied by a banker's or broker's declaration that they had not, since the outbreak of war, passed into enemy ownership. Such rules, framed when the Exchange reopened after its August 1914 closure, sought to keep securities held by enemy nationals out of wartime dealings. The austere, text-only sheet, printed by E. Couchman & Co. of Throgmorton Street, embodies the administrative machinery by which a great market reconciled open trading with the controls of total war.
creator: London Stock Exchange (Share and Loan Department)
notes: London Stock Exchange WWI Regulation 10 good delivery certificate. City of Baker 5% 1910 Bond No. 58707, £20. Signed Secretary, Share and Loan Department. April 29, 1916. WWI wartime enemy-ownership regulations.
transcription:
# Transcription

**TEMPORARY REGULATIONS FOR THE RE-OPENING OF THE STOCK EXCHANGE.**

---

**REGULATION 10.**

No Securities to bearer or endorsed in blank will be a good delivery unless impressed with the Government stamp dated previous to the 1st October and accompanied by a declaration by a Banker, Broker or other responsible party that they have not, since the outbreak of war, been in enemy ownership, except in cases permitted under Regulation 4 (3) or Securities sent for registration abroad under Clause 9 and such other Securities as may be expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery on special cause being shown.

Title of Security .... *City of Baker 5% 1910* ....

Bond for £20 Numbered .... *58707* ....

I hereby certify that the above Security has been expressly passed by the Committee as a good delivery, special cause having been shown.

Signature .... *[Handwritten signature - illegible]* ....

Secretary .... *SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE.* ....

Date .... *29 4 1916* ....

---

**REGULATION 4 (3).** — No dealings will be allowed in any new issue, other than an issue of British Government Securities made after the 4th January, 1915, unless specially allowed by the Committee and approved by the Treasury.

**REGULATION 9 (Sentence 3).** — Securities which have been transmitted abroad merely for registration shall for the purpose of this regulation be deemed to have remained in physical possession in the United Kingdom.

**REGULATION 11.** — *The expression "Enemy" in these regulations means any person or body of persons of whatever nationality, resident, or carrying on business in an enemy country, but does not include persons of enemy nationality who are neither resident nor carrying on business in an enemy country. In the case of incorporated bodies, enemy character attaches only to those incorporated in an enemy country.*

COPYRIGHT. — Printed and sold by E. COUCHMAN & CO., LTD., 23, Throgmorton Street, E.C.

---

- Handwritten signature (Secretary's signature - illegible)
- Date stamp: "29 4 1916" (29 April 1916)
- Bond number: 58707
- Bond denomination: £20
- Security title: City of Baker 5% 1910
- Printed stamp: "SECRETARY, SHARE AND LOAN DEPARTMENT, STOCK EXCHANGE."
- Copyright attribution to E. Couchman & Co., Ltd.

title: United States Treasury Six Per Cent Deferred Stock Certificate, Philadelphia, 1792
description: This is a registered certificate of United States public stock, issued by the Treasury's Register's Office and dated 16 June 1792. It acknowledges that the United States owes Daniel Crommelin & Sons, merchants of Amsterdam, or their assigns, the sum of ten thousand dollars, bearing interest at six per cent per annum from the first day of January 1801, payable quarter-yearly, and redeemable in any one year only to the proportion of eight dollars upon a hundred of the principal. The stock was created under the Act making Provision for the Debt of the United States of 4 August 1790, and is transferable solely by personal appearance or by attorney at the proper office. Signed by Joseph Nourse, Register of the Treasury, with a notarial attestation begun by Clement Biddle of Philadelphia, the certificate embodies Alexander Hamilton's funding program: the deferred interest start and gentle redemption schedule were designed to consolidate Revolutionary debt on terms the new federal credit could sustain, while its placement with a great Dutch house records the role of Amsterdam capital in financing the early republic. The engraved frame holds the small seal of the Treasury rather than allegorical ornament, a restraint befitting an instrument meant to project fiscal sobriety.
creator: Treasury of the United States; Joseph Nourse, Register
notes: US Treasury 6% Funded Debt Certificate No. 42.94, $10,000. Daniel Ternwiles & Wife. Register Office, June 16, 1792. Signed Joseph Nourse, Register. Notarized by Clement Biddle. Created under Funding Act of August 4, 1790. Hamilton's federal debt consolidation.

title: Dutch Deferred Debt Compliance Certificate, Amsterdam, 1815
description: This is a Dutch certificate of compliance with the law (Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet) for the Uitgestelde Schuld, the deferred class of the national public debt, drawn for a capital of ƒ1400. The Commission named in it was established by decree of His Royal Highness (Z.K.H., the Sovereign Prince Willem) of 7 June 1814, charged with executing the Law of 14 May 1814; the certificate attests that articles 5, 10 and 18 of that law have been satisfied, the sum being inscribed in the Oude Grootboek der Publieke Schuld in the name of the village of Heemstede, for which W.H. Geerlings signs as burgemeester. Issued at Amsterdam in March 1815, the document records how the restored House of Orange consolidated the debts left by the Batavian Republic and French annexation, deferring part of the principal rather than repudiating it. Engraved Gothic blackletter headings lend the printed form the gravity of an official instrument of the new monarchy.
creator: Dutch Government Commission (Commissie voor de Uitgestelde Schuld)
notes: Bewijs van Voldoening aan de Wet, Uitgestelde Schuld No. 6231, f. 500. Pursuant to Besluit Z.K.H. June 7, 1814, No. 23, and Wet of May 14, 1814. Holder: W.F. Holling. Post-Napoleonic Dutch deferred debt restructuring. Recorded in Hollandsche Schuld Grootboek.

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, No. 131
description: A bill of exchange, partly printed and partly completed by hand, issued in the name of the United States of America and dated the ninth day of October 1778. Drawn at thirty days' sight upon the Commissioners of the United States at Paris, it directs payment to Anne Brown, or order, of three hundred dollars rendered in fifteen hundred livres tournois at the stated rate of five livres tournois per dollar, for interest due on money borrowed by the United States. It is the third bill of a set of four, payable if the first, second, and fourth remain unpaid. Countersigned by Tho. Smith, Commissioner of the Continental Loan-Office for Pennsylvania, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Issued amid the Revolutionary War, the instrument shows how the Continental Congress, lacking specie at home, serviced the interest on its Loan Office certificates by drawing bills on its agents in France, binding the new republic's credit to its French alliance. The engraved capital and restrained type betray no ornamental program, the document's authority resting on signature and form rather than imagery.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Pennsylvania
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 131, $300 / 1,300 Livres Tournois. October 9, 1776. Payable to Annie Brown. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Pennsylvania Loan Office; signed by H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Among the earliest-dated Continental bills of exchange.

To the Commissioner or Commissioners of the

title: Hessian Rye Loan Bond
description: A 5% Schuldverschreibung (bond) of the Volksstaat Hessen (People's State of Hesse), part of its Roggen-Anleihe (Rye Loan), issued at Darmstadt on 12 December 1923 by the Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Hessian State Debt Administration). Its face value is fixed not in money but in kind: the cash value of 10 Zentner (hundredweight) of rye, the bond declaring that the state owes the bearer "den Geldwert von 10 Zentner Roggen." Against it the state pledges its whole property and revenues, with interest and amortisation charged on general receipts and the state domains. Dated to the closing weeks of the great inflation, when the paper Mark had become worthless, the certificate exemplifies the rye-indexed Sachwertanleihen by which German authorities restored confidence by promising real value rather than nominal sums. The dense black Fraktur lettering and the central rye-sheaf cartouche tie fiscal promise to the harvest meant to secure it.
creator: Hessische Staatsschuldenverwaltung (Volksstaat Hessen)

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, Massachusetts Bay
description: This is a Continental Loan Office bill of exchange, a partly printed, partly manuscript draft by which the United States of America paid interest on its domestic war debt in hard foreign coin. Dated 12 November 1778, it is the First Bill of a set of four (only one of which need be honoured), promising at thirty days' sight to pay Jesse White or order thirty-six dollars, reckoned at five livres tournois to the dollar, hence one hundred and eighty livres tournois, "for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States." It is addressed to the American Commissioners at Paris, countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton, Commissioner of the Continental Loan Office for Massachusetts Bay, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. Issued amid a depreciating paper currency, the instrument let the young confederation reward its lenders in stable specie drawn on French credit, binding the Revolution's finances to its Parisian alliance. The engraved foliate border frames an otherwise plain, businesslike form.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Massachusetts Bay
notes: US Bill of Exchange No. 64, $36 / 180 Livres Tournois. November 12, 1778. Payable to Jesse White. Interest on Loan Office Certificates. Massachusetts Bay Loan Office; Nath. Appleton, Commissioner; H. Atkinson, Treasurer of Loans.

title: Continental Loan Office Bill of Exchange, No. 71
description: This is a bill of exchange, partly engraved and partly completed by hand, drawn by the United States of America on the fourth day of November 1778 and numbered as the first of a set of four ("First Bill, Second, Third and Fourth not paid"). At thirty days' sight it directs the American loan commissioners at Paris to pay John Simpkins, or order, one hundred and twenty dollars rendered as six hundred livres tournois at the fixed rate of five livres to the dollar, the sum representing interest due on money borrowed by the United States. It is countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton, Commissioner of the Continental Loan Office in Massachusetts Bay, and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. The draft embodies the young republic's wartime expedient of paying interest on its domestic loan-office debt in hard foreign currency abroad, drawing on credit extended by France to sustain confidence in the Continental cause when paper money was depreciating at home.
creator: United States of America, Loan Office, Massachusetts Bay

title: Suriname Plantation Loan Conditions
description: This is the printed sheet of conditions (Conditien eener Negotiatie) governing a one-million-guilder loan — "een Millioen of Tienmaal honderd Duysend Guldens" — raised under the direction of the Amsterdam merchants Lever en de Bruine, with Samuel en Jacobus Walland as commissioners. The negotiatie is offered to planters in the Colony of Suriname (Colonie Zurinamen), who pledge their plantations, produce, and movable and immovable goods as mortgage security against capital advanced at six percent annual interest, repayable in fixed installments drawn from the estates' yield. Such terms exemplify the Dutch plantation-mortgage funds (plantageleningen) by which Amsterdam capital financed West Indian sugar, coffee, and cotton estates; the document's plain typographic dignity, free of allegory, presents credit as a sober contractual instrument. The articles continue beyond this opening page (signature A).
creator: Lever en de Bruine; Samuel en Jacobus Walland (Commissioners)
notes: Conditien eener Negotiatie, f. 1,000,000. Suriname plantation mortgage negotiatie. Directors: Lever en de Bruine, Amsterdam. Commissioners: Samuel & Jacobus Walland. Dutch plantageleningen system for West Indian colonial plantation finance.
transcription:
# Original:

CONDITIEN EENER NEGOTIATIE
Van een Millioen of Tienmaal honderd Duyfend Guldens, onder Directie van de Heeren LEVER EN DE BRUINE, Kooplieden te Amsterdam, en ten overflaan van de Heeren SAMUEL en JACOBUS WALLAND, als Commisariffen deczer Negotiatie en in alle gevallen Geauthorifeerde Representanten van de Gevolmachtigde Geinterseffeerders in dezelve, ten behoeve van Planlyke Geintereffeerders in de COLONIE ZURINAMEN, welke geneecgen zyn en aaneemlyк bevonden worden daar in Deel te neemen, opgerigt.

Art. I.

De Planters dewelke geadmitteerd zullen worden, uit deeze Negotiatie Penningen op te neemen, zullen hunne Plantagiën (met uytfluitiing van Meubilen en andere Zaaken, onnodig tot het voortzetten derfelven) behoorlyк doen Inventariseeren en door besiglde oin daar toe aangeftelde Prijzeurs doen prijzeeren.

En wyders die Plantagiën met alle derzelver aan en toebehooren Legaal en Speciaal na style Locaal der COLONIE ZURINAMEN, verbinden en verhypothequeeeren aan de Heeren Directeurs dezer Negotiatie, en zulks voor en ten behoeven der Negotiatie, welck Hypotheeck geftarkt zal moeten worden met willinge Condemnatie van den Ed. Hove van Civiel Justitie der gedagte Colonie.

Art. II.

De Planters zullen zig by dat Hypotheecq verpligten, alle de producten profluerende van hunne Plantagien, van tyd tot tyd te Configneren aan de Directeuren dezer Negotiatie en van dezelven hunnen benodigheden voor de Plantagien te doen toekomeli, en wyders overgefte dat ingavallen zy daar pan niet mogten voldoen, of hunne Producten in ZURINAMEN kwamen te verkoopen of op andere wyzen elders te diverteeren, het by hun verschuldigde Capitaal met de Intresten en kosten diretkelijk kan teeren gebruikt en by Executie ingevorderd by Executie ingevorderd zy by dezelven hun verhalt aan hunne Persoonen, Plantagiën, roerenде en onroerenде Goederеn.

Art. III.

De Heeren Directeuren zullen moeten werden geequalificeerd eens voor al tot het laten doen van Affuitantie, zo op de benodigtheeden, als op de af te zenden producten, van welke afzending de Planters ter behooryker tyd advertentie zullen moeten geeven.

Art. IV.

De Planters verbinden zig, om van de verfchuldigde Somma Jaarlycks te betaalen de Intreffеn van zes percent tot de darelycke voldoening en aflossing toe, welke Intreffеn uit de Producten der Plantagiën voor alle anderen schulden zullen moeten werden voldaan.

Art. V.

De Restitutie of aflossing der Capitaalen zal gefchieden in Termynen, duydelyk in het Hypotheeck te expreffeeren, zodanig die door de Planters zullen werden verkoeен, edog welke verfchuldige zodanig zal moeten zyn gereculeerd, dat binnen de tyd van 20 Jaaren de Termynen wel micerder, maar niet minder dan 10 percento zal mogen bedragen.

Art. VI.

De Planters zullen gehoude zyn binnen drie Maanden, na dat zy de Jaarlycke Reckening Courant van de Heeren Directeuren bekomen hebben, dezelve te approbeeren of hunne belanпgen daar tegen op te geeven en aan Directeuren toe te zenden, en zulks niet gedaan hebbende, zal de Reckening als door hun geapprobeert zynde, gehoude worden.

[Followed by signature mark and "A" at bottom]

---

## English translation:

CONDITIONS OF A NEGOTIATION
For one Million or Ten times one hundred thousand Guilders, under the Direction of the Messrs. LEVER AND DE BRUINE, Merchants in Amsterdam, and with the representation of the Messrs. SAMUEL and JACOBUS WALLAND, as Commissioners of this Negotiation and in all cases duly Authorized Representatives of the Authorized Shareholders in the same, for the benefit of Planters and interested Shareholders in the COLONY OF SURINAME, who are willing and found suitable to participate therein, established.

Art. I.

The Planters who shall be admitted to take up monies from this Negotiation shall cause their Plantations (with the exclusion of Movables and other goods unnecessary for the continuation thereof) to be properly inventoried and valued by appointed Appraisers.

And further they shall bind their Plantations with all that belongs thereto legally and specially in the manner customary in the COLONY OF SURINAME, and mortgage them to the Messrs. Directors of this Negotiation, and this for and on behalf of the Negotiation, which Mortgage shall be strengthened with willing Condemnation by the Honored Court of Civil Justice of the said Colony.

Art. II.

The Planters shall obligate themselves by that Mortgage to assign all the products flowing from their Plantations, from time to time, to the Directors of this Negotiation and to receive from the same their necessities for the Plantations; and further that if it should happen that they cannot satisfy this, or that their Products are sold in SURINAME or diverted elsewhere in any other way, the Capital owed by them with the Interest and costs can be directly pursued and recovered by Execution, with recourse against all their Persons, Plantations, movable and immovable Goods.

Art. III.

The Messrs. Directors shall be authorized once and for all to make settlements regarding both the necessary supplies and the products to be sent, of which shipment the Planters shall give proper notice in due time.

Art. IV.

The Planters obligate themselves to pay yearly on the owed Sum the interest of six percent toward the yearly payment and repayment thereof, which Interest from the Products of the Plantations shall be satisfied before all other debts.

Art. V.

The Repayment or redemption of the Capitals shall occur in Terms, clearly expressed in the Mortgage, to be determined by the Planters themselves, provided that such payment obligation shall be so calculated that within the period of 20 years the Terms may become less, but not less than 10 percent per annum.

Art. VI.

The Planters shall be obligated within three Months after they have received the yearly Account Current from the Messrs. Directors to approve the same or give notice of their objections thereto and send them to the Directors, and if they do not do so, the Account shall be held as approved by them and shall be maintained accordingly.

title: Vlaardingen Orphanage Lottery Loan Conditions
description: This printed prospectus sets out the conditions of a negotiatie (loan-lottery) of f. 75,000, headed "Dankbaarheid en Erkentenis" (Gratitude and Acknowledgement) and sanctioned by the Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam des Bataafschen Volks, the representative assembly of the Batavian Republic. Dated 9 May 1800, it was raised to sustain the orphanage (Weeshuys) of the town of Vlaardingen. The loan is divided into 1,500 shares of f. 50 each, allotted by drawing. A prize table runs from a single f. 10,000 down through f. 5,000, f. 2,500, f. 1,000, f. 500, f. 250, f. 125 and f. 50, with 500 prizes totalling f. 48,000 plus premiums. The 500 highest prizes and 14 premiums (f. 50,000) are paid as 3 percent obligations; the remaining 1,000 prizes (f. 50,000) take the form of expiring life annuities (instervende lyfrentebrieven) graded into five age classes, from f. 300 yearly for holders under twelve to f. 500 for those above forty-eight. The instrument fuses charitable endowment, public lottery and annuity finance in one revolutionary-era civic appeal.
creator: Vertegenwoordigend Lichaam van het Bataafschen Volks; Weeshuys Vlaardingen
notes: Negotiatie f. 75,000 / 1,500 shares at f. 50. For Weeshuys (Orphanage) of Vlaardingen. Batavian Republic, May 9, 1800. Lottery prizes: f.10,000 down to f.125. Annual life annuity interest by age class for non-prize holders.

title: "Voor den Armen" Charitable Bond, City of Haarlem
description: A Dutch municipal charitable bond headed "Voor den Armen" (For the Poor), issued at Haarlem and signed "den eersten January 1805" by the Ontfanger van de Stedelyke Belasting (Receiver of the City Tax). Acting under special qualification of the Raad (Council) by resolution of 5 June 1804, the receiver acknowledges f. 500 (Vyf-Honderd Guldens) received from M. Bodisco, placed at interest "tegen den Penning 40," that is one-fortieth, or 2½ per cent, yielding f. 12.10 (twelve guilders, ten stuivers) yearly. A resolution of the Raad of 14 December 1804 pledges the city's incomes and goods, especially the revenues of the former Gilden (guilds), as security, and sixteen coupons of f. 12.10 each, running from January 1806, are appended. The instrument shows a small city financing poor-relief on the credit of its own dissolved corporations during the Batavian Republic, on the eve of the kingdom Napoleon would impose. Manuscript endorsements track coupon renewals from 1822 to a final talon dated 1903, a near-century of unbroken service. Engraved italic and roman capitals frame the text in a sober, restrained late-eighteenth-century manner without allegorical ornament.
creator: City of Haarlem (Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting)
notes: Voor den Armen, Haarlem. Charitable bond No. 110, f. 500 at Penning 40 (2½%). Issued by Ontvanger der Stadslijke Belasting, Haarlem. Council resolution June 5, 1804. Signed M. Bodisco. Coupon annotations from 1822–1860s. Municipal charitable finance.
transcription:
# VOOR DEN ARMEN

**Original:**

Num. 110. — Ik ondergeschrevene, Ontvanger van de STEDELKE BELASTING HAARLEM, geurresteerd den 3de April 1804, bekenne in die qualiteit, uit krachte der speciale qualificatie van den RAAD dezer Stad, bij Resolutie van den 5de June 1804 daartoe verleend, ontvangen te hebben van

de Somma van VYF-HONDERD GULDENS, geliggt op Infveft tegen den Penning 40. in 't jaar, te betalen over één Jaar na dato dezes, tot de effectueele Aflosing der Hoofdsomme toe, en tot meerdere verzekering is deeze bij STEDTS Wedouwdert dezer Stad gesgrocëerd en geteekend.
In oircode deze geteekend den eersten January 1805.

Capitaal f. 500 - - -
In Intresse = f.2-50-

Hierla afgeaam TEENEN
Striks COUPONS cum No. 1.
tot 16s, ledet groef f. 12-10-:
beginnende primo January 1806: Als izo de voorichteere Somma van VYF-HONDERD GULDENS, op Intrest geliggt op Penning 40. in 't jaar, aan dienste der Armen, aen de Stad verkrekt is: Z00 is 'T, dat wy 300 af goudert der Sad Haarlem / als daartoe special gequalificeerd, bij Resolutie van den RAAD, in dato den 14de December 1804, tot meerdere verzekering van de betaling van het vooricheve Capitaal en Intresfen, tot de effectueele Aflosling toe, verbonden hebben en verbinden bij dezen, ten behoeve van den voornoemden

alle de Stads-Inkomsten en Goederen, en speciaal de Inkomsten der voormalige GILDENS ingevolghe het 56e Articul der bovengemelde Publicate.

[Signature] Uting van Berthuall & S: Druijsteeÿn

In kennisse van my,

[Signature] Willm Spallang?

the Sum of FIVE HUNDRED GUILDERS, placed at interest at the rate of 40 Pennies per year, to be paid in full after one year from the date hereof, until the effectual Redemption of the Principal Sum, and for further security this is registered and signed by the CITY [authority] of this City.

all the City-Revenues and Goods, and especially the Revenues of the former GUILDS in accordance with the 56th Article of the aforementioned Publication.

[Signature] [unclear]

In witness whereof,

[Signature] [unclear]

title: Habsburg Imperial Obligation (Deutz Loan), Amsterdam
description: This printed Dutch-language obligation ("Obligatoir Instrument") records a sovereign loan contracted in the name of Charles VI, by the Grace of God elected Holy Roman Emperor, whose full Habsburg titulature opens the text: "WY KAREL de Zesde... Koning in Germanien en Spanjen... Aards-Hertog van Oostenryk... in Opper- en Neder Silesien..." The Emperor acknowledges a borrowing of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Current money at five per cent interest, raised at Amsterdam through Willem Gideon Deutz as director and agent. The capital is to be amortised within fifteen years, the annual redemptions running from Anno 1737 to Anno 1747, charged upon the revenues of the Silesian duchies. Issued at Amsterdam and authenticated by the notary J. Barels, the document exemplifies how a land-rich but cash-poor Habsburg court tapped the deep Dutch capital market, pledging provincial income to foreign rentiers. The dense, unornamented letterpress, framed only by marginal annotations, presents itself as a legal-financial instrument rather than a decorative title, its authority resting on imperial style and notarial form alone.
creator: Habsburg Imperial Government (Charles VI / Karel de Tweede)
notes: Dutch bond/obligation document. "Wy KAREL, de Tweede, door Gods Roome Keyser..." Habsburg imperial bond. Agent: Willem Gideon Donz., Amsterdam. Aandelen and Obligaties in Hollandsch Courant Geld. Likely refers to Emperor Charles VI (1711–1740). Dutch-intermediated Habsburg imperial finance.

WY KAREL de Zesde, door Gods gnaden verkooren Rooms Keyzer, ten allerijen en Boheemien, Lands-Hertzog van Oost-enryck, Hertzog van Burgondien, Styrien, Carinthien, Crain, en Wirtemberg, in Opper- en Neder-Silezien, Markgraaf tot Mahren, in Opper en Neder-Lausitz, Graaf tot Habsburg, Tyrol en Gortz &c. beken-nen, voor Ons., onze Vryen en Nakomlingen, in kracht dezes schuldbrief openlijk., en doen de daar voor ontfangende behoorlyke equipanten, voldaan is zekere Capitaal, ende Courant geld, bedragende zevenaaml hondert duyzend gyldens Hollands Courant, en wie dierulyk in de gemeene Somme was Gevollmachtigdens gehoueden, tegeevoguiven, en vyt tieden beleeven, ioezeggen, en verrigsdelen, dat wy voor het geneelde for leen opgenoomene Capitaal 's jaarlyks aan Intereilen wy rato tot betalen ten erbelteemder ryd, op de hler vorengeltalte termynen, zo wel de gestipuleerde Inte-

# English Translation:

(1)

WE CHARLES the Second, by the Grace of God Elected Roman Emperor, [etc.]. To all and every one, whom this present deed may concern, be it known, that We, together with Our Heirs and Successors, do hereby openly and publicly declare and make known by this letter of obligation and bond, that through the mediation and directed agency of Willem Gideon Deutz, of Amsterdam, from diverse Persons and Individuals, partly within the jurisdiction of the States of the United Netherlands and partly residing in other Places, in service to our commonly administered treasury, and in the form of a loan, has been brought into the hands of Our Imperial Treasury in current money, against appropriate securities, a certain Capital Sum, and Holy Roman money, amounting to seven hundred thousand guilders in Holland Currency, and this common sum having been computed at one hundred thousand Guilders Holland Currency, [which] to Willem Gideon Deutz., and the further and mutual Interested Parties, the which aforementioned loans taken together have been subscribed, obligated and concluded between Us, Our Heirs and Successors, and from that time forth against Us, Our Heirs and Successors, with knowledge and agreement, on the condition and understanding that, having received the aforementioned loan amount of Capital, in yearly Interests We shall pay at a rate to the existing and stipulated Interest pro rata, the previous nominal borrowed sum of seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency for fifteen Years within Amsterdam, in like Holland Currency Coinage, shall be repaid and discharged from Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and thirty, until Anno one thousand seven hundred seven and forty, and two thousand seven hundred eight and forty, and two thousand seven hundred nine and forty, one thousand seven hundred fifty, and one thousand seven hundred fifty-one, the Capital itself in each and every Year pro rata to be placed; such being the aforesaid discharge regarding the Capital from half Year to half Year, but the payment of the Interests every quarter Year shall be made, and well through the resources from Our Duchies of Upper and Lower Silesia granted, and from time to time as the aforementioned obligations fall due, as also at that time by his loyalty and promise shall hold themselves, according to our gracious Obligation, bound in the best form of law and of right, to fulfill and to execute's Lands Income for the aforementioned matter and time at the aforementioned terms, so well the stipulated Inte-

rests, as the Capital itself for said seven hundred thousand guilders Holland Currency, to the satisfaction and acceptance of him Willem Gideon Deutz, and the likewise also Interested Parties, without any hindrance whatsoever satisfied, and further over like discharge by Our Princes and States in Silesia for these purposes drawn up, that namely the payment so well of the amount loaned out of Capital, as of the Interests pro rata in the above manner, and in the time fixed, without any exception or evasion whatsoever shall be executed, all in every way for approved held, and thereupon our gracious...

title: German Government International 5½% Loan of 1930, Belgian Issue (Berlin, 1930)
description: This is a bearer bond of the Internationale 5½%ige Anleihe des Deutschen Reichs 1930 — the German Government International 5½ Per Cent. Loan of 1930 — printed trilingually in German, English, and French. It belongs to the Belgian issue (Belgische Ausgabe / Tranche Belge), carrying a face value of 100 Belgas, equal to 500 Belgian Francs, and paying 5½ per cent interest. The bond is dated Berlin, 15 August 1930, and bears the seal and signatures of the Reichsschuldenverwaltung, the Reich Debt Administration. The loan was the financial instrument of the Young Plan, which rescheduled German reparations after the First World War; it was floated simultaneously across several markets and currencies — pounds, French and Belgian francs, kronor, Swiss francs, and dollars — to spread the burden among the creditor powers and lend the settlement an air of commercial normalcy. The red eagle seal asserts the sovereign credit of the Weimar state standing behind a debt born of defeat.
creator: German Government (Reichsschuldenverwaltung)
notes: German Government International 5½% Loan 1930. Belgian Issue. No. A.22399, 100 Belgas = 500 Belgian Francs. Trilingual German/English/French. Reichsschuldenverwaltung. Young Plan international bond issue, simultaneous multi-currency placement.

title: Dutch Essequibo Plantation Negotiatie
description: This printed copy ("Copie") of a notarial deed of obligation and mortgage establishes a plantation negotiatie — a syndicated loan secured on West Indian estates and placed with investors in the Dutch Republic. Drawn before Albertus Bakker and Paulus Cordes, councillors of the Court of Policy, Criminal and Civil Justice of the Essequibo river and its districts, it binds the spouses Johan Gottlieb Detericks and Johanna Maria van de Vyver, owners of the plantation "De Vyver" and adjoining Midland estates, to a capital of 80,000 guilders "Hollands Courant." The fund, raised through 160 bonds of 500 guilders each at six per cent, runs ten years from 1 July 1789, ten per cent of capital redeemed annually by lottery before notary and witnesses. Managed from Amsterdam by Remy & Comp. as directors, the instrument shows how planters mortgaged distant colonial soil to satisfy creditors in the fatherland, channelling Caribbean produce into metropolitan credit.
creator: Remy & Comp. (Amsterdam); Albertus Bakker; Paules Cordes (Notaries)
notes: Dutch Essequibo plantation negotiatie. "De Vyver" plantation and Midland Plantations. Capital f. 13,000. Directors: Remy & Comp., Amsterdam. Notaries: Albertus Bakker, Paules Cordes, Essequibo Colony. July 1789. Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system.

# Transcription

**Original:**

Copie

Compareeren voor ons ALBERTUS BAKKER en PAULUS CORDES, Raaden in den Edelen Aghtbaren Heeren van Politie, Criminele en Civiele Justitie der Rivier en onderhorige districten van Essequebo,
De Heer JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, en Vrouwe JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER Echtelieden, zynde de Vrouwe Comparante ten deezen met dezelve haare Man geadmitteerd, en door hem tot redeneering en posteering dezes (speciaal gerechtigd[?]eerd;

Te kennen geevendes: dat zy Comparanten waarneemens zynde te Negotatoeren en van diverse Persoonen op te neemen, een Capitale Somma van TAG TIG DUYZEND GULDENS, HOLLANDS COURANT; ter meede voldooge van huune Crediteuers in den Vaderlanden, op Conditien en onder zodanige speciale Hypothecair Verband van de Comparanten nametmelde Plantage De VYVER als in 't vervolg zal werden gemeld; daar over by blisfive van dato 25 Augustus 1788. hadden gefchreeven, aan de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, en dezelve met de Commisie gechargeerd, om het zelve aan hun Comparanten Crediteuers voortestellen, en unsere het moggelyck deeldens door hunne Interesses te perfeitaisueren, op het deelmeeing door deezen en dezelve Negotiatie byna volleekend was; Edog niet ziin onder de Rihere tyo die Agte van deelneming gemaeld, als:

**Articul 1.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie zal gefchieden onder directie van de Heeren REMY & COMP. Kooplieden t'Amsterdam, als Directeuren, en onder 't opgzegvang van de natetoemene Heeren Commissarischen'.

**2.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie groot en niet groozer zal mogez zyn, als TAGTIG DUYZEND GULDENS's Capitaal HOLLANDS COURANT;

**3.**

Dat de voorz. Negotiatie gfchied, voor den tyd van TIEN JAAREN, ingaande primo July 1789, op die van de na opgezegene Somma, 's Jaarlyka zal moeten werden afgeslost tien per Cento van 't Capital of AGT DUYZEND GULDENS, waar meede 's jaarlyka Sestien fluks der natetoemene te formeere Obligatien, en overplan van Noaris en genuygen uyrrelooten, zullen moeten worden afgeslost, op de respective verftchyiden, zynde primo July van vder Jaar, en motecende over zulks de certe Allosding eerste inschuiving primo Jaren af 1790, bencyens de Interessen, gereekend tegens zas per Cento in 't Jaar, eerot van 't geheele en vervolgeans van 't retgecende Capitaal;

**4.**

Dat door him Comparanten, direct na dat by hun ryding van de volleeckelung deezes Negotiatie zoude zyn bekoomea, ten behoeven van de respective Deelneemers in behoorfyke forma en orden renuntatiatie van afte hypotheken, als plaatse zoude kunnen en mogen hebben; Dezelve alle specificiid zullen moeten werden gepaasterd, behoorfyke en duuderkeike Acte van Obligatie en Hypothecq Inschrift en voorz. Plantage gemaeld. De VYVER cum annexis, gewergigd, met Intresse van de Conditien waa op de deelneming is gefchicd, en bafotte van Hipte markooning van alle deelche, of the by forme van den Hypotheeck Acte als allen ryden zal mogea worden opgezegd, en de misgaders dat by de voorz. Plantage cum annexis, als eenige geretefcyck Declaratorit, dat dezelve Acle van Hypotheecq, zoude mogea worden gevorgd, een behoorfyke, deugsdelyke en gerechts[?]ck Declaratorit, dat dezelve van Hypotheecq, is, burrijen de natetoemele, deeren Ed. Aghtbare Raaden ten behoeven van voorn. Heeren REMY & COMP; ingecertificeerd' waas van 't' y werelde noch zal worden gemeld, en datzelve met geen andere en meedere Hypothecquaire of Laslen bewaard: —

**5.**

Dat van de voorz. Adte van Obligatie en Hypothoecquarie, zullen worden uysgegeven 160 Copies authentici door een Notaris t'Amsterdam onderteekend, en met een daar onder ftaande Acte van deelgewing, welke groote VIF HONDERD GULDENS Capitaal, behoorly genaamoot van No. 1 tot No. 160, explicatie, en welke blanco in op naam, door de deelneemers refpectivvely te verkezzen, onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren, en ten einde daarvan geen meedere an de voorz. Houeeren Commissarischen reconnostreeren en door een Notaris t'Amsterdam geprocureerd ingeschi werden, als Directeuren onderteekend door de Heeren REMY & COMP.

**6.**

Dat ten einde de voorz. Allosding van 't Capitaal en betaaling der Interessen, door de voornoemde Heeren REMY & COMP. als Directeuren behoorlyк zal kunnen gefchieden, door hin Comparanten als Achthuseigenares, alle Producten hoe gaanaaml, die van de voorz. Plantage voorkomen, altos een inpoedigllen zullen moeten worder afgezonden, aan de voorz. Heeren REMY & COMP., om dezelve t'Amsterdam te verzoggen, en dan wytz, zoo evenegenoemde Allosding en betaaling als de onkosten van de Premien van Artillante ze zenden van de nodige provisien en benoodigheeden zoo Voor- als Najars, voor de voorz. Plantage te hanenen doen; Edog desze laastte niet o doen, dan met godesvindea en overwieg van de natetoemehe Heeren Commissarischen.

**7.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP., voor hunne moeite voor provisie zullen genieten, zoo veel als in diergelyce Negotiation gebruskely en t'Amsterdam bereekend werd; —

**8.**

Dat de Heeren REMY & COMP. dxog on dat deze Negotiatie tot volkcomen land zal syn gebracht, en de Pentingen (die met de voorn. primo July 1789. in gereechheid zullen moeten zyn,) het zy in contani

Appear before us ALBERTUS BAKKER and PAULUS CORDES, Councillors in the Noble and Honorable Gentlemen of Police, Criminal and Civil Justice of the River and subordinate districts of Essequebo,
The Gentleman JOHAN GOTTLIEB DETERICKS, and the Lady JOHANNA MARIA von de VYVER, Spouses, the Lady Comparant being herein authorized with her Husband, and by him admitted to reason and posting of this (specially authorized);

To make known: that they the Comparants, intending to arrange to receive from diverse Persons, a Capital Sum of THIRTEEN THOUSAND GUILDERS, DUTCH CURRENCY; for the satisfaction of their Creditors in the Fatherland, on Conditions and under such special Mortgage Bond of the Comparants named Plantation De VYVER as in the following shall be stated; whereupon by letter of date

title: Changuion Plantation Mortgage Loan, Essequibo & Demerara, 1816
description: This printed prospectus sets out the Conditien van Negotiatie, the terms of a Dutch mortgage loan fund directed by Daniël Changuion, raising f 400,000 in guilders for planters along the Rio Essequebo and Rio Demerary (Essequibo and Demerara, in present-day Guyana) to continue and improve their estates, at 6 percent interest for a term of ten years. Issued in the Netherlands in 1816, with ten claviers and ten coupons, it belongs to the wave of colonial plantation finance that followed the Napoleonic wars, when Dutch capital again sought returns from the newly British Guianas. The articles bind each planter to mortgage his plantation and its inventory, including the enslaved people held there, valued conservatively at a fifth of assessment, and to consign produce to the directors, with foreclosure on default. Plain and untitled, the sheet's austerity is itself revealing: the security behind the paper was human bondage, stated as routine collateral.
creator: Daniel Changuion (Director)
notes: Conditien van Negotiatie. Director: Daniel Changuion. Plantation loans for Essequibo & Demerara planters. f. 400,000 at 6% p.a. for 10 years. 10 Claviers, 10 Coupons. Ca. 1816. Mortgages on plantations and inventories. Dutch colonial plantation finance, post-Napoleonic period.

# ORIGINAL TEXT (Dutch):

**Conditien van Negotiatie, tot een Fonds waar uit, onder Directie van DANIEL CHANGUION, aan eenige Planters in Rio Essequebo en Rio Dém-merary, tot voortzetting en verbeteering hunner Plantagien een Somma van ƒ 40000:- voor 10 Jaaren zal werden gefoumcert, tecgens den Intreft van 6 pC⁰. 's Jaars.**

**Art. 1.**

De Planters in ongemelde Colonien, welke geneegen zyn eenige Gelden ter verbetering en voortzetting hunner Plantagien op te neemen; en daar toe door den Directeur zyn geaccepteerd, zullen gehouden zyn ten hunnen kosten, gemelde Plantagien door de Beedigde Prijseurs behoorlyks te laten Inventarisseren, zoo de Gronden, Gebouwen, Werken, Slaven en Slavinnen, en generaalsyk wat verder daar toe behoord, met uitsluiting van Meubelen en andere Zaken, onnodig tot her he-werken van den Plantagien, en den vollen en onbelisten Eigendom van dien Inventa-ris door hun betotsigd, door gemelde Taxateurs doene waarde brengen.

**Art. 2.**

Den Inventaris zal hier voren bepaald, opgemacke en geprisserd wesfende, zal aan Daniel Changuion, moeten warden gezonden, beneevens de behoeifvens der Verbandbrieff, waar uyt zal moeten blyken dat deifele Hypotheeck, ter Secretarij van de Colonie behooriyck geregistreerd is, en dat geen andere Speciale of Generale verbanden op deifele Effecten zyn leggenge, als meede dat geen verban-den zullen warden geaduanceerd of geregistreert, buyten confent van de Geldschuiders in deifeen.

**Art. 3.**

Aan de Planters welke den Inventaris en Verbandbriefs voors zullen overmaken, zal worden geflooten de helft of uytterlyck vyff agtle der waarde waar op derzelpatagien, met hun toebehooren, zyn gercexeerd, tecgens den Intreft van 5es pCento in 't Jaar, dz zy zich verbinden jaarlylka boven alle anders Laffen te voldogh op Penne in de Verbandbrief vermeld.

**Art. 4.**

De Geld - opneemers, welke Jaarlylks met Valuable bewygen kunnen aantoonen, dat hunne bezitttingen daar door zyn verbetert, zullen vryheid hebben, op een niewe Prifatie als boven gemeld te doen, van het meerder bedragen der waardee, nog op te neemen de helft van die verbeetering, mits dezelve aan voorn: Daniel Chan-guion mede Vebbogueerende.

**Art. 5.**

De Respectieve Planters Geld-opneemers, verbinden zig alle hunne te zendene Producten te addresseeren aan, nodige Provisiën te ontbieden van, en de Wiffelt-brieven te teekenen op Daniel Changuion, als mede by Affentding hunner Producten, aan hem in tyds behoorlylke opgave ter bezorging der nodie affiraatie te geeven, mits overeengst dat zy daar aan niet moeten voldoen of ook hunne Producten in de respective Colonien kunnamen te Verkioopen, of op andere wyzen te divertecren, hij welke geval hunne Intreft te voldoen, het hy hun genoeme Capitaal met de Intretten directedyck zal kunnen warden opgeeysfyck ten kaffen van zodanige Planters, by Executie verhaald, aan hunne Perloonen, Plantagien doen al dierzelver toebehooren, en verdere zoo roerende als onroerende Goederen, zig hier toe voor den Hove van Justitie in de Colonie, waar der ander nog worden waarigt worden, by vryvillige Condemmatie verbindende.

**Art. 6.**

De Geldgeevers behouden aen zig de vryheid, zodanige en zoo veele Persoonen in de Rivieren van Essequebo en Demerary aan te [leilen en te qualifisecren, als zy zul-
