# 170 BROADWAY BUILDING BOND - TRANSCRIPTION

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**
**STATE OF NEW YORK**

[EAGLE VIGNETTE]

**500** [left and right corners]

**170 BROADWAY BUILDING**

**CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.**

**FIRST MORTGAGE LEASEHOLD 4½% SINKING FUND GOLD BOND**

No. **B25** Secured by Leasehold Estate in the Improved Property at the Southeast Corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York. No. **B25**

---

**CORNER BROADWAY-MAIDEN LANE, INC.**, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the Company), for value received, hereby acknowledges itself indebted and hereby promises to pay to the bearer hereof, or if registered, to the registered holder hereof, at the office of **MANUFACTURERS TRUST COMPANY**, Trustee, in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York, or at such other place as the Trustee may designate, on the **first day of May, 1975**, the principal sum of

**FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($500)**

with interest thereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption), payable semi-annually on the first day of May and the first day of November in each year, and interest hereon until maturity hereof (unless this bond shall have been called for previous redemption), at the rate of four and one-half per centum (4½%) per annum, on the presentation and surrender of the interest coupons hereto attached at the principal office of the Trustee in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

The bonds are issued under and pursuant to the terms and conditions of a certain Indenture of Mortgage, Security Agreement and Indenture of Trust, dated as of the first day of May, 1952, executed and delivered by the Company to the **MANUFACTURERS TRUST COMPANY**, as Trustee (hereinafter called the Indenture), to which Indenture and the modification and amendment thereof (if any), reference is hereby made for a description of the property mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security for these bonds and the rights of the holders hereof, and for the definition of the terms herein used.

This bond is entitled to the benefit of a certain sinking fund established in the Indenture, and is subject to the conditions specified in said Indenture relative to the application of such sinking fund.

The principal of and interest on this bond are payable in gold coin of the United States of America of or equal to the present standard of weight and fineness without deduction for or on account of any tax or taxes which the Company, as the issuer on the principal thereof, may be required or permitted to pay or deduct therefrom.

The Company covenants, in the manner, upon the conditions and subject to the limitations provided in the Indenture hereto-fore mentioned, to redeem on such dates as therein set forth, any of these bonds issued by registered holder thereof who shall be a resident of the State of Columbia or of the United States of America, at the request of such registered holder by reason of such residence and the ownership of, or receipt of income from, this bond, provided that application for such redemption shall be made to the Company as provided in said Indenture, within forty (40) days after payment of the then last coupon.

This bond is one of a duly authorized issue of bonds of the Company known as its First Mortgage Leasehold 4½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond of the issue (unless and deduction) issued and to be issued in an aggregate principal amount not exceeding **FIVE MILLION DOLLARS ($5,000,000)**, all of which bonds are issued under and are secured, respectively, of the same date of issue, by the indenture of mortgage dated as of the first day of May, 1952, executed and delivered by the Company, and are entitled equally and ratably with all other bonds issued or to be issued under said Indenture to the benefits and security of the Indenture and the property mortgaged thereby.

Lessor to the Company, as Lessor, [unclear], or to be recorded in the office of the Register of the County of New York. Reference is hereby made to the Indenture, for full and complete description of the security mortgaged, the nature and extent of the security.

This bond is entitled to the benefit of a certain sinking fund established in the Indenture and, upon the operation of said sinking fund, the principal of this bond, or any installment of interest due on this bond or, the Worthlessness or observance thereof at the times hereinafter specified in said Indenture shall be applied toward such principal or interest, and in case of default in the payment of the principal hereof, be declared, void and ceased to become due [illegible] in the manner, at the time of times and with the effect provided in said Indenture.

The bond shall be subject to the Company's obligation to effect and set to the security, in addition to the issue and effect thereof. But this bond is a corporate obligation only and to secure it whatsoever, either directly or through any trustee, receiver, personal or other agent shall be liable on the payment of the principal of or interest on this bond, for the enforcement thereof, except as may be prescribed by law, except as may be prescribed, or the enforcement of the Company or, that American corporation as follows of any original or contribution or by the amendment of any assessment, penalty, subscription or limitation affecting obligations and provisions thereof, all as provided for in the Indenture.

This bond shall pass by delivery unless endorsed as to principal in the holder's name at the office of the Trustee and unless such endorsement shall not relieve the original holder from any liability hereunder, unless bonds by the registered holder in person or by duly authorized attorney and strictly noted herein, but this bond may be discharged from registration in lawver and thereafter transferred by delivery of the registered holder hereof shall be entitled to receive the principal hereof, but such registration shall not affect the negotiability of the interest coupons hereto attached, which coupons in themselves be transferable by delivery of registered holder hereof should the Company pay or discharge this bond held by the Trustee or held hereunder or by any person whomsoever for any purpose until it shall have been authenticated by the dropsignature or endorsement of the Trustee and underestimate, shall be in possession in the past trust.

**170 BROADWAY MAIDEN LANE, INC.** has caused this bond to be signed by the president or a vice-president and the corporate seal to be hereunto affixed, attested by its secretary or an assistant secretary, and coupon for such interest bearing the facsimile signature of its treasurer to be hereto affixed, all as of the first day of May, 1952.

---

**Attest:**

[SIGNATURE] **H. L. Darbo**
**Secretary**

[CORPORATE SEAL - circular with decorative border]

[SIGNATURE] **Charles R. Handa**
**President**

title: 170 Broadway Building First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond Stub
description: The face of a registered First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond for $500, issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc.—the 170 Broadway Building company—and secured by the leasehold estate in the improved property at the southeast corner of Broadway and Maiden Lane in the Borough of Manhattan. Principal falls due May 1, 1949, with interest payable May 1 and November 1 at the office of Manufacturers Trust Company, the indenture trustee whose certificate and assistant officer's signature authenticate the instrument. The reverse permits exchange of $500 and $1,000 bonds of the issue for an equal aggregate amount. Such gold bonds financed lower Manhattan office construction by pledging the ground lease itself; the engraved frame, green guilloche, and Roman lettering project the sober permanence on which a long-dated mortgage obligation depended. A blank registration ledger occupies the left panel.

title: Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc. (170 Broadway Building) First Mortgage Leasehold Gold Bond, New York, 1928
description: This is the coupon-and-conditions sheet for a First Mortgage Leasehold 6½% Sinking Fund Gold Bond issued by Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc., the corporate vehicle behind the 170 Broadway Building in Manhattan, dated the first day of May, 1928 and maturing 1 May 1949. Each surviving semi-annual coupon, running from May 1933 through May 1949, promises the bearer sixteen and 25/100 dollars in gold coin of the United States at the principal office of Manufacturers Trust Company in the Borough of Manhattan — $32.50 a year, the 6½% yield on a $500 bond. The dense legal legend at right, secured against the building's leasehold and sinking fund and signed in facsimile by the company's president and secretary, embodies real-estate finance in the late 1920s building boom: a single Lower Manhattan tower mortgaged into transferable gold-clause paper on the eve of the 1929 collapse.
creator: Corner Broadway-Maiden Lane, Inc.

title: Tyler Building First Mortgage Gold Loan Bond
description: Coupon bond of the Nineteen John Street Corporation, issued in New York City as of October 1, 1925 and representing a $1,000 share in its First Mortgage Six Per Cent Sinking Fund Gold Loan. The loan financed the Tyler Building and is secured by the premises at 17 to 23 John Street in the Borough of Manhattan, with The New York Trust Company as trustee. Principal falls due October 1, 1953, with semi-annual interest payable in gold coin each April 1 and October 1. Printed in orange within a dense engraved border, the certificate ties a Manhattan office building to the gold-clause mortgage financing common before the abrogation of such clauses in the 1930s.

Sheet of attached interest coupons, each "DUE TO BEARER" for thirty dollars in gold coin on April 1 or October 1, running through October 1, 1953. Every coupon cites the corporation's First Mortgage Six Per Cent Sinking Fund Gold Loan and is dated October 1, 1925. Page 3 of 3.

# Tyler Building Bond Certificate - Transcription

**No. M 1259**

**UNITED STATES OF AMERICA**

**Share or Part $1,000**

---

State of New York, City of New York

## TYLER BUILDING

**Nineteen John Street Corporation**

Certificate Representing a Share or Part in Nineteen John Street Corporation's

**FIRST MORTGAGE SIX PER CENT SINKING FUND GOLD LOAN**

Secured by the premises situated at 17 to 23 John Street in the Borough of Manhattan, City and State of New York.

---

THIS IS TO CERTIFY, that the bearer, hereof, or if this certificate be registered, the registered holder hereof, is the owner of a share or part of the principal sum of

**ONE THOUSAND DOLLARS ($1,000)**

In certain bonds and mortgages of or assumed by Nineteen John Street Corporation, a corporation existing under and by virtue of the laws of the State of New York (hereinafter called the "Mortgagor"), for an aggregate principal amount of Four Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars ($4,400,000), executed and delivered to The New York Trust Company, as Trustee (hereinafter called the "Trustee"), and secured by a mortgage dated as of October 1, 1925, of the real property known as the Tyler Building, given to secure the Nineteen John Street Corporation's First Mortgage Six Per Cent Sinking Fund Gold Loan, bearing date as of October 1, 1925, and bearing interest from October 1, 1925.

The said bonds and mortgages are payable in gold coin of the United States of America of the present standard of weight and fineness, or in gold certificates, legal tender for gold coin, with accrued interest thereon, at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York.

This certificate shall be payable (unless the same shall have been called for redemption) in gold coin of the United States of America or in gold certificates as aforesaid, at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York, on the surrender thereof duly endorsed on the back hereof, and upon the surrender of the interest warrants hereto attached as they severally mature.

Both the principal amount herein expressed and the interest in respect thereof shall be payable at the principal office of the Trustee in the City of New York, provided that, at the option of the Trustee, payment of interest may be made by transfer to any banking house in the City of New York. [Text continues with extensive legal terms regarding bonds, mortgages, and conditions - significant portions are obscured by red stamp markings]

The principal of and the interest on this certificate, and all series thereof, shall be paid in full without deduction or diminution on account of any taxes, assessments, governmental charges or claims of any kind whatsoever, which now are or hereafter may be imposed by the United States or any State or Municipality therein, by any foreign Government, or by any other authority, upon the Trustee, the Mortgagee or the holder hereof...

[Extensive legal text continues regarding mortgage terms, conditions, prepayment provisions, and sinking fund requirements - much obscured by red administrative stamps and markings]

---

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned has executed this certificate as of October 1, 1925.

**THE NEW YORK TRUST COMPANY.**

By

**Assistant Secretary**

title: Maplewood Suburban Home Company Mortgage Sinking Fund Bond
description: Six percent mortgage sinking-fund bond for $500 issued by the Maplewood Suburban Home Company, a real-estate venture incorporated in 1890 with capital stock of $2,000,000. Dated the first day of August 1890 at Nashville, Tennessee, the bond matures August 1, 1900, with interest payable quarterly and secured by a trust mortgage on the company's lands held by the American Loan & Trust Company as trustee. Printed in green, the certificate frames an engraved view of a domed civic building captioned "Nashville, Tenn." within an elaborate scrollwork border, with the denomination repeated as a large screened watermark across the text. Signed by the president and treasurer beneath the corporate seal, it embodies the speculative suburban land-and-home schemes of the era.

pages.description: Bond face, printed green. Ornate title "Maplewood Suburban Home Company," incorporated 1890, 6% Mortgage Sinking Fund Bond, $500. Engraved vignette of a civic building labeled "Nashville, Tenn." Body text, embossed seal at lower left, and ink signatures of president and treasurer dated August 1, 1890. Page 1 of 3.

Verso and endorsements. Central panel repeats "Maplewood Suburban Home Company / $500 / Six Per Cent Mortgage Sinking Fund Bond," principal due August 1, 1900, interest payable quarterly. title: Dutch East India Company (VOC) Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts
description: Two related documents of the Middelburg (Zeeland) Chamber of the Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie, photographed together. The lower sheet is a handwritten obligatie in which the Reecken-meesters (accounting masters) of the Chamber, acting through its receiver Dominicus van Hoontshoerle, acknowledge a loan of £1333:6:8 Flemish (about 8,000 guilders), bearing interest at 6¼ per cent per year and repayable to the bearer forty-four months after its date. It is drawn and signed at Middelburg, 26 October 1622, by the Chamber's officers, among them Cornelis Jauckly[?]. The upper sheet is the matching printed payment order of the "Ontfangers der Oost-Indische Compagnie," completed by a manuscript receipt acknowledging full satisfaction of capital and accrued interest, signed by Van Hoontshoerle at Amsterdam, 9 November 1623. Together they document the early VOC's reliance on short-term interest-bearing borrowing from private lenders to finance its trading voyages, and preserve the paper mechanism by which such an obligation was issued, serviced, and discharged.
creator: VOC (Vereenigde Oost-Indische Compagnie), Kamer Middelburg
notes: Dutch East India Company (VOC) – Middelburg Chamber Obligation Receipts, 1622–1623

title: Monte di Pietà of Florence assignment of luoghi, Florence, 1647
description: A printed assignment of luoghi (assegnazione di luoghi) issued by the Officials, Protectors and Administrators of the Monte di Pietà of Florence, conveying to a named holder—here an illustrious Marchese—a stake in the Monte's non-redeemable funded capital. Manuscript entries record four and a half luoghi, each valued at 100 scudi fiorentini reckoned at seven lire to the scudo, yielding five scudi a year, a steady five-per-cent return paid in half-yearly portions. The text grounds the issue in the provision of 23 April 1616 by which the Grand Duke of Tuscany authorised the perpetual capital, while the document itself is dated at Florence on 11 January 1647. Such instruments turned a charitable pawn-bank into a vehicle for funded Tuscan public credit, drawing patrician capital into a secure, interest-bearing endowment. A wax seal, secretarial and ducal signatures, and engraved figures flanking the Medici arms attest its authority.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Bond certificate assigning luoghi of the Florentine Monte di Pietà. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year. Established under Grand Ducal senate ordinance of 1616.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original (Italian):**

NOI Officiali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile eretto d'ordine del S. G. D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 23. di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo à [manuscript: Mario di Iacopo della Pia] Luoghi [manuscript notation] di detto Monte di valuta di [scudi] cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti à ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanui in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi [manuscript: scudi cento] come à Entrata Da [manuscript notation], di feniere credittore, e padrone di sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte eretto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Liberti à pagarii i frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi la rata portione, e con i priuileggi, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordinamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi farà, che paghi detti frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale sarà lortocritta da vno de' nostri Officiali, e dal nostro Prouedittore, e Cancelliere, e sfigliata con il liolito figillo di detto Monte.

Data in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il [manuscript: 11 Novembre 1647]

---

**English Translation:**

We, Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the perpetual Monte established by order of His Serene Highness, the Grand Duke of Tuscany, our Lord, for the Provision published in the Most Serene Senate of 48, on the 23rd day of April 1616, do hereby cede and assign to [Mario di Iacopo della Pia] Places of the said Monte with a value of one hundred scudi for each place at seven lire per scudo in Florentine currency, with interest at the rate of five scudi per year for each place from this day forward. The price of which places of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our general Treasurer in scudi [one hundred scudi], as Entry, [notation], as creditor owner and proprietor of the said places in the ledger of the said Monte established for that Provision marked D of the Liberti, to be paid the interest every six months at the aforesaid rate and portion, and with the privileges, conditions, and declarations contained in that Provision and in no other manner. And by virtue of this present document we hereby order the general Treasurer of our Monte that for the time being he shall pay the said interest every six months, and in witness thereof we have caused this document to be made, which shall be signed by one of our Officials and by our Provision Officer and Chancellor, and sealed with the holy seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà on [11 November 1647]

---

**Notable markings:**

- **Coat of arms**: Medici/Tuscan coat of arms with crown at top center
- **Allegorical figures**: Two classical female figures (likely Charity and Justice) flanking the coat of arms on either side
- **Wax seal**: Large wax seal impression visible in lower left portion
- **Manuscript annotations**: Multiple handwritten insertions and notations in the printed text, indicating names and specific details filled in by hand
- **Signatures**: Manuscript signatures visible at bottom right [illegible due to image quality]
- **Serial/reference notations**: Various manuscript numbers and abbreviations throughout

title: De Woning-Maatschappij, Batavia Bearer Share
description: A bearer share (aan toonder) of f 100 in the Naamlooze Vennootschap De Woning-Maatschappij, a limited liability housing company established at Batavia in the Dutch East Indies. The certificate records an authorised capital of f 500,000 in Dutch guilders, divided into 5,000 shares of f 100 each, and recites the company's legal foundation: incorporated by notarial deed of 27 February 1908 before Notary H. Schotel at Batavia and sanctioned by Government Resolution of 9 March 1908, its statutes wholly amended by deed of 25 January 1909 before Notary E.H. Carpentier Alting and approved by Government Resolution of 18 February 1909. Issued at Batavia and signed by a commissioner and a director, the share speaks to the colonial property market of the Indies, where a chartered company gathered guilder capital to build and let dwellings. The ornate vignette of a villa among palms, framed in a warm terracotta Art Nouveau border, advertises the comfortable tropical residence the enterprise promised to supply.
creator: De Woning-Maatschappij
notes: Colonial Dutch East Indies housing company bearer share, Batavia (Jakarta). ƒ100 share No. 1499, out of 5,000 total shares. Company established 1908.

title: Ostend Company Share Subscription Receipt
description: This subscription receipt records the first installment paid on capital stock of the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie, the Imperial East India trading company seated at Ostend and chartered by Emperor Charles VI. The directors here order their cashier, Joan Baptist Cogels junior, to receive 250 guilders in bank money (“Wissel gelt”) from the subscriber as the first payment on a share (“actie”) of one thousand guilders, on the conditions set forth more fully in the Imperial Charter (“Octroy”). Done at Antwerp, the thirteenth of August 1723. Endorsements beneath the text acknowledge three further installments (October and December 1723, and 1724–1725), so the printed engraving doubles as a running quittance. Issued at the height of the speculative enthusiasm that greeted the Company's founding, the document shows how Habsburg overseas ambition was financed by drawing the merchant capital of Antwerp into divisible, transferable shares.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: Ostend Company share subscription receipt, 250 guilden, Antwerp 1723. Multiple payment endorsements through 1726. Ostend Company chartered 1722, suppressed 1731.

Ontfangenvan D.Heer Jodeph Bergkin de Somme van twee hondert vijftigh guldens Wissel gelt voor het eerste paijement adje 8. October 1723

Solvit als boven onder de helft van het Derde Rayement 2. Novemb. 1725

He paid as above the 2nd payment 8 December 1723.

He paid as above the remaining half of the third payment 2 November 1725.

title: Ostend Company Engagement Contract for the China Trade
description: A French-language engagement contract (No. 7) by which the Directors of the Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie, the Ostend Company, retain Sieur Jean de Jonghe to conduct the Company's trade to China ("pour faire le Commerce à la Chine"). For all emoluments ("pour tous appointemens") he is granted one thousand florins (Mil Florins), with the customary right to carry goods back on his own account under stated conditions. It was drawn at Antwerp on 24 January 1726, sealed with the Company's arms and subscribed by directors including Jacobus Maelcamp, Louis Bernaert, and Pietro Proli, countersigned "Par ordonnance de Messieurs les Directeurs, Guy Couturier," with a later endorsement of April 1729. The sheet documents the personnel and incentive structure of the short-lived Habsburg-chartered company (1722–1731) that briefly contested Dutch and English dominance of the East India trade from the Austrian Netherlands.
creator: Generale Keyserlyche Indische Compagnie
notes: French-language Ostend Company payment receipt, 300 florins, Antwerp January 24, 1726. See also goetzmann1029 (Dutch-language receipt, 1723).

title: Monte di Pietà di Firenze Bond Certificate
description: A printed and manuscript-completed certificate of assignment (assegnazione di luoghi) issued by the Officials, Protectors and Administrators of the Monte di Pietà of the city of Florence, the standing "Monte non vacabile" established by the ordinance of the Senate of the Forty-Eight published 23 April 1616. The form assigns shares (luoghi) in the Monte's capital to a named holder; each luogo is valued at 100 scudi fiorentini, reckoned at seven lire per scudo, and yields frutti of five scudi yearly, a fixed 5 percent return. The holder is entered as creditor and owner in the Monte's register, the income payable in six-monthly instalments. The manuscript date reads 11 January 1627. Such Monte shares let the Tuscan state convert charitable-pawn capital into a funded perpetual debt, offering Florentine investors a secure, transferable rente. The sheet is headed by the engraved Medici grand-ducal arms flanked by classicizing nude figures, and bears a wax seal and manuscript signatures.
creator: Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze
notes: Second Monte di Pietà bond of same printed type as goetzmann1027; different assignee. Interest 5 scudi per luogo per year.
transcription:
# Transcription

**Original:**

NOI Offiziali del Monte di Pietà della Città di Firenze, Protettori, & Amministratori del Monte non vacabile retto d'ordine del S.G.D. di Toscana nostro Sig. per la Prouisione publicata nell'Amplissimo Senato de' 48. il dì 24.di Aprile 1616. cediamo, & assegniamo a [manuscript: Girolamo da Montauto?] Luoghi quattro è[?] mille di detto Monte di valuta di scudi cento per ogni luogo di lire sette per scudo moneta Fiorentina con i frutti a ragione di scudi cinque l'anno simili per ogni luogo da hoggi in auuenire. Il prezzo de' quali luoghi di detto Monte ci è stato pagato di contanti in mano al nostro Camarlingo generale in scudi [manuscript: Duecento cinquanta come è Entrata D a 300]

E noi l'habbiamo fatto descrivere creditor, e padrone di sudetti luoghi al libro di detto Monte ereto per detta Prouisione segnato D de' Liberi à per pagarli i frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi la rata porzione, e con i priulegij, condizioni, e dichiarazioni di che in detta Prouisione, e non altrimenti, nè in altro modo. Et in virtù della presente ordinamo al Camarlingo generale del nostro Monte, che per i tempi sarà, che paghi detti frutti di fei mesi in fei mesi, & in fede habbiamo fatta fare la presente, quale farà sottoscritta da vno de' nostri Offiziali, e dal nostro Prouedutore, e Cancelliere, e figillata con il solito sigillo di detto Monte.

Data in Firenze nell'Offizio di detto Monte di Pietà il [manuscript: undecimo giorno]. [manuscript: 1622?]

[Manuscript signatures and notations appear at bottom]

**English Translation:**

We, Officials of the Monte di Pietà of the City of Florence, Protectors and Administrators of the perpetual Monte, established by order of His Grand Ducal Highness of Tuscany, our Lord, for the Provision published in the Most Ample Senate of 48, on the 24th day of April 1616, do grant and assign to [Girolamo da Montauto?] four thousand places (luoghi) of the said Monte, valued at one hundred scudi each, at the rate of seven lire per scudo in Florentine currency, with interest at the rate of five scudi per year, in like manner for each place, from this day forward. The price of these places of the said Monte has been paid to us in cash into the hands of our general Camerlengo (treasurer) in scudi [two hundred fifty, entered as 300].

And we have caused this to be written and registered as creditor and owner of the said places in the book of the said Monte established for the said Provision, marked D of the Books, to be paid the interest every six months at the proportional rate, with the privileges, conditions, and declarations contained in the said Provision, and not otherwise, nor in any other manner. And by virtue of this present [ordinance] we do command the general Camerlengo of our Monte, whosoever shall hold that office for the time being, to pay said interest every six months, and in witness thereof we have caused this present [document] to be made, which shall be subscribed by one of our Officials and by our Provost (Provveditore) and Chancellor, and sealed with the usual seal of the said Monte.

Given in Florence in the Office of the said Monte di Pietà on the [eleventh day], [1622?]

**Notable markings:**

- Decorative coat of arms with crown at top center
- Two woodcut illustrations of allegorical figures (left and right sides)
- Wax seal impression (lower left)
- Multiple manuscript annotations and signatures at bottom
- Date notation in manuscript: appears to reference 1622

title: North American Land Company Share Certificate
description: An engraved share certificate of the North American Land Company, certifying that James Greenleaf is entitled to ten shares (numbered 141 to 150) in the entire property of the Company, with a guaranteed dividend of not less than Six Dollars on each share annually, conformable to Articles of Agreement executed at Philadelphia on the twentieth day of February 1795. Signed at Philadelphia on the tenth day of March 1795 by James Marshall as Secretary and Robert Morris as President, with shares transferable only in person or by legal representative at the Company's Philadelphia office. The instrument captures one of the earliest large land-syndication schemes in the young republic: Robert Morris, financier of the Revolution, and his associates consolidated millions of acres of unimproved western land into a single joint enterprise, promising fixed dividends to attract European capital. The promise would prove illusory, for the venture's collapse drove Morris into debtors' prison. The certificate's restrained copperplate engraving and unadorned text reflect a commercial-document tradition prizing legibility and the authority of signature over ornament.
creator: North American Land Company
notes: No. 15, 10 shares, min. $6/share annual dividend. Signed by Robert Morris (President) and James Marshall (Secretary). Morris was a principal financier of the American Revolution.
transcription:

title: Compagnie des Indes Life-Annuity Contract, Paris
description: A notarial contract of life annuity (constitution de rente viagère) passed before the King's Notaries at the Châtelet de Paris on 13 January 1726, by which the Syndic and Directors of the Compagnie des Indes constitute a yearly rente viagère of 920 livres in favour of Louis Despioch, conseiller du Roy and trésorier de France at the bureau des finances of the généralité of Montpellier, payable on his head and, after his death, on that of his daughter Antoinette Despioch. The Company binds itself to pay the rente in hard coin (espèces sonnantes), in two terms each year, at an open bureau, against the annuitants' acquittances and proof of life. The act invokes the Arrêt du Conseil d'État of 15 February 1724, which granted the Company the privilege of operating lotteries, and a deliberation of 13 December forming a tontine of twenty-eight thousand numbers whose proceeds, payable in cash and in life annuities, funded such contracts. The document captures the Compagnie des Indes—the reorganised vehicle of John Law's collapsed System—turning to tontine and lottery finance to raise capital from Crown subjects and foreigners alike in the aftermath of the bubble. The engraved fiscal stamps of the généralité of Paris head the sheet; the hand is otherwise plain chancery script without pictorial ornament.
notes: Company constitution/statutes notarial act, Paris January 18, 1785. Company name partially illegible; possibly a trading or insurance company. Mentions Bordeaux.
transcription:

19 Janvier 1785

[ORNAMENTAL SEALS/STAMPS - Two decorative heraldic devices visible]

PAR DEVANT les Conseillers du Roy; Notaires, Gardes-notes & Gardes-scel au Châtelet de Paris, soulsignés, furent prefens Etienne D'Hemeville Garnier blxt [?] 8.

Indes, Et Etienne le Cordier D'Olive Syndic de la Compagnie des Directeurs D'Aguecc [unclear] [several handwritten insertions and corrections]

Juin 1724. ainsi qu'il est plus au long porté [several handwritten additions]

[Lower section with handwritten text:]

M. M. Louis Despioch con

de Roy tresoreir & finances au bureau de l'Infrance à la generalité à Montpelliers pour l'oir par lui à l'esrante o [unclear] apres la lateste et pendant la l'r d'Antoinette Despioch l'Afle qui enjoyunt apres la decesir des 2e Impiees Denestreint à Paris anse d'Richelieu funeire d't Rochon cognement & acceptant

Sous Quittances qui ne pourront estre panées que parle devant Notaires à Paris, [unclear], avant les memes Notaires que auront recû les Contracts, lesquels certifieront la vie & l'existence des Rentiers; Et à l'egard des sinistres domiciliez en la Ville de Paris, on se pratique pour les Rentes Viageres dues par Sa Majesté: Et ce en deux termes, l premiere [unclear]

M. Cappe No

[Lower handwritten section discusses:]

M. M. Louis Despioch, Treasurer and [Court official] at the Bureau of Finance at the Generality of Montpellier, to hear by him [the terms] after [the decease?] and during the death of Antoinette Despioch the daughter who enjoys it after the decease of the Second [term?] Registered in Paris with Richelieu, furnished by Mr. Rochon, competent and accepting.

Under Receipts which can only be passed before Notaries in Paris [before] the same Notaries that shall have received the Contracts, which shall certify the life & existence of the Annuitants; And regarding the unfortunate domiciled in the City of Paris, as is practiced for the Life Annuities due by His Majesty: And this in two terms, the first [unclear]

title: Middelburg Plantation Mortgage Bond for Essequebo and Demerara
description: This printed and hand-completed bond, struck on a standard form headed "Register MIDDELBURG," records a money obligation in the Zeeland mortgage-lending trade that financed plantation agriculture in the Dutch colonies of Essequebo and Demerary (now Guyana). The undersigned, Kornelis van den Helm Boddaert, styles himself director and qualified agent of the geld-opschieters (money-advancers) on diverse plantations there, whose advances were secured by hypothecation (mortgage) registered at the colonial secretariat. Against a principal of one thousand guilders courant, received here from Mejuffr. Anna Susanna van Citters, he promises repayment with interest at five per cent yearly, the holder ranking with other lenders upon the special mortgages. Dated at Middelburg in January 1794, the instrument exposes the machinery of colonial credit at the close of the Dutch West India trade: a metropolitan investor's capital bound, through a notarised intermediary, to the soil and enslaved labour of distant plantations. The form is austere and typographic, its authority resting on the printed text, the company seal invoked in the body, and the clustered manuscript signatures below rather than on ornament.
notes: Plantation bond No. 502, Register Middelburg, January 1794. Secured on plantations in Essequibo and Demerara (Dutch Guiana). Part of Dutch colonial plantation mortgage system.
transcription:
# Register MIDDELBURG – Plantation Bond Transcription

## Original Text (Dutch):

Ik Ondergeschreeve KORNELIS VAN DEN HELM BODDAERT, als Directeur en gequalificeerde van de geld Opsfieters op diversche Plantagien in de Colonie van Estequebo en Demerary, volgens de Hypotheeken ter Secretarie aldaar, in beholpzake Ordres alle rest van dit fonds gepasteerd, waar van de Coypen Authentieq met 'sCompagnie's Zegel bezegeld, en door den Secretaris der Colonie geteekend onder my zyn beruftsende, Bekenne in die qualiteit aggervolgens het gebruikte Project en Conditien (waar van den inhoud hier agter is geïncercert) Ontfangen te hebben

van **[illegible] Anna Brianna Van Citlacht**

Eene Somma van **[illegible Ducaton/Dukaten?] Loons** [unclear]

Welke somma ik Ondergeschreeve, beloove dan dezevlve of deszelfs of deszelfs Regt en Actie vervogde, en zullen doen Restitueeren na Expiratie van den tyd in het Plan hier agter vermeld, en inmiddels daar van te zullen, uitgegeeve, den Intrest tot [illegible] p Cento in 't Jaar ingaande primo January 1700 **[& te Cloosen?]** gedurende alzoo van Jaar tot Jaar, tot de volle en Effectueele betaaling der Hypotheeken, waar van de cerste vervolgde zou zijn op primo January 1700 op de Plantagien en zoo vervolgends alle Jaar, en dat van alles vry-geld zondert eenige kortinge aan. Capitaal of Interesten te mogen doen, zullende de Interesten werden betaald ten Comptoire van den Ondergeschreeve, tot nakominge van al het welke de Geld-opnemers boven hunne [speciale?] Hypotheeken hebben verbonden, hunne Perzoonen en goederen, ten

Ter Oirkonde dezes by my onderteekend
Middelburg den **[8?]sten Januarij 1794**

from **[illegible] Anna Brianna Van Citlacht**

A Sum of **[illegible amount] [currency]**

Which sum I, the undersigned, promise the same or its or its right and action moreover, and shall do restore after Expiration of the time in the Plan stated here after, and meanwhile thereof to shall, issued, the Interest to [illegible] per Cent in the Year commencing primo January 1700 [& to Close?] during thus from Year to Year, to the full and Effectual payment of the mortgages, of which the first continued would be on primo January 1700 on the plantations and so successively all year, and that of all free-money without any abridgement to. title: French Royal 3% Rente (Droit sur les Cuirs)
description: A constitution of rente at 3 percent on the French crown, passed before the King's Notaries at the Châtelet of Paris and dated 3 October 1761, the rentier's capital standing at 10,000 livres. The printed heading ties the annuity to the Édit of May 1760, which created these rentes upon the proceeds of the duty on hides (Droit sur les Cuirs), itself established by an Édit of August 1759, together with the King's other revenues. Its substance lies in the redemption scheme: rather than a true perpetual, the principal is repaid in cash, beginning at 1,200,000 livres the first year and rising as freed arrears accrue toward a yearly fund of three millions, the holders chosen twice a year by lot. The numbered rentes are placed in a wheel in the great hall of the Hôtel de Ville and drawn each January and July before the Prévôt des Marchands and Échevins, the first drawing set for January 1762. The document shows a crown financing the Seven Years' War by pledging a fresh consumption duty and sweetening the loan with a lottery-like sinking fund. Signed by the notary Gervais.
creator: Kingdom of France
notes: No. 46460, 3% French royal rente, Paris October 1761. Derives from 1739 state debt refinancing. Part of pre-revolutionary French government bond market.
transcription:
# French Royal 3% Annuity Bond - Transcription

**Original (French):**

d. Octobre 1761.                                    M. Vaudeuil d'Harteville Sig.
                                                    10000

RENTES À 3 POUR CENT, créées par Édit du mois de Mai 1760, sur les derniers provenans du Droit sur les Cuirs, établi par autre Édit du mois d'Août 1759, & sur les autres Revenus du Roi.
No. 46460

PARDEVANT les Conseillers du Roi, Notaires, Garde-notes & Gardes-scel de Sa Majesté au Châtelet de Paris, soussignés, furent présens M.rs [followed by handwritten names and text which are largely illegible in the cursive script]

Tous Commissaires nommés par Sa Majesté, par les Lettres patentes données à Versailles le pour l'exécution de l'Édit du mois de mai 1760, ci-après énoncé:

Lesquels ont dit que le Roi par son Édit du mois de mai 1760, enregistré le 23 du même mois, a créé dix-huit cents mille livres de rentes effectives & héréditaires à Trois pour cent, à les over & percevoir par privilege & préférence sur les deniers provenans du Droit que Sa Majesté a établi par les Curs tannés & apprêtés, par un Édit d'août 1759, enregistré au Parlement le 11 septembre suivant, & qu'Elle a spécialement & par privilege affectés, obligés & hypothéques, ensemble tous les autres revenus, tant au payement des arrérages desdites rentes qu'au remboursement des capitaux de la manière ci-après énoncée, après néanmoins le prélèvement sur lesdits droits, des sommes définies au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux les liquidations des Offices supprimés par ledit Édit d'août, conformément à l'article 111 dudit Édit qui feroit exécuté félon la forme & teneur.

Que lesdites rentes feroient vendues & aliénées par lesdits seigneurs Commissaires, sur les quittances du sieur Garde de son Trésor royal, & les contrats de constitution passés en conséquence par tels Notaires que les Acquéreurs voudroient choisir; lesquelles rentes seroient exemptes de la retenue des Vingtiemes, Deux fois livre du Dixième, & de toutes autres impositions généralement quelconques, tous quelconque imposition que ce put être.

Que chaque constitution particulière desdites rentes ne pourroit être moindre de mille livres de capital, pour faire trente livres de rente, dont les arrérages commenceroient à courir au profit des Acquéreurs, au premier jour du quartier courant de la date desdites quitances de finances, & feroient payés de fix en ix mois, aux premiers jours des mois de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par le sieur Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, sur les simples quittances des Rentiers, à commencé du premier Janvier 1761.

Qu'il seroit annuellement fait fonds dans l'État de la régie dudit Droit sur les Curs & autres revenus de Sa Majesté, en que lesdites rentes substitutiroient en tout ou partie, de la somme de trois millions de livres, qui seroit annuellement remise par les Régisseurs ou Fermiers desdits droits, entre les mains des Curs du Trésor royal, chacun à l'année de son exercice, pour être par eux employé au payement des arrérages & au remboursement des capitaux desdites rentes, sans que lesdits fonds puissent être diminués ni retranchés jusqu'à l'entier remboursement desdites rentes, dans préjudice du prélèvement ci-dessus mentionné.

Que les capitaux desdites rentes feroient remboursés en derniers compans, à raison de la somme de deux cents livres par mille livres pour la première année, de pareille somme pour les années suivantes, & par augmentation de la somme à laquelle le trouveroient monter les arrérages des capitaux qui auroient été remboursés chaque année, en sorte qu'il seroit employé nous les ans de fonds jusqu'à parfait remboursement desdites rentes, dans pouvoir distraire aucune partie de ce fonds, sous quelque prétexte que ce fut.

Que les remboursemens feroient faits par le Garde du Trésor royal en exercice, aux premiers jours de Janvier & de Juillet de chaque année, par la voie du sort en forme de Loterie, à commencer du premier Janvier 1762 & ainsi de suite de fix en six mois; à l'effet de quoi les contrats de constitution desdites rentes feroient numérotés, & les numéros mis publiquement dans les derniers jours de Juin & de Décembre de chaque année, dans une roue en la grande Roue de l'Hôtel de ville de Paris, pour, en présence de Messieurs les Prévôt des Marchands & Échevins de la même ville, être tiré

[Signature] M. Garnier Bp.

---

## English Translation:

**October 2, 1761**                                M. Vaudeuil d'Harteville, Sig.
                                                    10000

**3 PERCENT ANNUITIES (RENTES), created by Edict of the month of May 1760, on the latest proceeds of the Tax on Hides, established by another Edict of the month of August 1759, & on the other Revenues of the King.**

No. 46460

BEFORE the Counselors of the King, Notaries, Record-Keepers & Seal-Keepers of His Majesty at the Châtelet of Paris, undersigned, appeared M.rs [followed by handwritten names largely illegible]

All Commissioners appointed by His Majesty, by Letters patent issued at Versailles for the execution of the Edict of the month of May 1760, hereinafter stated:

Who declared that the King, by his Edict of the month of May 1760, registered on the 23rd of the same month, has created eighteen hundred thousand livres of effective & hereditary annuities at Three percent, to be recovered & collected with privilege & preference on the funds deriving from the Tax which His Majesty has established on tanned & dressed hides, by an Edict of August 1759, registered in Parliament on September 11 following, & which He has specially & by privilege assigned, obligated & mortgaged, together with all other revenues, both for the payment of arrears of said annuities and for the reimbursement of capital in the manner hereinafter stated; provided nevertheless that there shall first be deducted from said revenues, the sums defined for the payment of arrears & for the reimbursement of capital for the liquidations of Offices suppressed by the said Edict of August, in conformity with article 111 of the said Edict which shall be executed according to its form & tenor.

That said annuities shall be sold & transferred by said gentlemen Commissioners, upon receipts from the Keeper of His Royal Treasury, & contracts of constitution passed in consequence by such Notaries as the Purchasers shall choose; which annuities shall be exempt from the assessment of the Twentieths, Two-fold book of the Tenth, & from all other general impositions whatsoever, any imposition whatsoever that might be.

That each individual constitution of said annuities shall not be less than one thousand livres of capital, to yield thirty livres of annual income, whose arrears shall commence to accrue for the benefit of Purchasers, on the first day of the quarter current from the date of said financial receipts, & shall be paid every six months, on the first days of the months of January & July of each year, by the Keeper of the Royal Treasury in office, upon simple receipts of the Annuitants, beginning from

title: Russian Imperial Perpetual Income Bond
description: A Russian Imperial perpetual-income certificate (Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ, "непрерывный доходъ по 5 на сто") promising 5 per cent in perpetuity on a principal of 960 silver rubles, rendered also as £148 sterling — a fixed parity of three shillings one penny per silver ruble that announces its intended London market. The capital is recorded in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Reduction of Debts (Коммиссія погашенія долговъ), the imperial sinking-fund body, and the instrument is dated 1 March 1822 at St Petersburg. It belongs to the celebrated Russian 5 per cent loan of 1822, the first sovereign issue Nicholas-era Russia floated through N. M. Rothschild of London, whose embossed seal appears at lower right; the dense Russian and French conditions on payment and redemption, and the central seal of Justice bearing the tablet LOI, advertise a bond drawn for foreign creditors as much as domestic ones, binding imperial finance to the western capital markets.
creator: Russian State Loan Bank (Государственный Заёмный Банк)
notes: Russian 5% Perpetual Income bond, 960 rubles = £43 Sterling, Class 1, issued March 1, 1822 by Russian State Loan Bank. Bilateral ruble/sterling denomination targets foreign investors.
transcription:
# Russian Imperial 5 % Perpetual-Income Certificate, 1822 — Transcription

## Original:

*(Ornamental heading band, left to right:)*

**ROUBLES 960**    *(centre: Imperial double-headed eagle above the date* **1822 ГОДА** *)*    **£148 Sterling**

*(Printed serial at left of band:)* **№ 69374.**
*(Printed serial at right of band:)* **№ 15172.**   *(handwritten beside it:)* 90

*(Centred heading:)*

**НЕПРЕРЫВНЫЙ ДОХОДЪ ПО 5 НА СТО.**

Свидѣтельство о непрерывномъ доходѣ на капиталъ, внесенный въ Государственную долговую книгу Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ, состоящій въ девяти стахъ шестидесяти рубляхъ серебромъ, равняющійся 148 фунтамъ стерлинговъ.

Записанный 1го Марта 1822.

Книга *1.* стран. *204.* 2 займа Лит. *С.*   *(the book-number, page-number and series letter are handwritten fill-ins)*

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства имѣетъ право на непрерывный доходъ, заключающійся въ сорока восьми рубляхъ серебромъ, платимый чрезъ каждые полгода по его произволу въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ, а именно: двадцать четыре рубля серебромъ 1го числа Марта и двадцать четыре рубля серебромъ 1го числа Сентября; если въ С. Петербургѣ, по серебрянымъ рублямъ настоящаго вѣса и пробы, если же въ Лондонѣ, по три шиллинга и одинъ пенсъ стерлинговъ за каждый серебряный рубль: въ томъ и другомъ случаѣ по предъявленіи купона на причитающійся къ платежу непрерывный доходъ.

Предъявитель сего Свидѣтельства можетъ, представивъ оное въ Государственную Коммиссію погашенія долговъ, требовать, чтобы вмѣсто онаго выданъ былъ безсрочный билетъ на туже сумму на его имя, или на имя лица, или лицъ, которыя онъ назначитъ: въ такомъ случаѣ непрерывный доходъ имѣетъ быть платимъ токмо въ С. Петербургѣ въ вышепостановленные сроки, и трансфертъ или передача таковыхъ билетовъ должна быть производима по существующимъ правиламъ.

Двадцать четыре купона принадлежатъ къ сему Свидѣтельству. Если при наступленіи срока послѣднему изъ нихъ, капиталъ не будетъ выкупленъ, или обращенъ въ безсрочный билетъ Коммиссіи погашенія долговъ; то имѣютъ быть выданы вновь двадцать четыре купона, и такъ далѣе, дабы обезпечить предъявителю полученіе непрерывнаго дохода въ С. Петербургѣ или Лондонѣ.

*Прим.* Особенный фондъ, или капиталъ, составляющій (ежегодно) одинъ процентъ со всей суммы сего займа, назначенъ на выкупъ его.

**Выписка изъ Устава Коммиссіи:**

§. 22. Безсрочный непрерывный доходъ, равно какъ и платежъ срочныхъ долговъ, производится и въ мирное и военное время безъ всякаго различія, къ дружественной ли или непріязненной націи принадлежитъ вкладчикъ.

§. 23. Въ случаѣ смерти иностраннаго вкладчика безъ завѣщанія, безсрочный долгъ имѣетъ принадлежать его наслѣдникамъ по порядку и правамъ наслѣдія той націи, къ которой онъ принадлежалъ.

§. 24. Капиталы, въ безсрочный долгъ обращенные, составляя ненарушимую собственность вкладчика, не подвергаются секвестру ни по казеннымъ, ни по частнымъ искамъ; изключая, если весь или часть капитала предъставлена въ залогъ по подрядамъ, или другимъ сдѣлкамъ съ казною или частными людьми, или въ обезпеченіе иска; въ такомъ случаѣ оный подлежитъ общимъ правиламъ о залогахъ и взысканіяхъ. Равнымъ образомъ таковые капиталы ни въ какомъ случаѣ не подлежатъ никакимъ налогамъ.

§. 25. Никто не можетъ быть противъ его желанія принужденъ къ обратному полученію всего или части капитала, въ безсрочномъ долгѣ помѣщеннаго. Но дабы съ другой стороны предоставить каждому болѣе удобности обратить оный, когда пожелаетъ, въ наличныя деньги, Коммиссія имѣетъ употреблять на выкупъ оныхъ по курсу особенный капиталъ погашенія, ежегодно сверхъ платежа непрерывныхъ доходовъ на предметъ сей назначаемый.

За Управляющаго *[handwritten signature — illegible]*

Бухгалтеръ *[handwritten signature — illegible]*

## English Translation:

*(Heading band:)* **ROUBLES 960** — (centre: Imperial double eagle, above the date **1822**) — **£148 Sterling**

*(Serial numbers:)* No. 69374 / No. 15172 (with handwritten "90")

**PERPETUAL INCOME AT 5 PER HUNDRED.**

Certificate of a perpetual income upon capital entered in the State Debt Book of the Commission for the Redemption of Debts, consisting of nine hundred and sixty silver rubles, equal to 148 pounds sterling.

Registered 1 March 1822.

Book *1*, page *204*, 2nd loan, series *С.* *(handwritten entries)*

The bearer of this Certificate is entitled to a perpetual income amounting to forty-eight silver rubles, payable every half-year at his choice in St Petersburg or London, namely: twenty-four silver rubles on the 1st of March and twenty-four silver rubles on the 1st of September; if in St Petersburg, in silver rubles of the present weight and standard, but if in London, at three shillings and one penny sterling for each silver ruble: in either case upon presentation of the coupon for the income falling due.

The bearer of this Certificate may, by presenting it to the State Commission for the Redemption of Debts, demand that in its stead a perpetual (registered) bond be issued for the same sum, in his name or in the name of the person or persons he designates: in that case the perpetual income shall be payable only in St Petersburg at the aforesaid dates, and the transfer or conveyance of such bonds shall be effected according to the existing regulations.

Twenty-four coupons belong to this Certificate. If, when the last of them falls due, the capital has not been redeemed or converted into a registered bond of the Commission for the Redemption of Debts, then twenty-four new coupons shall be issued, and so on, so as to secure to the bearer the receipt of the perpetual income in St Petersburg or London.

*Note.* A special fund, or capital, constituting (annually) one per cent of the whole sum of this loan, is appropriated to its redemption.

**Extract from the Statute of the Commission:**

§ 22. The perpetual income, as well as the payment of term debts, is carried out in both peacetime and wartime without any distinction, whether the depositor belongs to a friendly or a hostile nation.

§ 23. In the event of the death of a foreign depositor without a will, the perpetual debt shall belong to his heirs according to the order and laws of inheritance of the nation to which he belonged.

§ 24. Capitals converted into perpetual debt, constituting the inviolable property of the depositor, are not subject to sequestration for either state or private claims; except where the whole or part of the capital has been given as security for contracts or other transactions with the treasury or with private persons, or as surety for a suit; in that case it is subject to the general rules on pledges and levies. Likewise such capitals are in no case liable to any taxes.

§ 25. No one may be compelled against his will to take back the whole or part of the capital placed in perpetual debt. But so that, on the other hand, each may have greater convenience to convert it, whenever he wishes, into ready money, the Commission shall employ for their redemption, at the going rate, a special redemption capital, appropriated annually for this purpose over and above the payment of the perpetual incomes.

For the Manager *[signature — illegible]*

Accountant *[signature — illegible]*

M. Rothschild seal at lower right.

title: Republic of Texas Texian Loan Bond
description: This is a subscription certificate for the Texian Loan, acknowledging receipt of thirty-two dollars as the first instalment upon a loan of three hundred and twenty dollars made to the Government of Texas for a term of five years, bearing interest at eight per centum per annum, payable annually. It is dated at the City of New-Orleans, the eleventh day of January 1836, and subscribed by the three Texas commissioners sent to solicit funds in the United States: Stephen F. Austin, Branch T. Archer, and William H. Wharton. Issued to finance the revolt against Mexico, the instrument doubles as a land warrant, promising that sums paid may be redeemed for Texas land at fifty cents per acre. The vignette of stacked arms and the marginal device proclaiming a 'Certificate of Texas' for 'Six Hundred and Forty Acres of Land' bind credit to conquest, offering the unconquered soil itself as the security of a government not yet recognized.
creator: Provisional Government of Texas
notes: No. 43, $320, 5-year term, 8% interest. Signed by Texas Commissioners authorized November 1835 to seek funds for the Texas Revolution.
transcription:
# TEXIAN LOAN

**TEXIAN GOVERNMENT AND GOVERNMENT LOAN**

**CERTIFICATE No. 43**

Received, of the General Thirty-two Dollars, the First Installment on a Loan of Three Hundred and Twenty Dollars, made by him this day to the Government of Texas for FIVE YEARS, bearing Interest at the rate of EIGHT PER CENTUM PER ANNUM, payable annually. Public Notice to be given, according to contract, when the other Instalments will be required, at which time they may be paid, or not, at the option of the said

For the amount paid, Land in Texas may be taken at FIFTY CENTS per Acre, with the guarantees and conditions of the contract aforesaid.

In Testimony whereof, we have hereunto set our hands, and affixed our seals, in the City of NEW-ORLEANS, this Eleventh day of January, one thousand eight hundred and thirty-six.

---

## Notable markings:

- **Three signatures:**
  - J. T. Austin
  - B. T. Archer
  - Wm H. Wharton

- **Serial Number:** No. 43
- **Date:** January 11, 1836
- **Location:** New Orleans
- **Decorative border:** Ornamental frame with floral and geometric designs
- **Printer attribution:** "Printed by BENJAMIN LEVY—NEW ORLEANS" (at bottom)
- **Heraldic seal/emblem:** Central eagle design with flourishes

title: Continental Bill of Exchange
description: This engraved bill of exchange, drawn on the 25th day of November 1778, obliges the Commissioners of the United States of America at Paris to pay thirty Spanish-milled dollars — reckoned at five livres tournois to the dollar, or one hundred and fifty livres tournois — to Moses Frazier or order, at thirty days' sight. It is the First of a set of four (First, Second, Third, Fourth), the duplicate form that guarded a remittance against loss at sea. The bill answers interest "due on Money borrowed by the United States," countersigned by Nathaniel Appleton at the Continental Loan-Office for Massachusetts Bay and signed by Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans. It is an artifact of the infant Republic's wartime finance: lacking hard coin at home, Congress drew on its agents in France — credit secured by the Franco-American alliance — to service the loan-office certificates by which patriots had lent their specie to the cause. The plain engraved frame, free of allegory, befits a workaday fiscal instrument rather than a public proclamation.
creator: Continental Congress / Continental Loan Office
notes: No. 85, $30 = 150 Livres Tournois. Interest on US war debt. Signed by Francis Hopkinson (Treasurer of Loans) and Nathaniel Appleton (Massachusetts Loan Commissioner). Payable in Paris.
transcription:
# Continental Bill of Exchange No. 85 Transcription

**Printed for 30 Dollars at five Sums, Journous Dollar, Numb. 85**

United States of AMERICA, 25. Day of November 1778

AT Thirty Days Sight of this First Bill, Second, Third and Fourth not paid, pay to Mr. Moses Frazier or Order, Thirty Dollars, in One Hundred and Fifty Livres Tournois, for Interest due on Money borrowed by the United States.

To the Commissioner or Commissioners of the
United States of America, at Paris.

Countersigned, Nathaniel Appleton
Commissioner of the Continental Loan-Office in the
State of Massachusetts Bay

S. Hopkinson
(Treas. of Loans.)

---

**Notable markings:**

- Serial number: **85** (appears in top right)
- Signature: **S. Hopkinson** (Francis Hopkinson, Treasurer of Loans)
- Countersignature: **Nathaniel Appleton** (Commissioner of Continental Loan-Office, Massachusetts Bay)
- Ornamental borders and decorative elements on left margin
- Handwritten annotations and stamps on left side [partially illegible]
- Standard format indicating this is the "First" of four bills issued simultaneously
